The system exhibits transitions from repulsion to attraction at distances where the effect is measurable. Such Casimir trapping is controlled by the choice of metal and ferrofluid materials. The thermal and quantum contributions are analyzed in detail, and the influence of the optical and magnetic properties of the ferrofluid on the trapping strength and the distance range where it is observed is shown.
threads: Обычно две поверхности вплотную слипаются из-за квантового эффекта Казимира. Но учёные нашли способ обратить это притяжение в отталкивание, смешав магнитную жидкость, — и заставили крошечную пластинку парить в воздухе без поддержки. Тонкая настройка материалов открывает путь к микроустройствам, где детали удерживаются квантовым полем.

title: A Solar Trap for Dark Matter
oneliner: Ultralight dark matter can collect in the Sun’s gravitational 'goblet', settling into distinct energy steps.
description: The Sun’s gravity creates an invisible funnel where dark matter particles not only accumulate but occupy strictly defined energy levels — like electrons in an atom. It turns out that over long observations, some of this matter regains wave synchrony, greatly amplifying the potential signal in detect
abstract: The Sun's gravitational potential forms a potential well for ultralight dark matter (ULDM), giving rise to a discrete spectrum of energy states. Even at extremely low occupation numbers, these states introduce a new characteristic timescale into the field dynamics, which requires a generalization of the standard concept of coherence time. It is shown that at long times, a recoherence phenomenon emerges: a certain sub-component of the ULDM exhibits a formally diverging coherence time, meaning it restores phase coherence. The generalized coherence time can significantly exceed the naive estimate, leading to enhanced sensitivity in dark matter search experiments that rely on long-term data accumulation. This result opens up new prospects for detecting ultralight candidates.
threads: Солнце работает как чаша-накопитель для невидимой тёмной материи. Удивительно, но в этой чаше материя начинает двигаться строго по «ступенькам» — как лестница в призрачном замке. Чем дольше мы смотрим, тем больше шансов её поймать.

title: Gravitational Echo Will Reveal the Number of Primordial Black Holes
oneliner: By analyzing gravitational echo delays, astronomers will count invisible primordial black holes.
description: Future gravitational wave detectors will see millions of black hole mergers. Sometimes the signal won’t arrive just once—massive invisible objects, like mountains, will create echoes. By measuring the delays between repeats, scientists will determine what fraction of dark matter is made up of primor
threads: Один и тот же гравитационный сигнал от слияния чёрных дыр может прийти к нам дважды или трижды — как эхо от невидимых «гор» из тёмной материи. Анализируя такие повторы и их задержку, учёные придумали способ пересчитать древние сверхмассивные чёрные дыры, даже если те никогда не излучают свет. Новый метод обещает ограничить их количество точнее, чем съёмки целых галактик.

title: How quantum fog tests the laws of gravity
oneliner: An ultracold atomic cloud behaves differently if gravity changes over large distances.
description: Scientists created modified gravity conditions (MOND) for a quantum fog and saw that the cloud expands and oscillates according to simple mathematical rules. This opens the door to lab-based tests of gravity theories that replace dark matter.
abstract: Analytically and numerically, we investigate the ground state and collective dynamics of Bose-Einstein condensates in two traps: Newtonian and logarithmic, based on modified Newtonian dynamics (MOND). In the ground state, the MOND potential yields bound states only in the deep MOND regime, and the condensate is notably larger than its Newtonian counterpart. Size growth with the repulsive parameter β is observed in both cases, but only in the MOND trap is scaling R ~ β^{1/3} revealed, confirmed numerically over a wide range; for the Newtonian trap, no simple law emerges due to the breakdown of the Thomas–Fermi approximation. Analysis of monopole collective excitations shows that the oscillation frequency of the MOND condensate is lower and, in the strong interaction limit, decreases as β^{-1/3}. The results are important for designing experiments on quantum simulation of modified gravity with cold atoms.
threads: Квантовый туман из сверхохлаждённых атомов растягивается сильнее при особой гравитации. В лаборатории воссоздали MOND — альтернативу тёмной материи для вращения галактик. Чем сильнее отталкивание атомов, тем больше и медленнее туман. Возможно, скоро загадки Вселенной проверят на столе у физика.

title: The Minimum Length and the Surprises of Quantum Physics
oneliner: At the smallest scales, space is like a digital screen: momentum gets blurry, and particles entangle on their own.
description: If space-time isn't smooth but grainy, like the pixels in a photo, a particle's usual momentum gains an imaginary, blurred part. But the main surprise: this 'pixelation' spontaneously entangles quantum states—no kicks or collisions needed. This new face of nonlocality promises a breakthrough in quan
abstract: The subtle difference between Hilbert spaces for canonical and generalized momentum operators in the context of minimal-length theories has been clarified. It is shown that the existence of a minimal length permits the canonical momentum to have complex eigenvalues. A new method for generating quantum entanglement, stemming from this difference, is reported. These results illustrate how incorporating a fundamental minimal length into the description of quantum phenomena can be fruitful, revealing new facets of nonlocal correlations.
threads: Оказывается, если в квантовой механике учесть, что пространство перестаёт быть гладким на сверхмалых расстояниях, то импульс частицы может стать комплексным, а частицы — самопроизвольно запутываться. Это ломает привычные представления и открывает дверь к разгадке квантовой гравитации.

title: Dark matter isn’t so cold after all
oneliner: New analysis reveals: dark matter isn’t entirely passive — it has a tiny negative pressure.
description: Scientists analyzed cosmological data and found that dark matter might have a subtle negative pressure. This contradicts the standard model, which treats it as completely inert. The result doesn’t depend on the dark energy model used. The discovery points to new physics, but leaves unsolved the myst
abstract: A comprehensive cosmological analysis is performed on an effective non-standard dark matter model with an equation of state w_dm = w₂ a², allowing a slight departure from the pressure of standard cold dark matter. This model is paired with three single-parameter dark energy scenarios: a freezing scalar field, modified emergent dark energy (MEDE), and constant w. Constraints are derived using Planck 2018 CMB (distance priors), baryon acoustic oscillations from DESI DR2, and three compilations of Type Ia supernovae (DESY5, Union3, PantheonPlus). In all combinations of dark energy models and datasets, a robust preference for negative values of the parameter w₂ is observed. This indicates that dark matter possesses a small but non-zero negative pressure—i.e., a non-cold nature. Although the inferred Hubble constant H₀ remains compatible with the Planck ΛCDM result and does not fully resolve the tension with local measurements, the consistent detection of w₂ < 0 across a wide range of independent probes provides compelling evidence for new physics in the dark matter sector.
threads: Вселенная подкинула сюрприз: тёмная материя — не просто невидимый клей, у неё есть «характер». Анализ данных показывает, что она слегка отталкивается сама от себя. Возможно, это ключ к разгадке её природы.

title: How to Hear Stellar Collisions More Clearly
oneliner: An enhanced neutron star simulation slashed errors and turned up the heat.
description: Scientists improved a model of neutron star mergers by including heating. This cut the error by a factor of 10 and showed that the main burst frequency shifts by 150 Hz depending on the star's composition. The finding will help next-generation detectors study ultra-dense matter.
threads: Столкновение нейтронных звёзд — это гравитационный оркестр, где каждый инструмент — свойство сверхплотной материи. Новая симуляция научилась «слышать» в нём температуру и снизила ошибки в 10 раз. Оказалось, главная нота после удара меняется на 150 Гц в зависимости от внутреннего рецепта звезды. Будущие детекторы смогут по этому звуку узнать, из чего состоят недра таких звёзд.

title: Quantum Trick: How Disorder Boosts Precision Measurements
oneliner: Physicists turned quantum noise into an advantage by mixing the order of events.
description: Ordinary noise is the enemy of precision instruments. But in the quantum world, they outsmarted it by entangling the sequence of events. Errors vanish on their own, and sensitivity reaches its limit. This paves the way for ultra-precise medical sensors and navigation without GPS.
abstract: Quantum sensors can theoretically reach the Heisenberg limit, but in realistic noisy devices, this remains elusive. Quantum error correction (QEC) suppresses noise, but for sensing it requires prior noise characterization, signal–noise compatibility, and global control. We present a QEC protocol based on indefinite causal order (ICO)—the first application of ICO to error correction. An auxiliary system and noisy evolution are combined in a quantum superposition of causal orders; noncommutative interference enables real-time error notification and correction, circumventing limitations and restoring Heisenberg scaling. The protocol is validated for single and multiple noise sources, demonstrated on qubits, multiparticle, and continuous-variable systems, and reveals regimes with purely unitary correction. The results establish ICO as a powerful resource for metrological QEC and noise-resilient information processing.
threads: Рябь на воде мешает измерениям. В квантовом мире шум — та же беда. Но если смешать два события в суперпозиции порядков, помехи гасятся. Точность становится идеальной. Квантовые датчики выходят на новый уровень.

title: Why Has Cosmology Cracked?
oneliner: Measurements of the universe's expansion have diverged: it seems the cosmic recipe needs revision.
description: Different ways to measure the expansion rate, the properties of dark matter and energy, yield contradictions. Before changing physics, scientists are checking if their instruments are off. But increasingly it seems: the yeast is alive, and the recipe of the cosmos is not eternal.
abstract: Cosmology has achieved unprecedented precision, yet growing accuracy has exposed cracks in the standard ΛCDM model. Joint analysis of data sets reveals a web of tensions affecting the Hubble constant, cosmic microwave background lensing, spatial curvature, neutrino masses, and the nature of dark energy. These discrepancies are critically examined, with an emphasis on the role of model assumptions, parameter degeneracies, and data consistency. Proposed solutions in both the early and late universe are considered, noting how recent DESI results on baryon acoustic oscillations alter the viability of late-time extensions, and scenarios of dark sector interactions are explored. The need for cautious interpretation of measurements and internal consistency of cosmological tests is underscored before claiming percent-level precision or invoking new physics.
threads: Разные космические «линейки» дают разную длину метра: измерения скорости расширения, тёмной энергии и скрытой массы перестали дружить. Трещины в стандартной модели копятся. Возможно, тёмная энергия — не константа, а меняющийся актёр, и его капризы определят судьбу космоса.

title: Ghost Neutrinos: Born in the Coronas of Black Holes
oneliner: Most neutrinos are born in the fiery coronas of black holes, not in their jets.
description: Scientists have discovered that quiet galaxies with hot coronas, rather than bright jets, generate almost all the neutrinos that reach Earth. The finding, made with the Antarctic IceCube detector, changes our understanding of the origin of these elusive particles.
abstract: IceCube observations point to active galactic nuclei (AGN) as likely sources of astrophysical neutrinos. Near the central black hole, protons can be accelerated to high energies through magnetic reconnection and interact with the intense X-ray radiation from the corona, producing neutrinos. The possibility that the diffuse IceCube neutrino flux originates from jetless AGN with acceleration in the corona has been studied. A library of neutrino spectral templates was built on a grid of parameters: proton magnetization σ_p, coronal X-ray luminosity, and black hole mass. Due to strong coronal magnetic fields, synchrotron cooling of pions and muons is important. The model was combined with an AGN catalog consistent with observations in X-ray and mid-infrared bands at z=0–4 to calculate the diffuse flux. Coronal emission explains IceCube measurements up to energies of ~1 PeV, provided that ~10% of coronae have σ_p ~ 10^5, while the rest are distributed at lower values. At higher energies, the neutrino emission is suppressed, so an additional population is needed, and AGN with jets are strong candidates.
threads: Оказалось, нейтрино рождаются не в ярких струях, а в раскалённой «короне» вокруг чёрной дыры — как у Солнца, только температура в миллиард раз выше. Такие короны могут объяснить почти весь поток частиц, которые ловит обсерватория IceCube.

title: The first galaxies lived in tiny clouds of dark matter
oneliner: JWST data: the first galaxies formed in surprisingly light, almost weightless halos of dark matter.
description: Analysis of early galaxies clustering revealed that their invisible cocoons were tens of times lighter than modern ones. This sheds light on the joint growth of matter and dark matter.
threads: Первые галактики оказались скромными жителями лёгких домов из тёмной материи. С помощью JWST учёные увидели, что 13 миллиардов лет назад звёздные города собирались в облаках в 20 раз легче, чем позже. Это меняет картину роста Вселенной.

title: Gravity Without Dark Matter: The Entropic Approach Triumphs
oneliner: Dwarf galaxies have shown that gravity may not be fundamental, but emerges from the informational structure of the cosmos.
description: Analysis of star motions in 23 dwarf galaxies showed that emergent gravity — a theory explaining attraction through the information capacity of space — is more accurate than modified Newtonian dynamics. The result with a confidence of 5.2σ casts doubt on the existence of dark matter.
abstract: Two alternatives to dark matter — modified Newtonian dynamics (MOND) and Verlinde's emergent gravity — were analyzed. For 23 dwarf spheroidal galaxies, radial accelerations were calculated and compared with observations. It was previously shown that on combined data, emergent gravity agrees well with reality. Additionally, it was found that in 21 out of 23 galaxies, emergent gravity more accurately reproduces the trend of observed values within each object than MOND. The combined statistical analysis of 23 samples (significance from −0.25σ to 3.41σ) gives a final advantage of emergent gravity at the level of 5.2σ. The result indicates that gravity as an emergent phenomenon better explains the dynamics of dwarf satellites than a simple modification of Newtonian mechanics.
threads: В 23 крошечных галактиках проверили, какая из двух альтернатив темной материи точнее. Гравитация, возникающая из энтропии и информации, обошла модифицированную ньютоновскую динамику с перевесом в 5,2 сигма — это очень уверенно. Тёмная материя может быть лишь временной заплаткой, а настоящий ответ скрыт в том, как Вселенная кодирует реальность.

title: Hiding in Plain Sight: A Nearby Neutron Star
oneliner: Astronomers have uncovered that a nearby star's invisible partner is likely a neutron star, one of the universe's densest objects.
description: The star J2354, a few hundred light-years away, has a hidden companion that heats one side of the star. Observations of the star's wobble and light spectrum suggest the companion is a neutron star, firing winds that create a permanent hotspot. This proximity makes it one of the closest neutron stars
abstract: Mass measurements of the invisible companion in the J2354 system (1.4–1.6 M⊙) allow for either a massive cold white dwarf or a neutron star. To refine the nature of the object, brightness variability modeling was performed, including ellipsoidal modulation and two spots — either cold or hot. The spot parameters were constrained by fitting the light curves. In the hot-spot scenario, they are mostly visible at phases 0.25–0.5, and this model matches the phase dependence of the Hα line better. Thermal emission from a massive cold white dwarf does not provide the observed localized heating without invoking additional mechanisms; conversely, a neutron star can create it via an energetic wind. The results indicate that the compact object in J2354 is highly likely a neutron star.
threads: A star just 300 light-years away has an invisible partner that superheats one side. That pattern points to a neutron star—a dead star so dense it blasts winds like a blowtorch. Finding nearby neutron stars uncovers where supernovae once rocked our cosmic backyard, perhaps raining rare elements on ancient Earth.

title: The Universe is a Giant Heat Engine
oneliner: The expanding universe works like a steam engine: an invisible force pushes space, like steam pushes a piston.
description: Scientists have shown that the expanding universe can be described by the same equations as a steam engine. They calculated the efficiency of this 'cosmic motor' and confirmed: even the universe cannot break the laws of thermodynamics.
abstract: The Friedmann–Robertson–Walker (FRW) universe constitutes a dynamic spacetime exhibiting thermodynamic behavior on its apparent horizon. Its equation of state takes the form of the Van der Waals equation, enabling the analysis of thermodynamic cycles and the treatment of the FRW universe as a heat engine. In this study, two cycles—the Carnot cycle and a rectangular cycle—are examined using the phase diagram, with the work performed and efficiency calculated. The results show that the rectangular cycle's efficiency always remains below unity and never exceeds that of the Carnot cycle—the theoretical maximum. This finding aligns with the fundamental principles of thermodynamics, underscoring the universality of these laws in the cosmological realm.
threads: Вселенная — гигантский тепловой двигатель: её расширение, как поршень, совершает работу. Учёные рассчитали эффективность такого «мотора» и убедились, что он не нарушает законы термодинамики — увы, вечный двигатель не построить даже из космоса.

title: Dark Energy as a Falling Pencil
oneliner: The Universe is accelerating because, in its early youth, it lost its balance — like a pencil toppling from its tip.
description: New research explains dark energy as a loss of spatial equilibrium. A tiny dilaton field, born from that event, drives the repulsive push. It resolves the conflict in expansion rate measurements and hints at the cosmos's future trajectory.
threads: Крошечная частица дилатон, рожденная нарушением идеальной симметрии Вселенной, может объяснить, почему галактики разбегаются быстрее, чем предсказывали. Её энергия, как медленно разматывающаяся пружина, постепенно меняет растяжение космоса.

title: Cosmic Explosions: The Secret of the Strange Vortex Star
oneliner: Short gamma-ray bursts might come not from black holes, but from rapidly spinning stars made of exotic matter.
description: An ergostar is a theoretical remnant of neutron star mergers that spins so fast it twists spacetime. Long thought to be unstable and quickly collapse into black holes, a new study suggests that if it consists of 'strange' matter (quark soup), it becomes stable, and its rotational energy is enough fo
threads: Две нейтронные звезды столкнулись. Вместо чёрной дыры возникло нечто иное — сверхплотный вихрь из «странной» кварковой материи. Раскрутившись, он выбросил короткую, но ослепительную вспышку гамма-лучей. Возможно, так работают самые мощные взрывы во Вселенной.

title: Black Holes as Ghost Particle Accelerators
oneliner: Shock waves near supermassive black holes efficiently accelerate protons, spawning neutrinos without gamma rays.
description: Using computer simulations, scientists uncovered how natural particle accelerators work in the searing coronas around supermassive black holes. Shock waves in the plasma transfer about 10% of their energy to protons—even from mild shocks. This explains why neutrinos arrive from active galaxies while
abstract: IceCube observations revealed an excess of high-energy neutrinos from nearby Seyfert galaxies (e.g., NGC 1068) in the absence of a corresponding gamma-ray flux, suggesting that neutrinos are born in a region opaque to gamma rays—likely in the hot corona around the supermassive black hole. The mechanism accelerating the parent protons to ~100 TeV energies remained unclear. This work investigates diffusive shock acceleration (DSA) in the coronae of active galactic nuclei using one-dimensional particle-in-cell (PIC) simulations across a wide range of plasma parameters. It is shown that DSA is a robust and efficient mechanism, channeling roughly 10% of the shock wave's kinetic energy into non-thermal ions, even at Mach numbers ~2. The electron acceleration efficiency is variable and below 1%. These results provide rigorous support for hadronic models of neutrino generation in active galactic nuclei and quantitatively explain the observed gamma-ray deficit.
threads: Призрачные нейтрино от далёких галактик долго озадачивали учёных: сами частицы приходят, а ожидаемые гамма-лучи — нет. Оказалось, в горячей короне чёрной дыры ударные волны разгоняют протоны до фантастических энергий, а гамма-излучение тонет в плазме, как крик в подушке. Это открытие объясняет работу природных суперускорителей.

title: Betelgeuse Is Not Alone: A Stellar Companion Found
oneliner: Astronomers have captured the first image of Betelgeuse’s dim companion, lost in its glare.
description: Betelgeuse, the star in Orion’s shoulder, was hiding a secret companion. The Gemini North telescope has for the first time spotted a tiny star orbiting within the giant’s atmosphere. The discovery sheds light on the strange dimming in 2019 and refines predictions of the coming supernova.
abstract: After Betelgeuse's puzzling dimming in 2019–2020, caused by a dust belch rather than a looming supernova, the question of a possible companion remained open. On July 21, 2025, the 'Alopeke instrument on the 8.1-meter Gemini North telescope snapped the first direct image of a stellar sidekick. The companion was caught at an angular distance of 52 mas with a position angle of 115° east of north; it's 6 magnitudes fainter than Betelgeuse in the 466 nm band and cruises inside the supergiant's extended atmosphere. The result has low statistical oomph (1.5σ), but the tidy match with dynamical predictions for position, brightness, and mass makes it tentatively solid. The find explains long-running quirks in the star's behavior and sets the stage for follow-up—the next prime window is expected in November 2027.
threads: Самая яркая звезда Ориона прятала «напарника». Представьте маяк, рядом с которым кружит тусклый огонёк. Телескоп Gemini North впервые поймал этот огонёк — звёздного компаньона Бетельгейзе, в 250 раз слабее. Значит, взрыв сверхновой откладывается, и за жизнью пары можно будет следить дальше.

title: Star Counterfeiter: How a Twin Mimicked a Planet
oneliner: A tiny exoplanet's signal turned out to be a clever illusion from a double star.
description: Astronomers mistook a dip in star brightness for a planet transit, but careful scrutiny revealed an indistinguishable pair of suns. The culprit: a companion star hiding in the glare of the primary. A story of how easy it is to mistake a shadow for reality.
threads: Телескоп TESS уловил сигнал крошечной планеты у тусклой звезды: падение света длилось полдня. Но сверхчёткие снимки показали невидимого ранее близнеца звезды. Оказалось, что 'планета' — лишь затмение в этой паре. Такие иллюзии — цена поиска миров у далёких солнц.

title: A Black Hole Like a Bell: How a Light Pulse Gives Rise to Echoes
oneliner: A flash of light near a black hole produces a long echo, much like the ringing of a bell.
description: Light delayed by a black hole generates a prolonged echo with bright bands—like a bell tolling after a strike. Physicists recreated this on a tabletop using a curved surface, and can now study the echoes in the lab.
abstract: Within the framework of analog gravity, we investigate the optical echo behavior from a pulsed point source near a black hole, which strongly depends on the interplay between the photon sphere and pulse duration. Schwarzschild spacetime was modeled as a Flamm paraboloid, and the response was calculated using analytical solutions of geodesics and the Huygens–Fresnel principle. It is shown that when the spatial extent of the pulse duration is comparable to the size of the photon sphere, continuous 'echo tails' appear in the temporal response along bright interference fringes. A joint time- and frequency-domain analysis indicates that these tails are a signature of resonance between the incident pulse and the photon sphere. This work offers a wave-optical perspective on the interaction of dynamic sources with black holes and opens a tabletop approach to studying strong gravitational lensing.
threads: Вспышка света у чёрной дыры рождает долгое эхо, как звон колокола. Оказалось, это можно повторить на столе — достаточно изогнутой поверхности. Открытие поможет понять гравитационные ловушки и, возможно, создать новые оптические приборы.

title: Particles Play Shy in an Expanding Universe
oneliner: Particles in an expanding universe actually put the brakes on their own creation when they interact, upending decades of cosmic lore.
description: The stretching cosmos can conjure particles from nothing. But new simulations find that if those particles tug on each other, they slam the door on further creation—challenging the idea that more expansion means more particles. The early universe may have been far less crowded than we thought.
threads: Empty space isn't empty. As the universe expands, it can conjure particles from nothing. But new simulations show that when these particles interact, they suppress their own birth—like too many chefs refusing to cook. This cosmic shyness changes how we think about the Big Bang's first moments.

title: Bubbles in a Boiling Universe: How Dark Matter and Gravitational Waves Were Born
oneliner: Bubble collisions in the early universe birthed super-heavy neutrinos that explain dark matter, and left a gravitational hum detectable by future instruments.
description: In the first moments after the Big Bang, the universe boiled, spawning bubbles of a new reality. Their collisions accelerated particles more powerfully than any collider. This is how super-heavy neutrinos were born – particles that explain both dark matter and the mysterious excess of matter, and th
threads: Вселенная когда-то «вскипела», и в столкновениях пузырей родились частицы, которых никто не видел. Их гравитационное эхо, возможно, долетит до нас уже скоро.

title: Mysterious Dark Object Challenges Theories
oneliner: Scientists have discovered an unusual dark object that changes our understanding of dark matter.
description: The cosmic lens effect offered a peek inside an invisible object with the mass of a million Suns. Its structure contradicts established theory: the density is uniform throughout, and a black hole likely hides at the center. Perhaps dark matter particles can collide.
abstract: A million-solar-mass object discovered via gravitational lensing was analyzed under various mass profile and redshift assumptions. The best-fit models require two components: an unresolved point mass with radius ≤10 pc, centered within an extended distribution of nearly constant surface density out to a truncation radius of 139 pc. Such properties do not correspond to any known astronomical object. If the object is dark matter-dominated, its structure is incompatible with cold dark matter predictions, but matches self-interacting dark matter scenarios where the central region has collapsed into a black hole. This detection may have significant implications for dark matter physics.
threads: Гравитационная линза — космическое увеличительное стекло — помогла разглядеть странный темный объект. Внутри него, вероятно, скрывается черная дыра, а окружающая темная материя распределена равномерно, что противоречит привычным теориям. Возможно, частицы темной материи взаимодействуют друг с другом сильнее, чем считалось.

title: First Stars: Fireworks Seen by Webb
oneliner: The bright dots in Webb's images are likely not distant galaxies, but lone fireworks — explosions of the first stars that briefly lit up the young Universe.
description: In the James Webb Space Telescope's images, bright dots thought to be distant galaxies might actually be explosions of the first stars — pair-instability supernovae. These cosmic fireworks happen when a massive star made of pure hydrogen and helium is torn apart as light transforms into matter and a
abstract: Spectroscopically confirmed galaxies by JWST reach z~14 (300 million years after the Big Bang), and candidates have been detected up to z~30 (100 million years). Such early objects were not predicted by theory. A study exploring the possibility of explaining the z~30 candidates as hyper-energetic transients associated with the first metal-free stars shows: pair-instability supernovae (PISNe) — extreme thermonuclear explosions leaving no remnant — could arise in ultra-dense regions at z~30–40 within standard cosmology. Estimates of the likelihood of such regions, the number of PISNe at z≳20, and their observable flux indicate that JWST has a non-zero probability of detecting such an event. Confirming a transient at z~30 would provide a direct look into the era of first star formation, significantly expanding the empirical boundaries of astronomy.
threads: Телескоп Джеймс Уэбб смотрит так далеко, что видит время, когда Вселенной было всего 100 миллионов лет. Вместо галактик там, возможно, прячутся взрывы самых первых звёзд — парно-нестабильные сверхновые. Подтверждение откроет эру прямого наблюдения рождения космических светил.

title: Quantum Clocks Sense Gravitational Time Dilation
oneliner: A light-based experiment reveals how gravity alters time's flow across different floors.
description: Scientists propose capturing the time difference across a couple of meters using entangled particles of light. By storing light in traps, they amplify gravity's minuscule effect, making it noticeable without space travel. It's a step toward testing quantum physics where time flows differently.
abstract: Gravitational time dilation causes the proper times of clocks at different altitudes to differ. A quantum interferometer is considered with photonic clocks based on frequency combs, stored in two vertically separated quantum memories, so that the joint state evolves in a superposition of two proper times. After retrieval from memory, the photons interfere in a Hong–Ou–Mandel scheme, for which analytical expressions for multi-photon counting statistics are obtained. When transitioning from pairs of entangled photons to 2N-photon frequency-entangled states, the proper-time-dependent phase is amplified N-fold, accelerating the collapse and revival of the interference signal. Accounting for finite efficiency and memory lifetime, regimes where the modulation remains observable are identified. For parameters compatible with existing rubidium and cesium memories, the first collapse occurs at height differences on the order of 10–100 m and storage times from fractions of a second to several seconds, and combinations with rare-earth ions and alkali elements allow the required height to be reduced to a few meters.
threads: Гравитация замедляет время: часы на первом этаже идут медленнее, чем на крыше. Новый квантовый эксперимент с запутанными фотонами превращает эту крошечную разницу в заметный сигнал, усиливая его во много раз. Возможно, скоро мы увидим «время разной высоты» прямо в лаборатории.

title: Hunting Dark Matter with Qubits
oneliner: Quantum detectors set record limits on dark matter interactions with electrons.
description: Scientists turned quantum qubits into dark matter hunters. Invisible particles, nudging electrons in a qubit, cause a jitter like a snapped note on a string. Analyzing this jitter yielded record constraints on dark matter properties.
abstract: Measurements from quantum devices, particularly transmon qubits, open new avenues for detecting dark matter interactions. Transmons suppress external noise while maintaining high sensitivity to ultra-small energy depositions. Dark matter particles from the galactic halo, scattering off electrons, can transfer energy to a qubit, shortening its decoherence time. Recent experiments have recorded a steady residual contribution to the decoherence rate that cannot be explained by thermal fluctuations or known external sources. Based on this data, the most stringent laboratory limits to date have been set on dark matter–electron scattering in the keV energy range, along with competitive limits on dark photon absorption. The result demonstrates the potential of solid-state quantum detectors as a tool for directly testing light dark matter models.
threads: Квантовые кубиты, которые должны быть идеально изолированы от внешнего мира, вдруг выдали себя: их необъяснимый шум оказался не помехой, а ценнейшим сигналом. Так охотники за тёмной материей превратили недостаток в рекордную чувствительность – и теперь самый тихий шёпот Вселенной можно услышать в лаборатории, а не только в далёком космосе.

title: Why Magnetic Fields Make Black Holes Quiet
oneliner: The thicker the disk around a black hole, the less often stars and their dense remnants collide inside it.
description: Researchers found: the thicker the disk around a black hole, the rarer objects collide. Magnetic fields puffing up the disk can cut merger rates by billions of times. Astrophysicists must rethink gravitational wave forecasts.
abstract: Accretion disk models range from the standard thin disk dominated by thermal pressure to geometrically thick configurations supported by radiation or magnetic fields. In all cases, objects embedded in the disk (compact objects, stars, gas) undergo gravitational and hydrodynamic interactions leading to captures, mergers, and other processes. It is shown that the rates of such events, mediated by gravitational cross-sections, depend extremely strongly on the disk's relative thickness H/R — the dependence is inverse and scales as ~(H/R)⁻⁸. Meanwhile, in the outer regions of the disk, H/R can change by more than 1000 times depending on the dominant pressure support mechanism. Consequently, predictions of event frequencies can differ by tens of orders of magnitude: for example, accounting for magnetic pressure reduces capture rates by factors of 10¹⁰–10²⁰ compared to models that ignore magnetic fields. The results highlight the need for precise accounting of disk geometry and pressure sources when calculating observational signatures.
threads: Астрономы считали, что окрестности чёрных дыр кипят от столкновений. Но всё меняет магнитное поле: оно делает диск толстым, и тогда звёзды почти не встречаются — оценки промахивались в триллионы раз. Тишина в центрах галактик оказалась куда глубже.

title: Catching an Interstellar Wanderer with a Solar Sling
oneliner: Scientists propose a risky plan: swoop in extremely close to the Sun to slingshot and overtake a fleeing interstellar object.
description: Interstellar object 3I/ATLAS is racing away, and a regular rocket can't catch it. But if you dive toward the Sun and fire the engines at peak speed, gravity acts like a giant sling. This maneuver gives a chance to reach the target in a few decades, but the probe must survive hellish heat.
abstract: The feasibility of reaching the third interstellar object 3I/ATLAS via an Oberth solar maneuver (SOM) with chemical propulsion was studied. Modeling optimal interplanetary trajectories showed that among launches from 2031 to 2037, 2035 is the most efficient. The baseline scenario assumes a SOM at 3.2 solar radii and target interception within 35–50 years. Maximum spacecraft mass is about 500 kg. The required impulse can be provided by two or three solid rocket boosters or a reusable Starship Block 3 after refueling in low Earth orbit. A portion of the payload is inevitably allocated for thermal protection against the intense solar radiation at perihelion.
threads: Межзвёздный гость 3I/ATLAS улетает от нас навсегда. Но если нырнуть к Солнцу на расстояние всего в 3,2 его радиуса и там врубить двигатели, можно рвануть вдогонку. Это не фантастика — расчёты показывают, что запуск в 2035 году даёт шанс через 35-50 лет увидеть вблизи кусочек чужой планетной системы.

title: The Red Dwarf Oxygen Trap
oneliner: In the race for light around red dwarfs, infrared bacteria pull ahead, leaving oxygenic photosynthesis in the dust.
description: Around dim stars like TRAPPIST-1, photosynthesis barely produces oxygen—visible light is scarce. Infrared bacteria, on the other hand, thrive, gobbling up nearly all the available energy. As a result, an oxygen atmosphere may never form, and complex life never arise.
abstract: The origin of oxygenic photosynthesis on Earth preceded the Great Oxidation Event (GOE, 2.3 billion years ago) by about 700 million years. If we proportionally extrapolate this delay to an Earth-like planet around TRAPPIST-1 (a late M-dwarf, Teff 2560 K), then due to the extreme weakness of photosynthetically active radiation (only 0.9% of Earth's) the linearly estimated time until a GOE would be 63 billion years. Accounting for photoinhibition (light saturation and decline of photosynthesis) and extending the PAR range by 50 nm (increasing the photon count by a factor of 2.5) reduces this estimate to 1–5 billion years. However, non-oxygenic phototrophs capable of using near-infrared up to 1100 nm have access to a 22-fold photon advantage and likely dominate in such low-energy conditions. Therefore, on planets around late M-stars, oxygen may never reach significant levels, making the emergence of complex macroscopic life extremely unlikely.
threads: Тусклый красный прожектор TRAPPIST-1 дает так мало видимого света, что кислородный фотосинтез едва тлеет. Зато бактерии с инфракрасным зрением захватывают планету. Кислородная атмосфера может не возникнуть, оставив мир без животных.

title: One membrane – two mysteries of the Universe
oneliner: A stretched membrane-diaphragm catches gravitational waves and senses dark matter.
description: A new type of detector uses a membrane inside an optical cavity to search for two substances at once: gravitational waves and dark matter. Light pressure stretches the membrane, and six such membranes cover frequencies from 0.5 to 40 kHz — from a low hum to a high squeak. Sensitivity to spacetime ri
threads: Крошечная мембрана, натянутая между зеркал, ловит рябь пространства — гравитационные волны. А если рядом расположить два разных материала, она почувствует и невидимую тёмную материю. Один прибор вместо двух.

title: The Stumbling Dance of Two Planets Around TOI-4495
oneliner: Two planets around the star TOI-4495 are nearly in sync, but their dance has stumbled, prompting a search for hidden causes.
description: Around TOI-4495, two planets orbit wrapped in thick gas blankets. Their orbits are almost tuned—two revolutions of the inner planet for one of the outer—but exact synchronization is absent. The elongated path of the inner planet persists for billions of years thanks to the unusual elasticity of its
threads: Две планеты у звезды TOI-4495 танцуют почти в унисон: пока внутренняя успевает сделать два круга, внешняя — только один. Но их шаг сбился, и внутреннюю планету слегка трясёт. Это может сохраниться только если внутри неё не слишком много тепла теряется.

title: Solar Storm Illuminated Magnetic Fields in Space
oneliner: For the first time, scientists have spotted how a solar storm twists the stream of ultra-high-energy particles, turning them into a remote probe for magnetic fields.
description: Usually, cosmic rays arrive evenly from all directions, but when the Sun ejects a magnetic cloud, their paths bend. On November 4, 2021, the LHAASO observatory caught a temporary lopsidedness, offering the first-ever glimpse of invisible magnetic structures in interplanetary space.
abstract: Large-scale anisotropy of cosmic rays at teraelectronvolt energies had not previously shown temporal variability. LHAASO data for the first time recorded transient anisotropy in ion fluxes. Analysis of hourly excess/deficit intensity maps revealed a gradient characterizing the direction and magnitude of the anisotropy. On November 4, 2021, over a few hours, the significance of the enhancement exceeded 5σ in four energy bands (median values 0.7–3.1 TeV). The gradient follows an E^{-0.5} dependence; at ≤1 TeV it corresponds to a dipole anisotropy of at least 1% (or a weaker higher-order component). This opens a new way to study interplanetary magnetic structures using wide-angle detectors, complementing direct plasma measurements.
threads: Солнечная буря 4 ноября 2021 года на несколько часов перекосила поток частиц‑тяжеловесов, летящих из глубин Вселенной. Энергия в тысячи раз выше света, а магнитное облако от Солнца изогнуло их пути, как ураганный ветер — струи дождя. Теперь такие «искривления» помогут ощупывать невидимые поля в космосе, словно эхолот.

title: A Lunar Telescope Will Reveal the Shadows of Distant Black Holes
oneliner: A telescope on the Moon will make the shadows of supermassive black holes in other galaxies visible.
description: Combining antennas on Earth and the Moon will deliver incredible sharpness. Astronomers have selected six black holes in alien galaxies whose silhouettes will become visible. The real twist: to glimpse the thinnest ring of light around them, we'll need antennas floating in space between planets.
threads: Лунный телескоп на обратной стороне превратит систему Земля–Луна в исполинскую антенну, способную улавливать тени чёрных дыр из чужих галактик. Чтобы их увидеть, учёные отобрали шесть подходящих целей. Самое удивительное: можно будет разглядеть даже фотонное кольцо — тончайший световой обруч вокруг чернильного пятна — и проверить теорию гравитации там, где она работает на износ.

title: Perfect Entanglement Defies Infinite Acceleration
oneliner: Scientists have uncovered a quantum state that maintains maximum connection even at infinitely fast speeds.
description: Usually acceleration destroys quantum entanglement—the invisible bond between particles. But in a new study, physicists found a four-particle state where entanglement freezes entirely at its maximum level. This challenges old notions and paves the way for ultra-stable quantum links in space.
abstract: The relativistic dynamics of quantum entanglement in a four-qubit cluster state (CL4) are investigated within the fully operational formalism of Unruh–DeWitt detectors. It is shown that the 1–3 bipartite entanglement of the CL4 state remains strictly maximal at any acceleration, including the infinite acceleration limit. Thus, a previously undescribed phenomenon—the complete freezing of initially maximal entanglement in relativistic motion—is discovered and systematically characterized. The result refutes the widespread belief in the inevitable degradation of maximal entanglement under acceleration. This makes the CL4 state a promising resource for quantum information processing in non-inertial frames and curved spacetime.
threads: Обычно движение ослабляет невидимую квантовую связь. Но учёные нашли состояние из четырёх частиц, где связь остаётся максимальной даже при бесконечном ускорении — словно нить, которую нельзя порвать. Это открытие сулит сверхнадёжную квантовую связь для спутников и космических компьютеров.

title: The Ultimate Stiffness Limit of Neutron Stars
oneliner: The ratio of pressure to energy density in neutron stars cannot exceed 0.385 — that's the universal limit.
description: In a neutron star, matter is compressed to the limit. But even that limit has a limit: physicists have calculated that the ratio of pressure to energy density never exceeds 0.385. This law holds for any ultra-dense matter in the universe.
abstract: The equation of state parameter φ ≡ P/ε determines the response of matter to extreme compression. Its value at the center of the most massive neutron star, φ_c, sets a universal upper density limit for all visible matter, which, due to the nonlinearity of GR, turns out to be much lower than the naive limit of special relativity (φ=1). In this work, the theoretical upper bound on φ_c is refined by self-consistently accounting for the mass-shell stability condition near the stellar center alongside the causality constraint. An analysis within the IPAD-TOV framework yields an improved estimate φ_c ≲ 0.385, slightly exceeding the previously obtained limit of 0.374 but fully compatible with it. A refined scaling relation for neutron star compactness is also derived, the universality of which is verified for 284 realistic equations of state that include first-order phase transitions and exotic components. The obtained limit opens a model-independent window into the microphysics of cold, ultra-dense matter in a strong gravitational field.
threads: Нейтронная звезда спрессовывает гору до крупинки, но даже там материя не беспредельна: у давления и плотности есть строгий верхний порог. Оказалось, их отношение не превышает 0,385. Это число перекраивает понимание сверхплотной материи.

title: Dark Dance: How Energy and Matter Swap Roles
oneliner: A new study shows dark energy and dark matter exchange energy, and the direction of exchange has changed over time.
description: Eight models of interaction between dark energy and dark matter fit observations better than the standard theory. Surprisingly, in the past, dark energy density could have been negative—like an energy debt in the dance. The finding refines the history of the expansion of the universe.
threads: Тёмная энергия и тёмная материя не просто соседи — они обмениваются энергией, как танцоры в космическом танго. Но иногда один партнёр отдаёт больше, чем имеет, и баланс нарушается. Это может изменить наше понимание расширения Вселенной.

title: Thin Disks of Galaxies Already Existed in the Young Universe
oneliner: Pictures from the James Webb telescope showed that flat, pancake-like stellar disks appeared way earlier than we thought.
description: Astronomers studied distant galaxies when the Universe was just a few billion years old. They developed a new way to more accurately measure the thickness of stellar disks, even if they are slightly tilted. It turns out that thin disks, similar to our Milky Way, had already formed by that early time
abstract: The thickness of galactic disks constrains formation scenarios: whether thick components arise from early turbulence or later dynamical heating. A new methodology for edge-on galaxies fits a full 3D model, correcting for slight inclinations that biased previous single-disc fits upward. Applied to JWST data of 10^9–10^10 M⊙ galaxies at redshifts 1–3, it yields median scale height z₀ = 0.25 ± 0.14 kpc and axial ratio h_r/z₀ = 8.4 ± 3.7 — consistent with the Milky Way’s thin disk and ~1.6× thinner than local estimates. Thus, thin disks were already in place at z ~ 3. A hypothetical thick disk contributing 10% of the thin disk’s luminosity would be detected, implying any thick component must be fainter, supporting a scenario where thick disks form progressively through dynamical heating at z < 1.
threads: Галактики-блины в ранней Вселенной. Телескоп Уэбба показал, что тонкие звёздные диски существовали уже 11 миллиардов лет назад. Раньше думали, что они толстеют со временем, а теперь выходит наоборот: сначала появились изящные «блинчики», а пухлые толстые диски наросли позже. Это меняет картину взросления галактик.

title: Wind for the swift: how turbulence heats the solar corona
oneliner: Chaotic electric fields in plasma, like a sieve, let only fast particles through, creating 'tails' of superspeeds and heating the solar corona to millions of degrees.
description: The theory discovered: chaotic electric fields in plasma, like a sieve, sift fast particles from slow ones, forming a universal 'tail' of super-fast particles. The reason lies in the shielding effect: slow particles wrap themselves in charge clouds and become invisible to the field, while fast ones
threads: В плазме, как в игре «Царь горы», быстрые частицы получают дополнительный разгон от турбулентности, а медленные остаются в тени. Именно это, возможно, греет солнечную корону до миллионов градусов — загадка, мучившая физиков 80 лет.

title: Dark Matter Is Born from Ghostly Neutrinos
oneliner: Sterile neutrinos may have arisen in the first moments after the Big Bang and become dark matter.
description: Scientists have shown how sterile neutrinos were born in the hot young Universe. This explains why we don't see their X-ray glow and turns dark matter into a tool for measuring the temperature of the early cosmos.
abstract: Sterile neutrinos are a minimal and testable solution to the dark matter problem, but their radiative decay is constrained by X-ray observations. It is shown that cold dark matter from sterile neutrinos is efficiently produced during post-inflation reheating from inflaton decays with a branching ratio BR ≲ 10⁻⁴. This opens up parameter regions where mixing is small, X-ray constraints are avoided, and the observed dark matter density is reproduced. The allowed parameter space is systematically determined: sterile neutrino mass, mixing angle, inflaton mass, reheating temperature, and BR. Sterile neutrinos become a probe of inflationary reheating: future X-ray observations will provide information on the inflaton mass and the ratio m_φ/T_rh. Given a known inflaton mass, this yields a lower bound on the reheating temperature, orders of magnitude stronger than the bound from Big Bang nucleosynthesis.
threads: Тёмная материя могла возникнуть в первые мгновения после Большого взрыва, когда пространство молниеносно расширилось и раскалилось. Частицы-невидимки, стерильные нейтрино, могут сохранить память о том древнем пожаре и выдать себя слабым рентгеновским свечением.

title: Black Holes 'Weigh' the Expansion Rate of the Universe
oneliner: Three characteristic masses of merging black holes allowed a more precise measurement of the universe's expansion rate.
description: Analysis of 137 black hole mergers revealed three favorite masses: around 10, 18, and 33 solar masses. A flexible analysis, like a tuner, picked out these 'notes' more precisely than previous methods. As a result, the expansion rate of the universe was refined by 21%. This shows that the better we h
abstract: The precision of cosmological constraints from gravitational-wave spectral sirens critically depends on the completeness of the mass distribution (MD) description of binary black holes (BBH). We analyzed 137 BBH events from GWTC-4.0 using a novel semi-parametric method based on B-splines that adaptively places knots around the most informative structures of the MD, while keeping the parameter space dimensionality moderate. The flexible models identify three distinct peaks at ~10, 18, and 33 M⊙ and are statistically preferred over standard parametric models (Bayes factor up to 226). Since these features correlate with H0, the semi-parametric model yields a 12–21% improvement in H0 precision (depending on prior assumptions) compared to parametric ones, resulting in H0 = 57.8+21.9−20.6 km/s/Mpc in the best case. The results demonstrate that capturing the full complexity of the BBH mass distribution is necessary to realize the cosmological potential of spectral sirens as gravitational-wave catalogs grow.
threads: Чёрные дыры падают в космическую паутину не случайно: у них есть любимые размеры — 10, 18 и 33 солнца. По этим «нотам» можно услышать, как быстро раздувается Вселенная, и даже расслышать тёмную энергию.

title: Dance of the Invisible Giants: Gaia Finds a 'Desert' of Brown Dwarfs
oneliner: Gaia telescope finds ten times more brown dwarfs lurking around Sun-like stars on close orbits than previously thought — the 'desert' was a mirage.
description: Astronomers are reimagining the map of nearby stellar pairs. The zone of tight orbits around Sun-like stars was once called the 'brown dwarf desert,' thought to hold few objects heavier than planets but lighter than stars. Fresh Gaia data reveal that many stars are dancing with unseen companions — a
abstract: Using Bayesian inference based on astrometric data from Gaia DR3 for nearby (within 200 pc) sun-like stars and assuming single companions, the authors identified 2,673 systems (2.41% of the sample) with signs of companions of brown dwarf mass and orbital period less than 5 years. After accounting for observational biases, the true fraction of such stars is estimated at 10.4 (+0.8 / –0.6)%, significantly higher than previously reported values (<1%). Acknowledging the limitations of DR3 and the lack of epoch measurements, the researchers modeled scenarios with multiple systems and showed that some signals could be explained by several low-mass brown dwarfs and massive planets (>10 MJ), while high-mass brown dwarfs (>50 MJ) on short periods are rare. These results allow predicting the number of similar companions in the future DR4 release.
threads: Раньше была пустыня, а теперь — сад: телескоп Gaia нашёл, что у каждой десятой звезды типа Солнца может быть невидимый сосед — коричневый карлик. Это в десять раз больше, чем думали. Значит, наши представления о рождении планет требуют пересмотра.

title: The Icy Magnets of Uranus and Neptune
oneliner: Temperature differences inside the ice giants turn ordinary ice into a magnetic generator.
description: Deep inside Uranus and Neptune, monstrous pressure transforms ice into a substance where an oxygen framework is threaded with flowing protons. Temperature differences within the planet set these protons in motion, generating electric current and a magnetic field. This explains the weird magnetospher
abstract: The magnetic fields of Uranus and Neptune are anomalously tilted and multipolar. Recent studies confirm that their icy mantles are dominated by superionic water, which is thermodynamically stable under these conditions. This work demonstrates that superionic ice exhibits a pronounced proton thermoelectric effect: the maximum Seebeck coefficient inside Uranus reaches ~620 µV/K, and inside Neptune — 570–585 µV/K. Thus, temperature gradients in the icy shells drive proton convection, which generates the magnetic fields. This mechanism fully explains the differences in the planets' magnetic induction solely by the difference in their internal temperature gradients, and the calculated values agree with observations.
threads: На Уране и Нептуне лёд работает как батарейка: перепады температуры заставляют протоны течь, создавая магнитные поля. Это объясняет загадку их разной силы и странных форм.

title: The Secret of Warped Webs: A New Way to Compare Networks
oneliner: A new method inspired by terrain relief instantly finds matches in the most complex networks.
description: Graphs are diagrams made of dots and lines. Determining whether two graphs have the same structure, even if drawn differently, is often difficult. Scientists have found a way by envisioning a graph as a landscape of hills and valleys. Each node gets its own curvature measure. By comparing these curv
threads: Два клубка ниток спутаны по-своему, но узор внутри один. Как это выяснить быстро? Новый трюк: измерить «кривизну» изгибов в каждой нити — как отпечатки пальцев у дорожек лабиринта. Сравнил — и готово! Даже самые хитрые симметричные схемы щёлкает за секунды. В перспективе — мгновенный поиск похожих молекул и нейросетей.

title: Distant Quasar Sets Gamma-Ray Record
oneliner: The MAGIC telescopes caught a record-breaking gamma-ray burst from a black hole that lived 7 billion years ago.
description: Astronomers observed a gamma-ray flare from a quasar — the ultra-bright core of a galaxy with a black hole inside. The light traveled for 7 billion years. Unexpectedly, it turned out to have originated not from the hole itself, but from the dust ring surrounding it. This helps us understand how blac
abstract: The quasar TON 0599 (z=0.7247) is one of the few flat-spectrum radio quasars detected in the very-high-energy gamma-ray range (E > 100 GeV). Observations with the MAGIC telescopes from 15 to 29 December 2017 revealed a flare with a peak flux ~50% of the Crab Nebula above 80 GeV, followed by a decline. The VHE spectrum smoothly connects with simultaneous Fermi-LAT data and shows a break around 50 GeV, indicating the emission region lies beyond the broad-line region. Multiwavelength analysis (optical, near-IR, radio from WEBT, X-ray and optical/UV from Swift) showed strong correlation between bands. The emission is modeled by a one-zone leptonic model, where the high-energy peak is primarily due to external Compton scattering off photons from the dusty torus. This constrains the gamma-ray source location and confirms the role of the environment in particle acceleration.
threads: В декабре 2017 года телескопы MAGIC заметили, как далекий квазар вспыхнул гамма-лучами, став в 50 раз ярче Крабовидной туманности. Свет шел 7 миллиардов лет от черной дыры, окутанной пылью. Такие вспышки — редкие окна в эпоху, когда Вселенная была вдвое моложе.

title: Radio signal from space rang like a bell: rhythmic fading detected
oneliner: A radio signal from space rang like a fading bell, revealing the trembling of a magnetic cocoon around the ultra-dense core of a dead star.
description: A powerful radio pulse from a distant galaxy revealed eight fading bursts—like a cosmic bell that tolled and then went silent. This is the first observation of fading rhythms in such signals, and it reveals the trembling of the magnetic cocoon around the ultra-dense core of a dead star. From the rhy
threads: Радиовсплеск из далёкой галактики прозвенел как космический колокольчик: восемь импульсов затухали ровно, словно кто-то дёрнул струну. Это может быть первый «ритм сердца» нейтронной звезды — редкая улика, приближающая нас к разгадке тайны FRB.

title: Cosmic Mirages Test the Law of Gravity
oneliner: Distorted light from distant galaxies helped test gravity theories that could replace dark energy.
description: The accelerated expansion of the universe is usually explained by dark energy, but its nature is unknown. Perhaps it doesn’t exist, and gravity on cosmic scales deviates from Einstein’s law. Using maps of weak lensing—subtle distortions in the shapes of galaxies—scientists tested modified gravity th
abstract: The mystery of the accelerated expansion of the Universe drives the search for alternative theories of gravity. f(R) models, which extend General Relativity, can reproduce the background evolution of ΛCDM without explicit dark energy but alter the growth of structures. This work investigates several viable f(R) models using weak gravitational lensing data. The impact of these theories on the matter power spectrum, as well as on convergence and cosmic shear spectra, is analyzed. Data processing is performed within a Bayesian framework using the Cobaya and MGCobaya codes, which provide consistent predictions and comparison with observational data from weak lensing and cosmic microwave background lensing. It is shown that standard cosmological parameters remain consistent with the ΛCDM scenario for all models considered, which is expected due to degeneracy at the background level. However, nontrivial and model-dependent constraints on the characteristic parameters of several f(R) theories are obtained.
threads: Свет далёких галактик искажается, как отражение в кривом зеркале. Это слабое линзирование помогает понять, что движет расширением Вселенной: невидимая тёмная энергия или сама гравитация работает иначе. Новый анализ показывает: поправки к закону Ньютона могут объяснить ускорение, и найдены их допустимые пределы.

title: How a Crystal’s Hidden Order Sharpens Gamma-Ray Telescopes
oneliner: A slight crystal tilt—within a hair’s width—transforms it into a super-efficient gamma-ray detector, enabling lighter space telescopes.
description: Gamma rays are hard to catch. In a telescope’s crystal detector, they create a particle shower that usually fans out, demanding heavy hardware. But if the crystal’s atomic grid aligns with the ray, the shower tightens. A small, oriented crystal then does a better job, even measuring the gamma ray’s
threads: High-energy gamma rays from space vanish in a flash, but a trick with crystal alignment turns that flash into a fountain of particles, making them easier to catch. This new telescope design could sniff out dark matter.

title: Invisible Vortices Sculpt Supermassive Black Holes
oneliner: Vibrations in an invisible field spawn swirling particles of dark light, driving clouds of dark matter into supermassive black holes at breakneck speed.
description: In the early universe, giant black holes were born at a dizzying pace. The key lies in a dance of invisible fields: the trembling of the axion field and dark photons created vortex mixers that prevented matter clouds from fragmenting and whisked away their spin. Thus, quantum crumbs sculpted the see
threads: Невидимые аксионные поля в ранней Вселенной породили вихри из тёмных фотонов. Эти космические миксеры помогли облакам тёмной материи сжаться сразу в гигантские чёрные дыры. Так крошечные квантовые дрожания могли стать семенами самых массивных монстров, озадачивающих астрономов.

title: Сверхновая из детства Вселенной
oneliner: Космическая линза из скопления галактик помогла разглядеть сверхновую из детства Вселенной.
description: Самую далёкую сверхновую SN Eos подтвердили, разложив её свет в спектр. Взрыв произошёл в среде, почти лишённой тяжёлых элементов, — прямое доказательство рождения массивных звёзд в ранней Вселенной. Открытие стало возможным только благодаря гравитационному линзированию и поможет уточнить темп рожде
threads: Самая далёкая сверхновая, обнаруженная благодаря космической линзе, показывает: массивные звёзды рождались уже в младенческой Вселенной. Это заставит пересмотреть раннюю историю космоса.

title: How Weak Gravity Does Without Dark Matter
oneliner: Scientists have found a way to explain galaxy rotation by changing the law of gravity on the outskirts—no dark matter required.
description: This approach rewrites gravity's rules where it's barely felt—at the edges of galaxies. Under normal conditions, it aligns with Einstein's theory, but for weak fields, it adds a tweak. The solution needs no new particles and explains why galaxies spin faster than their visible mass permits.
threads: На окраинах галактик гравитация настолько слаба, что начинает вести себя не как обычно, а подчиняется странной симметрии, связанной с размером всей Вселенной. Новая теория объясняет это без темной материи и, возможно, изменит наше понимание космоса.

title: The Secret of Neutron Stars: Sizing Them Up
oneliner: A new method directly links the mass and radius of a neutron star to its internal structure—without any extra hypotheses.
description: Neutron stars are the densest objects in the universe. Their internal makeup has long been a mystery. A new approach uses mass and radius to pinpoint what’s inside, without relying on assumptions.
abstract: A new Bayesian method is proposed for inferring the equation of state of neutron stars from mass and radius observations and neutron matter theory. Unlike traditional parametrizations in the pressure–energy density space, here prior distributions are placed directly in the mass–radius space, which directly characterizes the observables. Exact inverse approximations are used to map back to the equation of state. A systematic comparison is carried out with inferences from standard parametrizations, quantifying systematic uncertainties stemming from prior assumptions in both approaches. It is shown that accounting for prior uncertainties is essential in all Bayesian methods. The proposed approach more fully covers the physically permissible mass–radius space, especially for small-radius configurations, while also boosting computational efficiency and greatly reducing dependence on the choice of priors. Direct parametrization in the space of observables offers a robust and efficient alternative to traditional methods.
threads: Как узнать, что внутри нейтронной звезды? Учёные придумали новый способ: смотреть прямо на её размер и массу, а не гадать о свойствах материи. Это всё равно что оценивать упругость мяча, сжимая его, вместо того чтобы лезть внутрь.

title: Why some bubbles grow and others collapse
oneliner: The fate of a true vacuum bubble is determined by its size and the strength of internal interactions — just like an ice crystal in supercooled water.
description: Physicists modeled the behavior of true vacuum bubbles inside a false vacuum using a simplified particle system. It turned out that a bubble either expands indefinitely or collapses, depending on its initial size and the strength of internal bonds. A similar mechanism could have triggered the Big Ba
abstract: False vacuum decay via bubble nucleation is investigated in the framework of a (2+1)-dimensional quantum Ising model with a longitudinal field. The field energetically splits the two ferromagnetic states with broken Z₂ symmetry, creating a true and a false vacuum. Using tree tensor networks, the microscopic dynamics of a single true-vacuum bubble (a region of spin-down) in a false-vacuum background (spin-up) after a sudden quantum quench are simulated. It is shown that the fate of the bubble — unlimited expansion or collapse — is determined by its geometric characteristics and the parameters of the Ising Hamiltonian. An experimental scheme based on Rydberg atom arrays is also proposed, allowing this dynamics to be reproduced and studied.
threads: Кубик льда в переохлаждённой воде растёт от малейшего толчка. Так и пузырёк истинного вакуума может раздуться до размеров Вселенной. Учёные выяснили, от чего зависит его судьба. Это приближает разгадку тайны Большого взрыва.

title: Neutrino Decay Reconciles Two Worlds
oneliner: Neutrino decay into invisible particles explains the discrepancy between cosmological and laboratory data on mass.
description: Cosmology saw neutrinos as lighter than fluff, while lab experiments demanded noticeable weight. Decay into light invisibles resolves the conflict: some of the mass simply vanishes over time. Ordinary neutrinos, however, aren't fit for such a trick.
abstract: The latest cosmological data (DESI DR2 and the cosmic microwave background) point to an upper limit on the sum of neutrino masses ∑mν ≲ 0.06 eV, which contradicts the lower limits from oscillation experiments. It is shown that neutrino decays into massless particles beyond the Standard Model with lifetimes τν ~ 0.01–1 billion years relax this limit to ∑mν < 0.23 eV (95% C.I.), bringing it into full agreement with oscillation data. For the first time, a cosmological analysis of neutrino decays into lighter neutrinos, consistent with measured mass differences, has been performed. Unlike decays into new physics particles, this scenario only slightly relaxes or even tightens the constraints depending on the mass hierarchy. The results demonstrate that moderately short-lived neutrinos can resolve the key cosmological contradiction without resorting to exotic models.
threads: Нейтрино могут распадаться на невидимые частицы, и это решает главный спор об их массе. Космологи считали, что нейтрино очень лёгкие, а физики требовали большей массы. Распад объясняет, куда делась разница: часть массы просто исчезла со временем, как картошка из дырявого мешка.

title: A Quantum Trick to Hunt for Faint Signals
oneliner: Sacrificing volume, noise filtering buys time to hear even a whisper.
description: Scientists have found a way to capture weak signals, sacrificing their strength to suppress noise. Quantum error correction, typically used to protect computations, here becomes a supersensitive filter: it makes an already quiet signal even quieter, but eliminates interference almost completely. Lon
abstract: A protocol for detecting rare signals in noisy environments using quantum error correction is proposed. The core principle is to separate signal from noise via unique higher-order correlations that arise from nonlinear processing during syndrome extraction. Error correction serves two functions: it suppresses physical noise, extending the coherence time of the logical qubit for signal accumulation; it discards part of the signal—the detected logical phase is on the order of the cube of the original signal strength. For rare signals occurring at random moments in the presence of local Markovian noise, a clear sensitivity advantage over conventional sensing strategies is demonstrated. This result paves the way for highly sensitive quantum sensors for sporadic events.
threads: Квантовый трюк: намеренно искажая сигнал, учёные слышат его лучше. Коррекция ошибок обычно исправляет сбои, но здесь она отсеивает шум, оставляя слабое эхо, выдающее скрытые сигналы. Так можно уловить шёпот из ранней Вселенной или едва заметные толчки тёмной материи.

title: Galactic Bubbles: How Black Holes Paint the Cosmos
oneliner: New simulation explains the nature of mysterious bubbles in the Circinus galaxy.
description: Simulations reveal that giant bubbles in the Circinus galaxy are sculpted by narrow black hole jets, not stellar winds. This unlocks the secret of how black holes govern galaxy growth.
abstract: Scientists have studied the Fermi and eROSITA bubbles in our Galaxy and similar structures in the nearby Circinus galaxy. Using hydrodynamic simulations, they showed that jets from the active galactic nucleus (AGN) create shock waves in the circumgalactic medium. Over ~0.95 million years, bubbles form that match the size and shape of radio and X-ray observations. Synthetic X-ray images and spectra reproduce the bright edges and spectral features; non-thermal emission from jet outflows significantly adds to the emission in the central hot spot. AGN winds produce bubbles that are too spherical with a broad base, contradicting the data. The misalignment of the bubbles' axis with the galaxy's rotation axis and the properties of the hot spot rule out the star formation hypothesis. The results confirm the jet-driven model as a common mechanism for the Circinus and Fermi bubbles; AGN jet feedback may play an important role in the evolution of disk galaxies.
threads: В центре почти каждой крупной галактики спит гигантская чёрная дыра. Иногда она просыпается и выдувает пузыри плазмы, сравнимые по размерам с самой галактикой. Новое моделирование показало, что эти пузыри формируются узкими джетами, словно раскалённое пламя паяльника в масле. Поняв их форму, мы сможем предсказывать, как чёрные дыры управляют рождением и смертью целых звёздных систем.

title: A Trap for Invisible Axions: A Magnetic Detector
oneliner: Scientists built a supersensitive detector to find dark matter particles, but so far they've only caught silence.
description: Physicists are hunting axions—light particles that might make up dark matter. They built a device with a levitating magnetized rod that should tremble if axions fly through it. In a new experiment, the rod stayed still, but the scientists set record constraints on how strongly axions interact with o
threads: Крошечный магнит парил в вакууме, как стрелка заколдованного компаса. Учёные ждали, что невидимый ветер тёмной материи качнёт его. Ветер не появился, зато физики поняли: он должен быть ещё слабее. Теперь строят детектор в сотни раз чувствительнее.

title: A Tiny Tweak to Inflation Theory Explains a Cosmic Anomaly
oneliner: A new perspective on the ripples of the newborn Universe, with a tiny addition, solves the mystery of the cosmic 'quiet zone'.
description: Generalized equations of the early Universe with a minimal correction better describe the 'quiet' large-scale patterns in ancient light maps, without disturbing the finely tuned small-scale picture. This hints at a gap in the standard inflation model.
threads: Ранняя Вселенная раздулась как пузырь, оставив рябь. Но на карте реликтового излучения зияет странная «тихая зона». Новая теория с крошечной поправочкой (всего 0,0001) объясняет эту загадку, не ломая стандартную модель. Возможно, мы нащупали кончик нити, ведущей к квантовой гравитации.

title: Gravitational waves refine cosmic expansion rate
oneliner: Combining gravitational wave signals with galaxy surveys yields a more precise measurement of how fast the universe is growing.
description: By combining gravitational waves from merging neutron stars with galaxy surveys, scientists refined the universe’s expansion rate to 67.9 km/s per megaparsec (6.4% uncertainty), bridging a long-standing debate.
abstract: A new measurement of the Hubble constant is presented, based on a joint analysis of standard sirens and three two-point correlation functions (3×2pt), which include weak gravitational lensing and galaxy clustering. Data from Dark Energy Survey Year 3 and the gravitational-wave transient catalog GWTC-4 from the LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA collaboration were used, along with measurements for event GW170817: the redshift of the host galaxy and the inclination angle of the superluminal jet. The result is H₀ = 67.9^{+4.4}_{-4.3} km/s/Mpc with 6.4% precision; the constraint on the matter abundance Ωm is improved by 22% compared to DES alone. Without jet information, the precision of H₀ drops to 9.9%. The work demonstrates that incorporating standard sirens into cosmological analysis of large surveys is a promising path toward resolving the Hubble tension.
threads: Two ultradense stars collided and sent tremors through space. By matching those shivers with a map of millions of galaxies, scientists have just clocked the universe’s growth spurt with record precision. The result: 67.9 km/s per megaparsec—hinting at a way to solve the biggest argument in cosmology.

title: Smashing a moon slowed down the main asteroid
oneliner: After DART rammed its moon, a day on asteroid Didymos stretched by 0.18 seconds.
description: The DART probe's impact with moon Dimorphos caused the main asteroid to spin slower. Measurements showed that Didymos's day lengthened by 0.18 seconds, and the asteroid itself became more flattened. It's the first time a kinetic impactor has reshaped and respun a celestial body.
threads: Казалось бы, врезаться на скорости 6 км/с в 160-метровый булыжник и чуть изменить его орбиту — уже успех. Но астрономы пошли дальше: они заметили, что главный астероид Дидим закрутился медленнее на доли секунды. Удар изменил его форму, сделав более приплюснутым. Как фигурист, который разводит руки, чтобы замедлить вращение. Теперь мы знаем: космический «толчок» может не только отклонить опасный объект, но и неожиданно изменить его собственное движение.

title: How Diamonds Communicate Over Distance
oneliner: For the first time, physicists have carried out a computational operation between two diamond qubits in separate rooms.
description: Two diamonds with atomic qubits exchanged quantum states via light. Thanks to instantaneous error correction, the operation went off without a hitch—a first for this type of gate. This advances the quantum internet: in the future, data centers in different cities will be able to compute a single tas
abstract: The work demonstrates an unconditional quantum CNOT gate between two remote solid-state devices based on diamond. The control and target qubits are implemented on the nuclear spins of carbon-13, while the electron spins of NV centers provide local logic operations, readout, and generation of remote entanglement via photonic channels. The system is calibrated by creating a Greenberger-Horne-Zeilinger state with confirmation of genuine four-particle entanglement between the nodes. Thanks to the use of deterministic logic, single-shot readout, and real-time feedback, operations are performed without postselection. These results demonstrate a key capability for solid-state quantum networks, paving the way to distributed quantum computing and testing of complex network protocols on fully integrated systems.
threads: В двух крошечных алмазах заперли по атому-кубиту. Потом натравили на них свет, и атомы, хоть и сидели в разных приборах, начали танцевать в такт. Один «позвонил» другому через оптоволокно, и вместе они выполнили квантовое вычисление. Так дальние города смогут обзавестись единым квантовым мозгом.

title: Wobbly Magnetars: The Secret Behind Bizarre Radio Flashes
oneliner: Fast radio bursts are born on lopsided magnetars, whose shaky rotation hides and reveals their beams.
description: Brief powerful radio signals from space behave oddly: some repeat, others are one-offs. A new explanation: their source is misshapen magnetars. The star spins like a lopsided top, its radio beam flickering toward us, then vanishing for years. This same crookedness blocks the detection of gravitation
threads: Астрономы терялись в догадках: почему одни космические радиосигналы повторяются, а другие — нет? Разгадка нашлась у кривых магнитаров. Если бы Земля была такой же неровной, нас бы швыряло, как на сломанной карусели. Но именно эта «корявость» прячет гравитационное эхо от наших приборов.

title: How Giant Black Holes Are Born in the Early Universe
oneliner: New simulations show that supermassive black holes grow from heavy seeds just 700 million years after the Big Bang.
description: A computer simulation of the early universe revealed: supermassive black holes are born from plump seeds with a million solar masses and grow into giants in mere hundreds of millions of years, explaining the mysterious 'little red dots' seen by the Webb telescope.
threads: В ранней Вселенной пылевые коконы скрывают гигантов: внутри них зародыши чёрных дыр массой в миллион Солнц быстро растут, поглощая газ. Именно эти объекты телескоп JWST видит как загадочные 'красные точки' — мгновение перед рождением сверхмассивных монстров.

title: The Mpemba Paradox: Hot Quantum Sensors Measure Temperature More Accurately
oneliner: The Mpemba effect enabled the creation of ultrafast quantum thermometers.
description: Waiting for thermal equilibrium is no longer necessary: physicists have found a way to use the excited state of a quantum probe for ultra-precise, instantaneous measurements. The principle resembles the sizzling of droplets on a skillet: a brief signal provides a rapid assessment. The discovery acce
abstract: This research links quantum thermometry with anomalous relaxation, showing that Mpemba-like inversions always amplify quantum Fisher information for temperature estimation at finite times. Thus, the Mpemba effect transitions from a thermodynamic curiosity into a concrete metrological resource.
