oneliner: An invisible laser trap caught 11,000 atoms — a record step toward quantum computers of a new scale.
description: Using a flat metasurface the size of a coin, replacing bulky lenses, scientists trapped 11,000 atoms for the first time. This breakthrough paves the way to quantum computers with tens of thousands of qubits, capable of solving problems beyond the reach of ordinary machines.
threads: Ловушка-невидимка шириной с монету удерживает 11 000 атомов, открывая путь к квантовым компьютерам размером с комнату. Такой чип однажды взломает любые коды.

title: Quantum fluctuations of light: how gravitons blur causality
oneliner: Quantum fluctuations of gravitons turn light cones from sharp boundaries into blurred probability clouds, which could radically change our understanding of black holes.
description: In classical physics, causality is strict: events can only influence each other within the light cone. But quantum gravity introduces uncertainty. Physicists computed the commutator of a scalar field in the presence of gravitons and showed that the support of the commutator becomes operator-valued.
abstract: We compute the commutator of a scalar field minimally coupled to gravity at first order in G_N. The commutator turns out to be operator-valued and contains terms with derivatives of Dirac delta functions, whose support is the Minkowski light cone. When evaluated on classical (coherent) graviton states, these terms bend the support of the commutator exactly so as to reproduce standard causality in classical curved spacetime. However, these same terms have variance, thus introducing uncertainty into causal connections between events. For a thermal state of gravitons at temperature T, we find that the probability distribution for [φ(t, x), φ(0)] ≠ 0 is Gaussian in x², centered on the classical light cone, with variance Var(x²) = 16 G_N T t³/3. This result is obtained after subtracting the universal vacuum contribution, which diverges logarithmically in the ultraviolet and is suppressed at large times.
threads: Световой конус — хрустальная граница между прошлым и будущим. Но квантовые дрожания гравитонов превращают эту грань в туманное марево. Вблизи чёрной дыры горизонт событий может стать размытым задолго до испарения. Классическая геометрия тает, уступая вероятностям — возможно, именно так природа скрывает сингулярности.

title: Why Do Early Galaxies Shine Brighter Than Expected?
oneliner: The James Webb Telescope revealed that in the young universe, stars were born in bursts — faster, but not more efficiently.
description: The Webb telescope studied the glow of early galaxies. The brightness puzzle is solved not by dust or giant stars, but by a sped-up birth rhythm. Stars ignited in short spurts, making galaxies shine unexpectedly bright.
abstract: To explain the slow evolution of ultraviolet luminosity functions at z > 10, as observed by JWST, a semi-analytical model was built and calibrated against data at z ~ 2–10. The analysis uncovered a shift in star formation regimes: at z ≲ 5, long timescales dominate with nearly constant efficiency, while at 6 ≲ z ≲ 10, short timescales take over. For z > 10, the best fit to observations comes from even shorter timescales without changing efficiency. Neither a dust-free scenario nor a ‘top-heavy’ initial mass function can reproduce the data at z ~ 14. A joint analysis of UV luminosity functions and stellar masses, derived with Prospector, allowed researchers to separate the contributions from various uncertainties. It turns out the evolution of star formation timescales—reflecting changing physical conditions during the era of galaxy assembly—is the main driver behind the observed picture. Moderate activity from active galactic nuclei may further amplify ultraviolet emission at z ~ 14.

title: Elemental Spectra of Cosmic Rays: Two Galactic Sources and the Extragalactic Transition Beyond the Knee
oneliner: A new analysis of protons, helium, and iron reveals two populations of galactic sources and an immediate emergence of an extragalactic component after the main knee.
description: Analysis of precision data from DAMPE, LHAASO, and IceTop on the spectra of protons, helium, and iron in the energy range 40 GeV – 500 PeV showed that the observed fluxes are well described by a sum of three components: a low-energy galactic component with a convex spectrum, a high-energy galactic c
abstract: The energy spectra of individual elements in cosmic rays in the range from 100×Z GeV to 1000×Z PeV exhibit two 'knee' breaks with rigidity dependence: at energies of about 15×Z TeV and 3×Z PeV, as well as three 'ankle' flattenings. The first ankle (≈500×Z GeV) also depends on rigidity, but the second (≈150 TeV for protons, ~1 PeV for helium) and, probably, the third (≈100 PeV for protons) do not. Thanks to the high measurement precision in the DAMPE and LHAASO experiments, as well as IceTop, it was possible for the first time to establish that all spectral features are well described by the sum of three components: a low-energy galactic component (with a convex spectral shape, fading after the TeV knee), a high-energy galactic component (including the PeV knee), and an extragalactic component. No additional galactic component is required in the 10 PeV – 1 EeV interval.
threads: На отметке 3 ПэВ галактический оркестр замолкает — но не слышно тишины. Внегалактические источники подхватывают мелодию без задержки, словно дирижёр передал палочку, и новый состав уже играет. Долгое время считалось, что наш Млечный Путь солирует вплоть до 4 ЭэВ. Теперь ясно: финал наступает раньше, и космические лучи с высочайшими энергиями — это гастролёры из далёкой вселенной.

title: Why do 'little red dots' vanish at z<3: evolution of environment and halo mass
oneliner: A change of environment and rapid halo mass growth turn the mysterious compact objects of the early Universe into ordinary galaxies.
description: 'Little red dots' (LRDs) — compact sources discovered by JWST at high redshifts — possess anomalously massive black holes and are much rarer at z<3. The study investigates the large-scale environment structure of 98 spectroscopically confirmed LRDs. It finds that at z>4 they prefer underdense region
abstract: The scarcity of Little Red Dots (LRDs) at $z<3$ stands in stark contrast to their abundance in earlier epochs. A study of their cosmic environment revealed that at $z>4$ they reside in underdense regions compared to the general galaxy population, but by $z \sim 3.5$ the contrast disappears. Dark matter halo masses, inferred from large-scale clustering, grow rapidly: from $\lesssim 10^{10.1} \, M_{\odot}$ at $z \sim 7.5$ to $\sim 10^{11.3} \, M_{\odot}$ at $z \sim 3.5$, approaching those of normal galaxies. The empirical stellar mass–halo mass relation yields stellar masses; the black holes remain overmassive at $z>4$, but converge to the local $M_* - M_{\rm BH}$ relation by $z \sim 3.5$. Increasing halo mass drives galaxy size growth due to enhanced rotation, and LRDs lose their compactness. Exhaustion of dense gas and/or boosted star formation alters their spectral energy distribution.
threads: В ранней Вселенной JWST заметил рубиновые точки — компактные коконы с чёрными дырами внутри. Они прячутся в разреженных областях, но когда кокон тёмной материи набирает массу, галактика расширяется и превращается в обычную. Этот танец объясняет, как возникали сверхмассивные чёрные дыры — словно куколки, становившиеся бабочками. Телескоп Roman сможет поймать миг исчезновения этих галактических куколок.

title: ExoVeil: How a Single Transit Shadow Reveals Exoplanets
oneliner: The ExoVeil machine-learning system detects exoplanets from a single transit, predicting a star’s normal brightness and flagging anomalies.
description: To detect distant worlds, astronomers usually wait for multiple passes of a planet across a star’s disk. But ExoVeil, using a Transformer architecture and self-supervised learning on 16,499 Kepler light curves, learned to recognize even single transits. A blind search uncovered 179 new candidates in
abstract: EXOVEIL is a transit-finding system that operates on raw flux time series and can detect single transits. Based on a Transformer model trained with transit masking on 16,499 Kepler light curves, it predicts the expected stellar flux; a variance-weighted matched filter extracts signals from the prediction residuals. An XGBoost classifier separates planets from false positives (AUC 0.938 on Kepler DR25). When injecting single transits with 1000 ppm depth, the system recovers 32%—a task where all classification methods show 0%. A blind search of 3737 Kepler stars revealed 179 new transit-like signals, including 46 single-transit candidates. Without retraining, the system detected all 47 confirmed TESS planets in the PLATO LOPS2 field, demonstrating cross-mission transferability. At PLATO’s 25-second cadence, transits down to 100 ppm are detectable, approaching the Earth-analog regime. Conformal prediction was applied for the first time, with an empirical coverage of 95.9%. The code, pre-trained weights, and candidate catalog are open-source (pip install exoveil).
threads: Одиночный транзит экзопланеты — как пропущенная буква в длинной книге. ExoVeil учит грамматику звёздного света, замечая малейшие «опечатки» на кривых блеска. Звезда пульсирует словно сердце, и даже лёгкий сбой ритма выдаёт невидимую планету. Благодаря такому подходу миссия PLATO сможет находить двойники Земли, даже если те лишь раз мелькнут на фоне своего солнца. Мы начинаем читать язык Галактики.

title: HC5N in TW Hydrae's disk: first detection of long cyanopolyynes in a protoplanetary disk
oneliner: Astronomers have detected HC5N molecule in a protoplanetary disk for the first time, pushing the boundaries of prebiotic chemistry in 'stellar nurseries'.
description: A team led by Steven C. Wampler, using the ALMA radio telescope, discovered the HC5N molecule in the protoplanetary disk of TW Hydrae — the longest cyanopolyyne ever recorded in a Class II disk. This finding shows that complex carbon chains can form and survive in the environment where planets are b
threads: В диске TW Гидры, молодой Солнечной системе в 60 парсеках от нас, астрономы поймали сигнал HC5N — углеродной цепочки из пяти атомов с азотом. Она пережила рождение планет, словно верёвка, прошедшая сквозь горн. Эти нити вплетаются в кометы и, возможно, несут пребиотические узоры на будущие Земли. Новые телескопы разглядят ещё более длинные цепочки — и, быть может, мы прочтём химическую партитуру жизни.

title: Nereid — Neptune's Last Regular Moon: Discovery with JWST
oneliner: JWST spectroscopy and dynamical simulations suggest Nereid likely isn't a captured Kuiper Belt object but formed around Neptune.
description: Neptune's third-largest moon, Nereid, was long considered an irregular moon captured from the Kuiper Belt. But new near-infrared observations from the James Webb Space Telescope revealed spectral features that don't match any known type of Kuiper Belt object. Combined with computer simulations recre
threads: Всю жизнь Нереида притворялась чужой. Её записали в пленницы пояса Койпера. Но JWST разглядел её истинное лицо: кристаллический лёд и ржавчина, невозможные для пришельца. Симуляции восстановили драму: она — последняя выжившая из первого хора спутников, искалеченная вторжением Тритона. Теперь она танцует одна — единственная уцелевшая нота разрушенной симфонии. И галактика, возможно, полна таких же сирот.

title: Light Poses as a Fermion During Acceleration
oneliner: On a special trajectory, an accelerated charge emits light that distributes its energy like fermions.
description: If an electric charge moves along a cleverly designed curve, its radiation starts behaving strangely: photons occupy strictly separate energy levels, as if passing through a turnstile. This effect blurs the line between bosons and fermions and opens up new possibilities for quantum technologies.
abstract: It is shown that an accelerated point charge can emit photons with an apparent Fermi-Dirac spectrum, despite the bosonic nature of the radiation and the lack of restrictions on occupation numbers (0 or 1). The effect is caused by a special class of acceleration kinematics and does not require invoking thermal equilibrium, event horizons, or statistical ensembles. Thus, purely kinematic conditions allow bosonic radiation to mimic fermionic statistics in the observer's perception. This deepens our understanding of the connection between charge motion and observed quantum distributions.

title: Hearing Precision: How to Predict the Capabilities of Quantum Sensors from the Sound of a Kettle
oneliner: Machine learning enables quick estimation of a quantum sensor's maximum precision from a few simple measurements.
description: A quantum system can act as an ultra-sensitive sensor, but its ultimate precision used to be calculated by fully reconstructing all properties — a laborious process. Now scientists have shown that a few simple measurements suffice: a machine learning algorithm predicts precision based on particle co
abstract: Quantum Fisher Information (QFI) is a fundamental measure in quantum metrology, setting the ultimate precision limit for parameter estimation via the Cramér-Rao bound. Direct computation of QFI requires knowledge of the full density matrix, which becomes resource-intensive as the Hilbert space dimension grows. Predicting QFI for many-body systems is possible from a limited set of experimentally accessible quantities using Support Vector Regression (SVR). Analysis of physically motivated features showed that dominant ones are collective covariance and low-order spectral moments of the density matrix. As system size increases, the predictive power of collective spin moments alone decreases. The results demonstrate that accurate determination of metrological sensitivity is achievable without full quantum tomography, relying only on key information sectors.
threads: Учёные нашли способ предсказывать точность квантового датчика по паре простых замеров, не восстанавливая всё состояние. Ключ — в спектральных тонкостях и связях внутри системы. Как угадать кипение чайника по вибрации, не открывая его.

title: Cosmic Strings: How a Precise Decay Calculation Reshapes the Gravitational-Wave Universe
oneliner: New numerical simulations reveal that metastable cosmic strings decay faster than predicted by the thin-string approximation, demanding a revision of cosmological forecasts.
description: Cosmic strings—one-dimensional topological defects with colossal energy density—could have formed in the early Universe. Their decay via quantum production of monopole-antimonopole pairs determines whether they survive to the present day and what gravitational-wave signal they leave behind. Traditio
threads: Космические струны — возможно, самые туго натянутые объекты в истории мироздания. Каждый сантиметр сжат чудовищной силой в миллион тонн. Их разрыв — квантовое исчезновение, прыжок из бытия в ничто. Мы думали, что этот процесс тянется веками; теперь знаем — он стремителен. Слишком быстр, чтобы звучать басами в гравитационном хоре. Но именно эта торопливая гибель дарит надежду наземным детекторам — уловить их высокий, пронзительный финальный аккорд.

title: Quantum Cat Made of 120 Photons
oneliner: Created a record-breaking quantum "coin": a light pulse of 120 photons spins, showing heads and tails at once, with 96% accuracy.
description: Physicists created a quantum state of light in which 120 photons act like a spinning coin—heads and tails are seen at once. The new method combines a quantum switch with a precise mathematical trick, achieving 96% accuracy even with noise. This pushes forward the test of the boundary between the qua
abstract: The study focuses on generating large "cat states" using the dynamic invariant of hybrid qubit-boson systems under time-dependent Hermitian or non-Hermitian Hamiltonians. The formalism of universal quantum control is employed, where the system dynamics are analyzed in an auxiliary representation via a unitary transformation conditioned on the qubit state. The Heisenberg equation for the dynamic invariant imposes constraints on the Hamiltonian, encoding controlled evolution. When the qubit is prepared in a balanced superposition, the bosonic mode deterministically evolves from the vacuum state to a cat state with an average photon number exceeding 120. In the Hermitian case, generation is perfect; in the non-Hermitian case, fidelity exceeds 0.962. The method is also applicable to creating large squeezed and four-component cat states. This work advances the theory of universal quantum control to hybrid discrete-continuous systems.
threads: Квантовые «кошки» — состояния света, которые живут сразу в двух реальностях. Новый метод позволяет создать такую кошку из 120 фотонов (прежний рекорд — 40). Даже с помехами точность 96%. Это шаг к пониманию границы квантового мира и созданию надёжных квантовых устройств.

title: Cosmic Sweetness: Erythrulose – The First Sugar in the Interstellar Medium
oneliner: In a molecular cloud at the Galactic Center, astronomers have detected for the first time the four-carbon ketose sugar erythrulose, pointing to the possibility of prebiotic synthesis of sugars in space.
description: Using the ultra-sensitive Yebes 40m and IRAM 30m radio telescopes, scientists identified spectral lines of erythrulose (C₄H₈O₄) in the molecular cloud G+0.693. This discovery proves for the first time that sugars, key components of nucleic acids, can be synthesized in the interstellar medium from si
abstract: The origin of monosaccharides in prebiotic conditions is a central problem in origin-of-life research. Laboratory syntheses yield insufficient concentrations, and findings of sugars in meteorites point to their extraterrestrial origin. This is the first report of a sugar (erythrulose, a chiral C4-ketose) detected in the interstellar medium. The detection was made using data from ultra-sensitive broadband spectral surveys of the molecular cloud G+0.693-0.027 in the direction of the Galactic center, performed with the Yebes 40m and IRAM 30m telescopes. Erythrulose is at least eight times more abundant than similar three-carbon sugars, which remained undetected. Quantum-chemical and astrochemical models show that erythrulose efficiently forms on interstellar dust grains from simpler two-carbon aldehydes and alcohols. Since ketoses easily isomerize to aldoses in aqueous environments, interstellar erythrulose could have replenished the pool of sugars available for early metabolic and replication processes.
threads: Эритрулоза — сахар, знакомый любителям автозагара, — плавает в облаке у центра Галактики. В морозной тишине, на ледяных пылинках, молекулы сами собираются в узоры жизни. Химия без пробирок, в галактическом масштабе. Фред Хойл мечтал, что облака сеют жизнь; теперь мы находим в них сахарную пыльцу. Возможно, наша биохимия началась с чужого рецепта.

title: Evaporating Primordial Black Holes and the Mystery of Lithium-7 Deficit
oneliner: Evaporation of primordial black holes could have destroyed the excess lithium-7, aligning Big Bang predictions with observed abundance.
description: The primordial lithium-7 problem is a major contradiction in standard cosmology: Big Bang nucleosynthesis theory predicts three times more lithium-7 than observed in old stars. The authors of this new work propose an elegant solution: the excess lithium is destroyed by neutrons born from the evapora
abstract: A mechanism is proposed to reduce the theoretically predicted overabundance of lithium-7 using baryons evaporated from primordial black holes. It is shown that neutron capture by lithium-7 nuclei leads to the formation of unstable lithium-8 or beryllium-8 nuclei, which rapidly decay into two helium-4 nuclei. As a result, the fraction of lithium-7 relative to baryon density can be lowered to observed values. This scenario connects the physics of primordial black holes with the solution to the longstanding cosmological problem of light element abundance.
threads: Пересолив литиевый суп, Вселенная тут же призвала на помощь крошечных поваров — первичные чёрные дыры. Их нейтронные щипцы аккуратно превратили избыток лития-7 в гелий, сшив теорию с наблюдениями и оставив шрам в первозданном свете. Ошибок нет — есть лишь тонкая настройка.

title: Hidden Growth of Supermassive Black Holes from a Parallel Brane
oneliner: Five-dimensional black strings allow mass to accumulate from a hidden sector, explaining the mysterious giants of the early universe.
description: Why did black holes of a billion solar masses already exist in the young universe? Standard accretion mechanisms can't keep up. A new hypothesis suggests that our brane shares a common horizon with a neighboring, invisible brane. Matter falling onto that hidden side of the horizon increases the tota
threads: Видимая крона массивной чёрной дыры в нашей Вселенной обманчива: её истинные корни уходят в невидимый субстрат параллельной браны. Там, в пятом измерении, скрыт источник роста гиганта. Детектор LISA готовится услышать гравитационный шум этих корней, превращая теорию в слышимую симфонию пространства-времени.

title: Dark Matter Trembles: How to Hear Its Cosmic Note
oneliner: Ultralight invisible matter shakes space, and cosmic laser antennas single out its sole note.
description: Ultralight dark matter shakes fundamental constants — the basic properties of light and matter. Future space antennas LISA and Taiji can pick up this tremor: clever data processing removes noise, leaving a clean signal from the acceleration of test masses. And a local observation method has increase
abstract: Ultra-light dark matter coupled to the Standard Model can induce coherent oscillations of fundamental constants, generating narrowband signals in precision interferometry experiments. A systematic analysis of such oscillations' impact on space-based laser interferometers (LISA, Taiji, BBO) is performed. Based on one-way intersatellite measurements, we examine instrument-level effects: composition-dependent acceleration of test masses, laser frequency variation due to cavity length changes, refractive index influence, and clock contribution. Signals are passed through the full data processing chain, including time-delay interferometry and clock noise suppression. It is shown that the observability depends on the single-link response structure: laser frequency variation is suppressed similar to phase noise, while directional signals, e.g., test mass oscillations, persist. A local observable isolating the differential motion between test mass and optical bench is constructed, and its sensitivity to dilaton-gluon (d_g) and dilaton-electron (d_e) couplings is derived.

title: Tiny Red Dots: An Ultraviolet Look at the Mysterious Compact Objects of the Young Universe
oneliner: JWST has discovered a population of compact red objects whose ultraviolet emission helps reveal the nature of growing supermassive black holes.
description: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) has uncovered a new enigmatic population of objects dubbed 'little red dots' (LRDs). Analysis of about a hundred of these objects shows their ultraviolet light is unlike that from normal galaxies: it's redder and more compact. Scientists suspect this light comes
threads: Всего через 600 миллионов лет после Большого взрыва в космической тьме уже горят красные огни. Это не обычные звёзды и не галактики — это растущие сверхмассивные чёрные дыры, окутанные плотными газовыми коконами. Их ультрафиолетовое свечение, пробиваясь сквозь толщу вещества, приобретает оттенок закатного солнца. Понять природу этих «красных фонариков» — значит приблизиться к разгадке одной из главных загадок: как во Вселенной так рано появились гигантские чёрные дыры.

title: How Cosmic Carbon Rings are Born: Ion Chemistry in Cold Clouds
oneliner: Ion-molecule reactions involving C₁₁H₉⁺ lead to the formation of acenaphthene derivatives — important precursors of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons in the interstellar medium.
description: Scientists studied the reactivity of C₁₁H₉⁺ ions with methylnaphthalenes under conditions mimicking cold molecular clouds. Experiments on the PIRENEA setup showed the formation of C₁₂H₁₁⁺ and C₁₃H₁₃⁺ products, which upon irradiation transform into the stable acenaphthylene cation. Quantum chemical c
abstract: Using the multipurpose PIRENEA setup, the reactivity of benzylium-type isomers C₁₁H₉⁺ with dimethylnaphthalene was investigated under isolated conditions mimicking radiative association. Products C₁₂H₁₁⁺ and C₁₃H₁₃⁺ were detected, their ratio depending on the specific dimethylnaphthalene isomer. Subsequent UV-visible irradiation allowed the identification of the acenaphthylene radical cation C₁₂H₈•⁺ as the most stable photofragment. Density functional theory calculations and molecular dynamics simulations confirm that long-lived ion-molecular complexes promote the formation of C-C bonds and the closure of a five-membered ring. The results point to a new pathway for the formation of five-membered rings during the growth of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons under low-pressure and low-temperature conditions, which may explain the recent detection of acenaphthylene-like particles in the cold cloud TMC-1.
threads: В сердце холодных облаков, где царит почти абсолютный ноль, углеродные ионы роятся, как пчёлы. Они прививают метильные группы к ароматическим кольцам, создавая стабильные катионы — зародыши космической органики. Возможно, именно так Вселенная вышивает молекулярные узоры, ведущие к жизни.

title: Game or simulation: what’s the best way to learn the photoelectric effect?
oneliner: Students enjoy learning through a game, while simulation helps solidify their knowledge.
description: The game 'Photon Jump' is like a cooking experiment, while the simulation is a tried-and-true recipe. Together they make the quantum effect understandable even to those far from gaming.
abstract: The study examined the influence of the sequence of using the educational game 'Photon Jump' and the PhET photoelectric effect simulation on students' perception of digital tools in physics education. In a quasi-experimental crossover design with counterbalanced order, 55 engineering-physics students were divided into comparable groups; each group went through the game and simulation in a different order, then answered 9 open reflective questions. Results showed a clear preference for the game activity. Frequency of gaming experience did not correlate with willingness to use such tools, indicating broad accessibility. Thematic analysis revealed that intuitive, inquiry-based learning through the game was complemented by the analytical component of the simulation.
threads: Маленькая игра «Прыжок фотона» помогла студентам лучше понять, как свет выбивает электроны из металла. Оказалось, что после игры даже серьёзная симуляция кажется полезной. Возможно, будущие инженеры будут учить кванты, спасая мир в видеоиграх.

title: Cosmic Duel: How Gravitational Waves and Lenses Help Resolve Cosmology's Biggest Dispute
oneliner: The combined use of gravitational-wave standard sirens and strong lensing has, for the first time, enabled a measurement of the Hubble constant without relying on any cosmological model.
description: The Hubble constant, which determines the expansion rate of the Universe, remains the subject of fierce debate: measurements in the early and late Universe diverge by 6σ. To adjudicate this 'Hubble tension,' astrophysicists combined data from 142 gravitational-wave events in the GWTC-4 catalog with
threads: Вселенная расширяется, и два классических метода дают настолько разные ответы, что это превратилось в кризис космологии. Новый подход сталкивает две независимые линейки: гравитационные волны от слияния чёрных дыр и гигантские линзы из света квазаров. Первые напрямую измеряют расстояние, вторые — геометрию. Вместе они выносят независимый вердикт. Пока точность хромает, но телескопы следующего поколения помогут выяснить, кто был прав — Planck или сверхновые.

title: How to Make Gold and Copper Conduct Without Resistance
oneliner: Scientists have discovered that ultrathin layers of gold and copper can conduct current without loss, and precise alignment with an insulator makes this possible at higher temperatures.
description: Ordinary gold and copper are not superconductors, but in layers just a couple of atoms thick, their resistance drops to zero at extreme cold. A boron nitride coating, properly aligned, raises the operating temperature to 7 K, bringing superconducting electronics closer to reality.
abstract: Inducing superconductivity in noble metals (Au, Ag, Cu) remains a fundamental challenge, where quantum confinement in atomically thin layers triggers only a marginal effect. First-principles calculations reveal elemental specificity: intrinsic superconductivity is suppressed in Ag by a stiff phonon spectrum, but it appears in trilayer Cu (TC ≈ 0.78 K) due to an enhanced density of states and in five-layer Au (0.63 K) thanks to phonon softening. In h-BN/Cu(111) heterostructures, the critical temperature critically depends on the interlayer configuration: the thermodynamically stable N-terminated interface yields TC ≈ 3.23 K, while the metastable B-terminated one raises it to 7.00 K. The enhancement is caused by a Lifshitz transition, where the Fermi surface touches the Brillouin zone boundary at the M point, increasing the electron-phonon coupling beyond the density-of-states effect. These results bridge the understanding of intrinsic two-dimensional superconductivity with atomic-scale interface design, offering a strategy for functionalizing noble metals as superconductors.

title: When a Quantum Particle Blurs Causality
oneliner: When the source of gravity behaves like a quantum object, the boundaries between cause and effect blur.
description: If a particle has no precise position, as if smeared across space, then light cones—the boundaries of causality—also lose their sharpness. Because of this, events can both have and not have a causal connection, erasing the line between past and future.
abstract: The influence of quantum matter on the causal structure of spacetime in the low-energy limit is investigated. A massless scalar field propagating in a spacetime with a Newtonian gravitational potential created by a nonrelativistic quantum particle is considered. It is found that light cones are subject to an operator-valued Shapiro delay, leading to three key consequences. First, the shifts of causal boundaries become non-commuting observables, endowing the causal structure with an irremovable quantum uncertainty. Second, the causal relation between two fixed points can be in a superposition of timelike and spacelike configurations. Third, taking the trace over the source smears the Wightman singularity on the light cone, creating an effective ultraviolet cutoff. Thus, quantum matter does not merely fluctuate in spacetime but makes the causal structure itself quantum, even without considering quantum gravitons.
threads: Когда источник гравитации — квантовый, границы причины и следствия расплываются. Световые конусы больше не строги: события могут и влиять друг на друга, и не влиять — одновременно. Такая квантовая причинность ведёт к новому пониманию времени.

title: Magnet in Superposition: Quantum Compass
oneliner: A method to create a quantum state of a large levitating magnet for ultra-precise measurements has been proposed.
description: Physicists have found a way to bring a large magnet into a quantum state where it spins both clockwise and counterclockwise at once. This split gives unimaginable precision for detecting weak magnetic fields. The main obstacle is collisions with air molecules, but a carefully chosen shape and a vacu
abstract: A top-down approach is proposed for generating macroscopic GHZ-type spin states using a levitating ferromagnet. The strong coupling between the collective spin and crystal lattice rotation allows mechanical control of the collective spin. It is shown that the resulting macrospin superposition provides the Heisenberg limit of precision—quantum Fisher information scales proportionally to the square of particle number. The role of symmetry and geometry in the decoherence process caused by gas collisions is analyzed, enabling the determination of experimentally achievable conditions. The applicability of a levitating cylindrical ferromagnet's macrospin superposition for testing spin-dependent wavefunction collapse models is discussed.
threads: Левитирующий магнит может вращаться сразу влево и вправо — квантовый фокус, обещающий сверхчувствительные датчики. Это приближает разгадку, почему наш «нормальный» мир не живёт по правилам микромира.

title: Neutron Stars Narrow the Limits of Color Superconductivity
oneliner: Multi-messenger observations of neutron stars, combined with Bayesian inference, have put tight limits on the color superconductivity order parameter in quark matter.
description: By combining data from NICER, LIGO, and heavy pulsars with quantum chromodynamics theory, scientists explored the properties of dense matter in neutron star cores. A hybrid parametrization of the equation of state using Gaussian processes and neural networks provided, for the first time, tight const
abstract: At asymptotically high densities, QCD predicts a transition of quark matter into a color-flavor-locked (CFL) superconducting phase, but away from the asymptotics, the gap magnitude is undetermined. Multimessenger observations of neutron stars are combined with perturbative QCD and chiral effective theory in a Bayesian analysis using a neural network parameterization of the equation of state. Matching with QCD at a baryon chemical potential of 2.6 GeV, including next-to-leading order corrections for the CFL contribution, yields a gap Δ*_CFL = 34^{+32}_{-28} MeV and an upper limit of 66 MeV (95% credible) — twice as precise and at the lower end of model predictions. For the first time, a constraint on the high-order pressure constant pQCD: c0 = -21^{+9}_{-8} is obtained. It is shown that color-superconducting pairing contributes only a small amount to the pressure, deepening the connection between observable stellar parameters and the phase diagram of strong interactions.
threads: В сердце нейтронной звезды кварки сплетаются в дуэты и кружатся в замысловатом танце, скованном цветом и ароматом. Новый анализ пульсаций и гравитационных волн показал: он куда тише, чем мы воображали, — словно безмолвный вальс под сурдинку. Когда инструменты будущего позволят расслышать самые слабые ритмы, мы, быть может, впервые уловим подлинный голос экзотической материи, что прежде звучал лишь со страниц фантастических романов.

title: Watery Surprise of a Young Super-Earth
oneliner: The JWST telescope found water in the atmosphere of a 23-million-year-old planet, hinting that planets, like slow-cooked meals, build up heavy elements over time.
description: For the first time, the JWST telescope studied the atmosphere of the young super-Earth V1298 Tau c. Water vapor was detected, but there were fewer heavy element 'spices' than in mature planets. This suggests that planets accumulate metals over time—like a dish that gets saltier as it cooks.
abstract: Using the NIRISS/SOSS instrument on the James Webb Space Telescope, a transmission spectrum was obtained for the atmosphere of planet V1298 Tau c — a young (23 Myr) super-Earth analog orbiting a solar-type star. Water was detected with a log volume mixing ratio of –1.83 (+0.68/–0.77); no other molecules were found. Atmospheric models with and without accounting for stellar heterogeneity yield consistent results. The metallicity was determined as [O/H] = 14.8 (+56.0/–12.3) relative to solar, comparable to other young planets, including the massive outer companion V1298 Tau b, but systematically lower than the metallicity of mature planets of similar masses and temperatures. These data point to a possible evolution of the mass-metallicity relationship of exoplanets with age.

title: First Confident SiO Signal in the Atmosphere of a Directly Imaged Companion TWA5B: A Key to Refractory Material and Clouds
oneliner: High-resolution M-band spectroscopy has detected gaseous silicon monoxide in the young super-Jupiter TWA5B, revealing its refractory composition and the absence of silicate clouds.
description: Using the CRIRES+ spectrograph on the VLT in the 3.5–5.2 µm range, the CRIMSON team for the first time robustly detected SiO lines (S/N=7.5) in the atmosphere of the directly imaged companion TWA5B. This allowed them to measure the refractory silicon abundance and, combined with CO and H2O data, rec
abstract: In the spectrum of the giant exoplanet TWA 5 B, obtained in the M-band with the CRIRES+ instrument, gaseous SiO was confidently detected (signal-to-noise ratio = 7.5) via its ro-vibrational band at 4 µm. The SiO abundance was log(SiO) = -3.56^{+0.42}_{-0.32} by volume, indicating nearly complete absence of magnesium-silicate cloud condensation — silicon is present almost exclusively in the gas phase. Joint analysis with volatile species CO (S/N=9.1) and H₂O (S/N=18.8) yielded C/O (stellar), O/Si and C/Si (moderately sub-stellar) ratios, as well as a super-stellar Si/H ([Si/H]_star = 1.41^{+0.42}_{-0.32}). These proportions are consistent with formation via core accretion beyond the CO snowline or via gravitational instability with subsequent solid enrichment. Gas-phase SiO is a unique tracer of cloud properties in hot gas giants, enabling the study of dominant condensates in populations of directly imaged planets and isolated brown dwarfs.
threads: Кремний — основа скал — в атмосфере горячего супер-юпитера TWA5B испарён и светится, словно газ в неоновой трубке. CRIRES+ поймал монооксид кремния — дым из космической плавильни, где выплавлялись силикатные глыбы. Обилие SiO говорит, что облака ещё не окутали этот мир. С новыми телескопами мы поймаем момент их конденсации и нарисуем первую карту облачности для сотен далёких планет.

title: Quantum Eraser Captures Two Pictures in One Snap
oneliner: Entangled photons let scientists take two different images at once, with the choice of which to see made after the photo is taken.
description: This research turns a famous quantum puzzle – the delayed-choice eraser – into a practical imaging method. It uses entangled photon pairs to record both brightness and phase information in one snapshot. Later, analyzing a partner photon determines which image is revealed. The technique ensures perfe
abstract: The quantum-erasure imaging (QEI) protocol converts the delayed-choice principle into a practical imaging method. Entangled photon pairs encode two classical modalities: absorption T(x,y) and the cosine quadrature of the phase φ(x,y). After a single acquisition of time-tagged coincidences, retrospective sorting by the state of the remote ancillary photon allows reconstruction of T (when measured in the H/V basis) or an interference pattern with visibility ∝ (2√T/(T+1)) cos φ (in D/A). Adjusting the analyzer provides a continuous transition between modalities. Balanced two-channel estimators are derived with analyzer-independent denominators (completeness/no signaling), along with Fisher information and Cramér–Rao bounds, proving equivalence to time division under marked randomization. Advantages of QEI include single-shot recording, perfect alignment, and remote/delayed mode selection. The work is illustrated by Monte Carlo simulations, with open-source code available.
threads: You take a photo of a flower. Later, by flipping a switch on a distant device, you decide whether the picture shows bright petals or a wavy heat map of the air around it. The switch affects the image even though the flower is long gone. This quantum trick lets scientists capture two truths in one click.

title: Diocotron Instability in Pulsar Magnetospheres: Charge Diffusion and Radio Variability
oneliner: 3D simulations reveal how diocotron instability in the closed field-line region of a pulsar magnetosphere triggers charge diffusion and radio emission modulation, explaining pulsar variability.
description: Diocotron instability is a non-axisymmetric plasma instability caused by differential rotation in the equatorial plane of pulsar magnetospheres. The authors ran a series of 3D PIC simulations for aligned and oblique rotators. The instability grows within roughly one rotation period and gives rise to
abstract: Diocotron instability is a non-axisymmetric plasma instability expected in the differentially rotating equatorial plane of pulsar magnetospheres. 3D PIC simulations were performed for aligned and oblique rotators. The instability grows on timescales of the rotation period and forms a stable m=1 mode—a rotating dipole charge asymmetry in the equatorial disk. Stochastic fluctuations in the mode’s amplitude and angular velocity trigger cross-field diffusion, rapidly transporting charges through the closed zone to the light cylinder. In the nonlinear phase, the m=1 mode generates electric field perturbations that can modulate the polar cap potential drop and the radiation beam angle, which are linked to nulling, periodic amplitude modulation, and drifting subpulses.
threads: Электрические вихри в сердце пульсара живут своей жизнью: они разрастаются за доли секунды и заставляют заряды танцевать поперёк магнитных линий. Словно дрожащий камертон, дипольная мода модулирует радиоимпульс, порождая загадочные нуллинги и дрейфы. Моделирование предсказывает, что новые радиотелескопы смогут увидеть этот танец в деталях — и тогда пульсары заговорят на языке плазменных неустойчивостей.

title: The shape of the xenon nucleus is no longer a mystery
oneliner: Physicists have reliably established for the first time: the xenon-129 nucleus is not a sphere, but a shape stretched in three directions at once, like a potato.
description: By colliding xenon and lead nuclei at the Large Hadron Collider, physicists reconstructed the true shape of the xenon-129 nucleus from the debris scattering, like splashes from an impact. It turned out to be a lumpy body, stretched in three directions—almost like an ordinary potato. The method turns
abstract: The correlation properties of the xenon-129 nucleus were quantitatively extracted from collider data using Bayesian inference. By merging a deformed rotor model, which captures the many-body dynamics of protons and neutrons, with hydrodynamic simulations of collision evolution, a global analysis of Xe-Xe and Pb-Pb events was performed. The shape of 129Xe was found to be nearly maximally triaxial, consistent with mean-field calculations for xenon isotopes away from closed shells. From the extracted ground-state information, two- and three-particle correlations were computed, imposing new constraints on ab initio nuclear theory. This establishes collider experiments as a pathway to quantitatively probe nucleon correlations driven by residual strong-force interactions.
threads: Ядро ксенона — не аккуратный шар, а тройная гантель. Физики на БАК, сталкивая его с другими ядрами, впервые точно измерили эту форму. Открытие сулит прорыв в понимании ядерных сил и, возможно, создании новых сверхтяжёлых элементов.

title: Relativistic dynamics of light sails: how specular and diffuse reflection govern acceleration to sub-light speeds
oneliner: A thorough analysis of radiation dynamics showed that the contribution of diffuse scattering to sail thrust turns into braking upon reaching a speed of 0.75c, but the total force continues to accelerate the craft.
description: The article systematically investigates for the first time the influence of incident, specularly reflected, and diffusely scattered light on the thrust of a high-speed relativistic light sail. Using radiation dynamics equations, it is shown that due to the Doppler effect, the thrust of all three com
abstract: Light sail technology is one of the most promising methods for interstellar travel, having passed the stage of orbital testing. This work, for the first time and based on radiation dynamics, systematically investigates the influence of incident, mirror-reflected, and diffusely scattered light on a relativistic sail (moving at near-light speed). It is established that due to the Doppler effect, the thrust of all three components decreases with increasing speed: the strongest is from incident light, weaker from mirror reflection, and weakest from diffuse scattering. A critical speed exists for the thrust from scattered light: exceeding it turns this component into a drag force, although the total radiation force continues to accelerate the craft. Equations of motion were constructed and numerically solved; it is shown that the main speed increase is concentrated in the initial phase of acceleration, and as speed grows, efficiency drops. The study provides a rigorous theoretical foundation for light sail dynamics.
threads: Разогнавшись до 0.75c, парус вдруг чувствует встречный ветер из собственного света — как сёрфер, которого его же брызги начинают тормозить. Будущее межзвёздных гонок будет балансировать на этой грани, где фотоны перестают быть попутчиками. Чтобы добраться до иных миров, придётся научиться танцевать с квантами так, чтобы не споткнуться о порог трёх четвертей световой.

title: Diamond Detective: Magnetic Dance Reveals the Secrets of Liquids
oneliner: A diamond chip with magnetic particles on DNA strands instantly recognizes any liquid by their movement.
description: A diamond plate with magnetic particles on DNA strands analyzes liquid by the trembling of particles. Their movement changes with thickness and composition: in syrup—slow, in alcohol—fast. The diamond reads the magnetic echo and creates a precise portrait of the liquid—no reagents, no waiting.
abstract: A concept for noninvasive multiparameter liquid analysis is presented, using NV-center magnetometry and relaxometry in diamond with magnetic nanoparticles anchored via DNA linkers. Thermal motion of the particles modulates the local magnetic field, detected optically through resonance shifts or changes in spin coherence time. Spatial patterning of the surface with DNA of different lengths, sequences, and chemical modifications turns a liquid droplet into a multidimensional quantum-response vector, where viscosity, molecular adsorption, and chemical interactions selectively influence the signal. The physical transduction mechanism, scaling relationships, and experimental feasibility with widefield NV magnetometry are discussed. This platform merges quantum sensing with surface heterogeneity, enabling parallel label-free analysis of complex liquid media.
threads: Крошечные магниты на алмазной струне танцуют под музыку жидкости — их движения выдают все секреты состава. Скоро так будут проверять качество воды и лекарств.

title: Weak Attraction: How Gravity Spares Quantum Superpositions
oneliner: Gravity destroys quantum superpositions, but so slowly that its influence is unnoticeable under normal conditions.
description: Scientists have rigorously described how gravity dampens quantum 'miracles'. The result: in terrestrial labs, superpositions die from molecular impacts, while the gravitational whisper remains inaudible. It grows only like a faint echo — each additional kilometer adds as much as the first meter.
abstract: Using the closed-time-path integral method, an influence functional was obtained describing gravitationally induced decoherence of a massive system in a spatial superposition, interacting with an ambient scalar field and linearized gravity. In the non-relativistic quasi-static limit, the decoherence exponent is expressed through a bilinear form of the difference of the system's stress-energy tensors and an effective noise kernel containing graviton propagators. For Newtonian long-range interaction and a dilute gas of finite wave packets with spacetime coarse-graining, analytical expressions were derived. It is shown that the dominant contribution accumulates logarithmically over a wide range of distances, remaining under realistic conditions small compared to decoherence from collisions.
threads: Гравитация, оказывается, самый ленивый разрушитель квантовых чудес. Когда частица находится в двух местах сразу, земное притяжение вмешивается в миллионы раз слабее, чем случайные столкновения с молекулами воздуха. Этот тихий шепот пространства-времени однажды станет главным шумом в сверхточных квантовых детекторах.

title: The Shape of the Universe: Measuring Spatial Curvature with Gravitational Waves
oneliner: Mergers of massive black holes can tell us whether our Universe is flat.
description: Gravitational waves from the mergers of compact objects like black holes act as 'standard sirens'—they can measure cosmological distances without calibration. If an electromagnetic counterpart is detected, the event becomes a 'bright siren', providing independent distances and redshifts. New work sh
threads: Каждое слияние — удар в исполинский космический гонг. По затуханию и обертонам гравитационных волн мы определяем натяжение ткани пространства. Детекторы Cosmic Explorer и Einstein Telescope измерят кривизну с точностью до 0.029 — и если она не ноль, стандартная модель треснет. Мы учимся слышать форму мироздания.

title: Accreting white dwarf: periodic radio pulses in a binary system
oneliner: Astronomers discovered a binary system with a white dwarf that emits strictly periodic radio bursts and X-ray emission, confirming the link between cataclysmic variables and mysterious long-period radio transients.
description: The recently discovered object ASKAP J1745−5051 turned out to be an accreting magnetic white dwarf in a close binary system with an orbital period of just 1.37 hours. Using radio interferometers ASKAP, ATCA, and MeerKAT, as well as optical and X-ray telescopes, scientists recorded periodic radio pul
abstract: Long-period radio transients (LPTs) are coherent bursts of polarized emission with periods from minutes to hours. Their origin remained unclear: slowly rotating magnetars or binary systems with white dwarfs were candidates. This work reports the discovery and classification of LPT ASKAP J174508.9-505149 as an accreting white dwarf binary. The object has an orbital period of ~1.3 hours, displays orbitally modulated X-ray emission, and radio bursts with elliptical polarization. The burst frequency drifts, possibly due to a longer beat period, and disappears for several hours. It is spectroscopically confirmed as a cataclysmic variable via optical emission lines and an ongoing X-ray outburst. The results point to a connection between at least some LPTs and white dwarf binaries.
threads: Белый карлик размером с Землю и красный карлик кружат в смертельном вальсе, порождая радиоимпульсы, в сто раз горячее солнечного ядра. Словно звездный оркестр играет на струнах магнитного поля. Такие системы могут оказаться ключом к природе самых загадочных радиотранзиентов и даже предвестниками сверхновых.

title: Levitating Graphite Catches Lunar Embraces: A New Miniature Gravimeter
oneliner: Scientists have created an ultrasensitive gravimeter the size of a teacup, capable of detecting the tidal forces of the Moon and Sun.
description: Researchers at the University of Southampton have demonstrated a levitated optomechanical sensor (LOMS) that measures Earth tides with a sensitivity of 18 microgal in 6 seconds. The device, with a volume of just 50 ml, uses diamagnetic levitation of graphite above permanent magnets and optical inter
abstract: Gravity mapping is a cornerstone geophysical technique for studying mass distribution and transfer. Natural processes and human activities (mining, ice melt) alter the gravity field, changes measured by gravimeters. Here, a levitating mechanical sensor (LOMS) is demonstrated, delivering a resolved sensitivity of 18 microgals, a wide dynamic range, and a 6-second integration time; the projected sensitivity is below 200 nanogals per root hertz in a package of only a few cubic centimeters. The device records Earth tidal signals, showcasing stability on a par with the best existing systems. Unlike conventional accelerometers, which suffer from limited stability, this unit functions as a true gravimeter. Its tiny footprint and low anticipated cost enable scalable deployment, including drone-borne aerogravity surveys (at 10–100 m altitudes), distributed networks, and multi-pixel gravity matrices. The platform paves the way for high-precision, cost-effective, planetary-scale gravity monitoring.
threads: Лепесток графита парит в магнитной колыбели, улавливая приливный вальс Луны и Солнца. Мягче паутины, он танцует под лазерным лучом. Скоро рой таких сенсоров поднимется в небо на дронах, раскинет невидимую сеть вокруг вулканов и начнёт выслеживать тёмную материю, вслушиваясь в шёпот Большого взрыва.

title: The Entire Sky in Radio Lines: Hunting for Alien Signals and Dark Matter
oneliner: Scientists scanned the entire sky with a record spectral resolution of 10 Hz to search for radio signals from other civilizations or traces of dark matter particles.
description: Using the OVRO–LWA radio interferometer, a research team conducted an unprecedented survey of the sky in the 50–86 MHz range at 10 Hz resolution. A 30-second exposure yielded over three million images of the entire hemisphere. The search for ultra-narrow lines found no candidates of extraterrestrial
abstract: A survey search for technosignatures in the decameter band (50–86 MHz) was performed using the OVRO-LWA array. Voltage data were digitized and analyzed on GPUs with a frequency resolution of ~10 Hz, yielding over 3 million all-sky images for a single 30-second epoch. Candidate selection employed multichannel matched filtering, empirical noise normalization, and false discovery rate control. After rejecting extended sources and interference, three narrowband candidates with signal-to-noise ratios above 10 sigma were examined with high temporal and frequency resolution; all proved inconsistent with compact extraterrestrial emitters. The characteristic sensitivity reached ~100 Jy per channel over the entire visible hemisphere, giving 10-sigma limits on the equivalent isotropic radiated power of about 10^14 W at 10 pc and 10^18 W at a kiloparsec. The method simultaneously probes millions of stellar systems and provides a scalable framework for deeper observations, including searches for axion-like particles near neutron stars.
threads: 30 секунд — и 3 миллиона снимков неба. Телескоп OVRO прослушал эфир, но нашёл лишь тишину. Представьте: мы ищем не симфонию, а один-единственный чистый тон среди космического шума. Этот тон может быть голосом тёмной материи, превращающейся в радиоволну у нейтронной звезды. Когда-нибудь такой сигнал перевернёт физику. Пока — тишина. Но мы только начинаем слушать.

title: One-Way Light: Axion Gatekeepers in Magnetic Crystals
oneliner: Ghostly axions in crystals create a one-way door for light—a breakthrough for quantum technology.
description: Axion excitations emerge in magnetic materials—collective magnetization oscillations that act like particles. Coupled with light, they form a hybrid that, under electric and magnetic fields, becomes a one-way valve for photons. Light flows freely in one direction but is completely blocked in the rev
abstract: The dynamic axion is a massive quasiparticle arising from fluctuations in magnetic order. Interacting with the electromagnetic field, it forms axion-polaritons (effectively magnon-polaritons). Under static electric and magnetic fields, the anisotropic coupling of the axion to the photon leads to nonreciprocal dispersion ω(k) ≠ ω(-k). A regime of ideal nonreciprocity is revealed: a photon traveling in one direction is completely detached from the axion, whereas the counter-propagating one hybridizes strongly. This dispersion acts as an optical isolator. The findings position nonreciprocal axion-polaritons as an effective tool for probing axion quasiparticles and point to experimentally accessible methods for their detection.

title: Polar Interstellar Meteor: A New Visitor from the Depths of the Galaxy
oneliner: NASA satellite data on a fireball over the South Atlantic strongly point to its interstellar origin.
description: Analysis of the fireball detected on April 1, 2026, over the South Atlantic showed its Sun-relative speed was 51.73 km/s — well above the local escape velocity. Accounting for Earth's gravitational acceleration, coordinate transforms, and Monte Carlo statistical modeling rules out a bound orbit with
abstract: A new interstellar meteor candidate, polarIM, has been registered. It entered the atmosphere on April 1, 2026, at 41.9° S, 54.7° W, at an altitude of 90.5 km. After transforming the velocity vector from the Earth-based system to the heliocentric frame and accounting for Earth's gravity, the heliocentric speed was determined to be 51.73 km/s, with the polar component (+47.09 km/s) itself exceeding the local escape velocity of 42.14 km/s. The specific orbital energy is positive at +450.1 km²/s², and the excess speed at infinity is 30.00 km/s. Monte Carlo modeling (1 million realizations) using the CNEOS error model (σ_v=0.55 km/s, σ_RA=1.35°, σ_Dec=0.84°) showed that no realization yielded a closed orbit, and the statistical confidence of interstellar origin is >99.9997%. The average velocity margin over escape is 9.60±0.75 km/s (12.8σ), making polarIM the most reliable candidate in the CNEOS catalog since 2018.
threads: Болид над Южной Атлантикой летел на 9,6 км/с быстрее, чем нужно для побега от Солнца. Это не камень из нашей системы — это бутылочная почта из-за пределов галактического архипелага. Даже одна вертикальная компонента скорости (+47 км/с) могла бы унести его в межзвёздную тьму. Такие гости приносят нам частицы чужих миров — без звездолётов. Мы на пороге «Свидания с Рамой»: тайны иных систем раскрываются при прямом контакте.

title: The Gaia Sausage Enigma: The Milky Way Survived Not One, But Multiple Major Mergers
oneliner: The Gaia Sausage/Enceladus structure, long thought to be the remnant of a single major merger, is actually composed of several stellar streams with distinct histories.
description: Using data from the DESI spectroscopic survey and a new clustering algorithm called GS3 Hunter, astronomers have discovered that the famous Gaia Sausage/Enceladus (GSE) structure in the inner halo of the Milky Way is not a homogeneous remnant of a single merger. Instead, four distinct substructures
abstract: The Gaia-Sausage-Enceladus (GSE) structure has traditionally been considered the remnant of the last major merger that shaped the inner halo of the Milky Way. However, analysis of DESI survey data using a new unsupervised clustering algorithm, GS³ Hunter, identified 17 structures in the local halo, including GSE and previously unknown streams. A detailed investigation of chronological, dynamical, and chemical properties revealed four distinct subgroups within the GSE region: GSE−GSH1 (age 12 Gyr), GSE−GSH2 (10 Gyr), GSE−GSH3 (8 Gyr), and GSE−GSH4 (7 Gyr). Despite overall similarities in phase-space distribution and elemental abundances, these subgroups exhibit notably different orbital actions and chemical compositions. These findings point to a complex formation history for GSE, which is not the product of a single event but a composite structure assembled through several successive mergers in the early evolution of the Galaxy.
threads: Галактическое гало — не монолит, а палимпсест из четырёх слоёв. Химический почерк сверхновых выдает историю каждого. Скоро телескопы превратят эти чернила в летопись, а распределение тёмной материи станет явным. Археология галактик начинается.

title: Late influx of material from a molecular cloud reshaped the outer Solar System
oneliner: Isotopic analysis of meteorites showed that gas giants accreted more than 30% comet-like material, and parent bodies of Ryugu-type asteroids formed near the water ice line.
description: Carbonaceous chondrites are the building blocks of the outer Solar System. Researchers from the University of Copenhagen analyzed the isotopic composition of silicon, iron, magnesium, and chromium in anomalous chondrites and found that their nucleosynthetic signatures reflect a mixture of two dust p
abstract: To understand the evolution of the outer protoplanetary disk and the delivery of volatiles (water, organics) to the inner region where terrestrial planets formed, scientists analyzed nucleosynthetic isotope systems of Si, Mg, Fe, and Cr in anomalous chondrites—unclassified analogs of carbonaceous chondrites. The results showed that the outer disk was replenished with isotopically distinct material from a molecular cloud, which constituted over 30% of the building block mass in the gas giant accretion zone. This late addition did not participate in the main accretion of terrestrial planets; instead, their volatile inventory was fully supplied by Ivuna-type planetesimals, such as Ryugu and Bennu. In the proposed model, these bodies formed near an inward-migrating snow line. The accretion of such icy planetesimals in the inner disk represents a fundamental shift in understanding Solar System evolution.
threads: Астероиды Рюгу и Бенну — не посланцы из ледяных бездн. Их изотопный почерк говорит: они сформировались у «снежной линии», где вода превращается в лёд. А потом в диск влилось кометное вещество — как специя, навсегда изменившая вкус блюда. Это переписывает историю доставки воды на Землю: возможно, она пришла не из далёкого холода, а из локального буфета. Образцы с Hayabusa2 и OSIRIS-REx раскроют детали.

title: Radio Barcode for the Quantum World
oneliner: Reflecting off a nanowire, a radio wave—like a barcode scanner beam—instantly reads its state, offering hope for stable quantum processors.
abstract: Radio-frequency reflectometry was applied to PbTe-Pb nanowire devices grown via selective epitaxy on a CdTe substrate. These nanowires are predicted to support Majorana zero modes. Compatibility of the RF method, including resistive and capacitive sensing, with such structures was demonstrated. Dielectric losses in the CdTe substrate were quantitatively assessed. The technique's viability under finite magnetic fields, required for zero-energy states, was confirmed. These results enable fast manipulation of PbTe quantum devices, paving the way for their integration into topological quantum computing.

title: Black Holes with Memory: Forgetfulness as the Engine of Dark Matter
oneliner: Tiny black holes might be dark matter, if their ability to forget their past slows down their evaporation in just the right way.
description: Primordial black holes, born alongside the universe, should have vanished long ago. But new research shows they're saved by their own 'memory', which slows down evaporation. The key question is exactly how it kicks in. That determines whether these holes make up dark matter.
abstract: For light primordial black holes (PBHs) with initial masses of 10⁵–10¹⁰ g, we studied how the memory burden effect impacts nucleosynthesis bounds. Instead of an instantaneous transition, we used a smooth crossover between semiclassical and suppressed Hawking radiation, described by a smoothed tanh profile with parameters (q, δ). Two speed-combining rules — additive and multiplicative — are implemented in the modified AlterBBN code. Monochromatic PBHs are sourced by a decaying scalar field. The additive combination always yields weaker bounds than the multiplicative one, but both are tighter than the instantaneous transition case. For these masses, the additive option allows a PBH fraction in dark matter of ~10⁻¹, the multiplicative one only ~10⁻². Thus, the details of the transition rule are crucial for obtaining BBN constraints on PBH models.

title: What's Inside 'Little Red Dots': The Anatomy of Galactic Cores in the Early Universe
oneliner: Spectral analysis of distant 'dot' galaxies with the James Webb Space Telescope reveals a dense gas torus fueling a supermassive black hole at their centers.
description: Scientists studied 14 'Little Red Dots' (LRDs)—mysterious compact objects at high redshifts discovered by JWST. Using medium-resolution spectroscopy, they broke down the Balmer hydrogen lines into components, measured their ratios, and found signs of extremely dense gas (n_H > 10⁹ cm⁻³). It turns ou
threads: Ранняя Вселенная полна «красных точек» — это не пыльные галактики, а «голые» чёрные дыры в водородном тумане. Их свет краснеет, словно закатное солнце в дымке. Плотность газа там такая, что обычные законы не работают — линии водорода «захлёбываются» от тесноты. Возможно, именно эти объекты помогут понять, как сверхмассивные чёрные дыры стали гигантами ещё до появления звёзд.

title: Dark energy may be losing energy: explaining strange behavior
oneliner: A subtle friction in dark energy explains why it changes — hinted at by the DESI telescope.
description: Recent observations suggest that dark energy, the force accelerating the universe’s expansion, is not constant. Around 6 billion years ago, it crossed a critical threshold, making its behavior even more puzzling. Scientists came up with a simple idea: the dark energy field experiences a tiny amount
abstract: New data from the DESI survey point to an evolving dark energy that appears to have crossed the phantom divide in the recent past. This work presents a dissipative scenario where quintessence (a scalar field for dark energy) undergoes weak dissipation, naturally accounting for both evolution and phantom divide crossing without introducing pathological phantom-like dynamics. It's shown that even minimal dissipation can reproduce current DESI observational data, offering a physically motivated alternative to exotic dark energy models.
threads: Тёмная энергия, заставляющая Вселенную расширяться всё быстрее, возможно, не постоянна. Новые данные телескопа DESI намекают, что около 6 млрд лет назад она изменила своё поведение, пересекла фантомную границу. Простая модель с едва заметным трением объясняет эту перемену без экзотических выдумок — как если бы маятник постепенно замедлялся.

title: Light Falls into a Trap of Chaos
oneliner: For the first time, scientists have reliably trapped waves in three-dimensional chaos.
description: An experiment with metallic particles proved that light can be stopped in three dimensions by chaos alone. By tweaking the concentration, they saw a sharp switch from free roaming to total lockdown. The finding could spark a breakthrough in controlling radiation.
abstract: Anderson localization—the suppression of wave propagation in disordered media due to interference effects—has long been known for various wave types, but for light in three-dimensional systems, its unambiguous observation remained impossible due to experimental artifacts. This work presents the first unequivocal experimental proof of three-dimensional Anderson localization of microwaves in disordered metallic aggregates. Studying samples with different metal volume fractions allowed a clear distinction between diffusive and localized regimes. Localization was confirmed by a scaling analysis of the transmitted beam width, with results in excellent agreement with theoretical and numerical calculations. This demonstration paves the way for fundamental research and practical applications of this extraordinary phenomenon.

title: Balance on the Edge: When a Star Can't Keep Up the Dance with a Black Hole
oneliner: New hydrodynamic simulations reveal a subtle mechanism that determines whether the eccentric mass exchange between a sun-like star and a black hole spins into uncontrolled destruction or remains stable.
description: In tight binary systems, where a star orbits a black hole along an elliptical path, mass loss can either stabilize the system or trigger a runaway disruption. Scientists ran multi-period SPH simulations and found that the system's fate hinges on the ratio of the initial pericenter distance to the ec
abstract: Binary systems can exchange mass while maintaining significant orbital eccentricity. Stellar-mass black holes can strip material from stars on eccentric orbits, causing micro tidal disruption events (micro-TDEs). Previous hydrodynamic studies focused on nearly circular systems, leaving the transition from self-regulated eccentric mass transfer to runaway disruption poorly understood. This work presents smoothed particle hydrodynamics (SPH) simulations of a Sun-like star interacting with a 10-solar-mass black hole at initial eccentricities of 0.30–0.70 and pericenter distances of 3.33–3.57 tidal radii; evolution was tracked over tens to over a hundred orbital periods. Two scenarios emerge: at b0 ≲ 3.45, mass loss at pericenter triggers adiabatic envelope expansion and runaway disruption, and the resulting accretion stream with a super-Eddington feeding rate can produce fast transients in X-rays/UV or optical; at b0 ≳ 3.57, the system enters a long-lived phase of stable mass transfer (up to 150 orbits), where the orbit expands due to mass loss.
threads: Всего несколько процентов расстояния решают, быть ли вечному вальсу или мгновенной вспышке. Моделирование показывает: за первые десятки витков становится ясно, устоит ли звезда. Лавина разрушения набирает силу от каждого сброшенного слоя — парадоксально расширяясь, звезда обрекает себя. Такие катаклизмы — ключ к расшифровке ярчайших космических взрывов.

title: Nuclear Clock: The Ticking Nucleus in the Search for Dark Matter
oneliner: The first nuclear clock is ticking: instead of electrons, the thorium nucleus does the ticking. This opens the door to hunting dark matter.
description: Physicists have built a clock where the pendulum is an atomic nucleus. Its ticking is almost immune to disturbances. It's accurate to 10⁻¹⁵ seconds per day, setting new limits on dark matter particles.
abstract: The nuclear transition in thorium-229 is a promising basis for optical nuclear clocks, offering superior stability to traditional atomic clocks. This work presents the first realization of such a clock: a continuous-wave laser at 148 nm is stabilized to the nuclear transition via fast feedback from absorption spectroscopy. The thorium-229 nuclei are doped into a millimeter-sized calcium fluoride crystal at room temperature. A subharmonic of the 148 nm light is continuously compared with a single Yb+ ion clock. A frequency instability of 3×10^{-12} √(τ/s) is achieved, averaging down to 10^{-15} after a day of continuous operation. The clock was used to constrain models of ultralight dark matter: searches for periodic fluctuations and slow drifts of the transition energy on timescales from 20 s to a day yielded bounds comparable to the best atomic clocks for dark matter coupling to photons, and surpassing previous ones for coupling to the strong interaction and quarks.
threads: В кристалле фторида кальция, размером с песчинку, спрятаны часы, которые тикают 2000 триллионов раз в секунду. Их «маятник» — ядро тория-229 — не боится ни тряски, ни тепла. Такие часы могут учуять дуновение тёмной материи.

title: Impact Rain 800 Million Years Ago: How the Breakup of Asteroid Eulalia Changed the Planets
oneliner: The breakup of asteroid Eulalia's parent body about 800 million years ago triggered a surge of bombardment that affected Earth, the Moon, Mars, and possibly the biosphere.
description: Modeling of the collisional and dynamical evolution of the Eulalia asteroid family reveals that its breakup led to a massive impact rain on the terrestrial planets. The method combines lunar crater chronology, dating of impact glasses and meteorites, and numerical simulations of debris migration. Th
abstract: The spike in large lunar impacts ~800 million years ago is linked to the breakup of a carbonaceous chondrite parent body of the Eulalia asteroid family ~800 million years ago near the 3:1 resonance with Jupiter. Collision and dynamical evolution modeling shows that ~75% of the family’s fragments slipped into the J3:1 resonance within ~150 million years: some right after the breakup, others gradually drifting via the Yarkovsky effect. Once in resonance, the fragments were pumped onto planet-crossing orbits, drastically increasing the hit rate on the Moon and terrestrial planets. This accounts for the birth of big craters like Copernicus. The event likely had global repercussions: on Earth, it aligns with biospheric disruptions possibly triggered by impacts, and on Mars, with a pulse of volcanic activity. So, collisions in the Main Belt can shape the saga of the entire inner solar system.
threads: 800 млн лет назад в поясе астероидов разбилась гигантская чернильница. Тёмные углистые брызги залили Землю, Луну и Марс, выбив кратеры-шрамы. Ударный дождь перетряхнул углеродный цикл и, возможно, изменил ход биосферы. Сегодня мы охотимся за этими космическими чернилами, чтобы прочесть летопись катастроф и подготовиться к новым грозам.

title: Dark mountains of neutron stars: gravitational waves probe dark matter
oneliner: Continuous gravitational waves from neutron stars with self-interacting dark matter allow the first constraints on unknown parameters of the dark sector.
description: Researchers combined a two-fluid model of neutron stars with fermionic dark matter and LIGO's continuous gravitational-wave search results. Anisotropic dark matter distributions can create 'dark mountains'—quadrupole deformations that amplify radiation. Comparing with LIGO's third observing run (O3)
threads: Внутри нейтронной звезды таится невидимая гора из тёмной материи — крошечный бугорок, но массой с Гималаи. Она превращает звезду в космический камертон: гравитационные волны разносят её звон по Вселенной. LIGO пока молчит, но это молчание уже очертило границы неведомого. Грядущие обсерватории смогут уловить эту музыку и, возможно, впервые коснуться тёмного сектора.

title: New Distances to NGC 1052 Galaxies: Testing the Bullet Dwarf Hypothesis
oneliner: Hubble and Webb measurements show that the mysterious line of dwarf galaxies without dark matter is not a projection illusion, but its exact structure still awaits an explanation.
description: A chain of ultra-diffuse dwarf galaxies lacking dark matter has been discovered in the field of NGC 1052. The “bullet dwarf” scenario explains their common origin via a high-speed collision. However, critics suggested the chain is an optical illusion caused by the superposition of two galaxy groups
threads: Словно с жемчужного ожерелья сорвали нить — галактики без тёмной материи застыли цепью. В группе NGC 1052 два газовых облака столкнулись лоб в лоб: тёмный каркас пролетел насквозь, а звёздные бусины остались. «Уэбб» случайно запечатлел эту сцену, охотясь на убегающую чёрную дыру. Теперь астрономы видят в этих сиротах ключ к разгадке тёмной материи.

title: Dark matter free-streaming length measured using 28 JWST gravitational lenses
oneliner: Observations of 28 gravitationally lensed quasars by JWST have constrained the free-streaming length of dark matter particles and confirmed the predictions of cold dark matter.
description: Using JWST data for 28 quadruply lensed quasar systems, astronomers measured a key parameter of dark matter models—the free-streaming length, which depends on the mass and formation mechanism of its particles. Joint analysis of image positions, relative brightnesses, and extended arcs suppressed unc
threads: Чтобы взвесить невидимое, астрономы превратили 28 далёких квазаров в космические микроскопы. Каждая расщеплённая дуга — слепок с тысяч тёмных сгустков. Вердикт: тёмная материя холодна, тяжела и чертит Вселенную без дрожи. Ни одной помарки. Но следующий шаг — проверить, не дрогнет ли рука, когда мы включим увеличение на полную.

title: Magnetic Pumping: How Plasma Pulsations Accelerate Particles to Ultra-Relativistic Energies
oneliner: Numerical simulations show that compression-expansion cycles in magnetized plasma can efficiently generate power-law energy spectra of particles, consistent with the generalized maximum entropy principle.
description: Astrophysical plasma with a high beta parameter often exhibits non-thermal particle distributions, the origin of which is debated. In a new study, for the first time, fully kinetic simulation was used to investigate particle acceleration in plasma undergoing cyclic compression and expansion. It is s
abstract: This work proposes a new 'pulsating box' configuration to study particle acceleration in high-beta plasma undergoing compression-expansion cycles. Fully kinetic simulations demonstrate efficient particle acceleration via magnetic pumping, forming non-thermal energy distributions with power-law tails. A generalized maximum entropy model is derived, linking the power-law index of the distribution to the injected energy; numerical results are in excellent agreement with the model. The results are significant for explaining the origin of high-energy particles in cosmic and astrophysical plasma — from turbulent regions of the interstellar medium to relativistic outflows.
threads: Магнитная накачка превращает пульсации плазмы в природный ускоритель. Каждый цикл сжатия, как удар кузнечного меха, вбивает в частицы порцию энергии. За 5–10 циклов электроны разгоняются до скоростей, близких к световой. Этот механизм объясняет потоки частиц от магнитосферы Земли до скоплений галактик. Похоже, Вселенная использует простой ритм, чтобы создавать самые быстрые объекты в природе.

title: Heavy Paleodetectors for Hunting the Higgsino: How Ancient Minerals Reveal the Secrets of Dark Matter
oneliner: Using ancient lead-based minerals allows detecting elusive inelastic dark matter particles, including the higgsino.
description: Dark matter remains one of the greatest mysteries of modern physics. A new study proposes a revolutionary method for direct detection of dark matter particles — paleodetectors based on heavy elements. Scientists have shown that ancient minerals, such as laurionite, containing lead, can accumulate tr
abstract: Heavy-element paleo-detectors are proposed for the direct detection of inelastic dark matter, using the higgsino as a benchmark. Existing paleo-detector methods lacked sufficiently heavy nuclei to overcome the kinematic scattering threshold. Suitable minerals may be sourced from ancient low-background sediments (e.g., lead-rich) from deep geothermal brines. The method shows enhanced sensitivity to the high-velocity tail of the dark matter distribution, particularly to a possible fast-particle stream triggered by a close passage of the Large Magellanic Cloud ~50 Myr ago, making younger samples preferable. Accessible higgsino mass splittings range up to δ≈920 keV. Due to the large higgsino-nucleon scattering cross-section, even samples with typical background levels from depths of only 2 km can probe new parameter space, partially relaxing the strict requirements on radio-purity and depth that are critical for the paleo-detector program.
threads: Кристаллы лаурионита — это природные свинцовые пластины, десятилетиями экспонировавшиеся потоком тёмной материи. Ионный микроскоп теперь проявляет скрытое изображение. Впервые можно поймать хиггсино — кандидата, ускользавшего от земных детекторов. Это космическая криминалистика: вещественными доказательствами служат целые минералы.

title: Quasar WISSH13: Ultra-Fast Winds from the Cosmic Noon Era
oneliner: X-ray observations reveal record outflows at up to 0.3c, uncovering the mechanics of super-Eddington accretion in the early Universe.
description: Deep observations of quasar WISSH13 (z=3.294) with XMM-Newton and NuSTAR revealed two components of ultra-fast outflows (UFOs) at speeds of ~0.1c and ~0.3c, detected via iron absorption lines.
