oneliner: From a barely noticeable dark line in X-ray light, astronomers identified a magnetar — a neutron star with a magnetic field 400 trillion times stronger than Earth's.
description: In galaxy NGC 3583, an object was found whose X-ray glow alternately flares up and nearly fades away. Breaking its light down by energy, astronomers spotted a dip — a single 'note.' It is created by protons caught in an invisible magnetic field. The pitch of the note revealed: the field is 400 trill
abstract: We analyzed archival data from XMM-Newton, NuSTAR, Chandra, and long-term Swift/XRT monitoring of the transient hyperluminous X-ray source 2SXPS J111416.1+481833 in the galaxy NGC 3583. The source sporadically reaches luminosities above 10^41 erg/s and drops to a deep low state (attenuation factor >45). The broadband spectrum reveals a break at 5–6 keV, modeled as a combination of a soft thermal component and Compton scattering in an optically thick medium or an advection-dominated disk. XMM-Newton spectra show a statistically significant (>3.9σ) absorption line at 1.97±0.04 keV with a width σ≈74±40 eV, which is interpreted as a candidate proton cyclotron resonance scattering feature (CRSF). This points to a local magnetic field strength B~4×10^14 G. A search for coherent X-ray pulsations yielded no significant signal: upper limits on the pulsed fraction are 19.3% (0.3–10 keV) and 36.3% (3–15 keV) at 90% confidence. The combination of extreme luminosity, hard spectrum, and probable cyclotron line supports a highly magnetized neutron star as the accretor.

title: Magnetism and Superconductivity: An Unexpected Alliance
oneliner: In a crystal with seven copper layers, magnetism and superconductivity don't conflict but coexist and likely reinforce each other.
description: Laser scanning revealed zones in the crystal where electrons encounter almost no resistance. Unexpectedly, superconductivity at –200°C was found to exist inside a magnetic structure — conditions previously thought incompatible with the formation of electron pairs.
abstract: Using spatially resolved laser-based angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES), a seven-layer cuprate Bi₂Sr₂Ca₆Cu₇O₁₈₊δ (Bi2267) was investigated. A system of multiple hole Fermi pockets was discovered, with energy gaps showing pronounced dependence on momentum, temperature, and Fermi surface sheet. High-temperature superconductivity with a critical temperature of ~75 K emerges in a system with multiple Fermi pockets and coexisting strong antiferromagnetic order and correlations. Particularly noteworthy is the significant electron pairing observed along a Fermi pocket with a gap of up to ~42 meV in weakly doped CuO₂ planes (hole doping p~0.05). These data challenge traditional views on the role of nodal and antinodal electronic states in the emergence of high-temperature superconductivity. It is shown that superconductivity and antiferromagnetism can coexist in a cuprate with several Fermi pockets, providing new clues to understanding the pairing mechanism in cuprate superconductors.

title: Proteins: From Still Pictures to a Living Dance
oneliner: Proteins are not statues but dancers: scientists now see their movements in real time to correct deadly mistakes.
description: A protein is a chain that folds into a 3D shape. Now we see not just the final pose but the folding dance itself. Mistakes in this dance cause diseases. Understanding it will lead to new medicines and the creation of artificial proteins.
abstract: Deep learning (AlphaFold) has enabled prediction of three-dimensional protein structures from amino acid sequences, yet quantitative understanding of dynamic conformational changes and supramolecular assembly remains an unsolved challenge. Folding is a dynamic stochastic process shaped by sequence, energy landscape, cotranslational constraints, chaperones, and cellular environment conditions. Single-molecule methods provide temporal resolution to observe folding trajectories and intermediate states hidden from traditional approaches. Computational innovations and data-driven methods integrate heterogeneous data across scales. The review’s roadmap charts a path toward a quantitative science of protein folding dynamics, conformational kinetics, and macromolecular self-assembly. This will transform understanding of molecular self-organization, from individual polypeptides to dynamic complexes, enable rational control of folding and misfolding, extend protein engineering beyond static design, and lay the foundation for personalized therapies for proteostasis disorders.

title: The Helium Decipherer: How to Read the Sun’s Invisible Past
oneliner: A neural network decipherer has learned to turn old helium-light images of the Sun into detailed pictures of its invisible corona.
description: Scientists have built a neural network that translates ground-based observations of the Sun in helium light into images of the hot corona, which are only visible from space. This makes it possible to reconstruct the corona’s appearance decades before ultraviolet telescopes were launched. The model a
abstract: To recover the extreme ultraviolet context of the corona in eras before direct observations, a conditional diffusion model, CH-aware DMT, has been proposed. It converts full-disk images in the helium line (He I, 1083 nm), where absorption indicates open magnetic fields, into synthetic SDO/AIA 193 Å images. The model was trained on paired SOLIS He I and AIA 193 Å data from 2011–2015, split by month: January–October for training, November for validation, December for testing. On the test set, it achieves a correlation of 0.92 for large-scale morphology and 0.84 for coronal hole structures. Historical applicability is confirmed by comparison with SOHO/EIT 195 Å (2005–2015), reconstruction from KPVT He I, and comparison with Yohkoh/SXT, as well as analysis of long-term (1974–2015) full-disk flux time series with sunspot numbers and 10.7 cm radio flux. The method enables multi-decadal studies of large-scale coronal evolution, previously impossible due to the lack of direct EUV images.

title: How Entropy Predicts Alloy Structures
oneliner: Information entropy works as an order detector: it lets scientists figure out which crystal structure a multi-metal alloy will adopt, even before it’s made.
description: When mixing many metals, atoms can arrange themselves in dozens of ways, making the final lattice almost impossible to predict. Scientists used information entropy as a measure of explored options: a neural network quickly sifts through millions of combinations, and entropy tells them when to stop s
abstract: The task of predicting the phase composition of chemically disordered alloys, including high-entropy ones, is complicated by the combinatorial explosion of possible configurations. The paper proposes an information-theoretic approach: using alchemical Monte Carlo sampling and an efficient graph convolutional neural network, the potential energy landscape is explored. The bond disproportionation vector (BDV) descriptor is assessed as a low-cost alternative and compared with the state-of-the-art SOAP descriptor. An information-entropy-based metric is applied to binary (CoNi, MoW, FeNi, TaW), ternary (CoCrNi, CrFeNi), quaternary (CoCrFeNi), and quinary (Al_x(CoCrFeNi)_{1-x}) alloys. The entropy measure allows quantitative evaluation of the completeness of configurational space exploration and successfully predicts phases in cases inaccessible to traditional methods.

title: Ultracold Condensate Turns Weak Molecular Asymmetry into Complete Handedness
oneliner: Turning a whisper into a chorus: physicists propose using a Bose condensate to amplify the energy difference between left-handed and right-handed molecules to near-complete dominance.
description: Physicists have found a way to amplify the tiny energy difference between left-handed and right-handed molecules. By placing them in a Bose condensate—a state where atoms act as a single quantum chorus—the minuscule asymmetry becomes an almost total predominance of one form.
abstract: A mechanism is proposed for indirect detection of the small energy difference between chiral enantiomers caused by parity violation (PVED). Chiral molecules form resonantly during ultracold collisions of achiral diatomic particles; PVED introduces a slight asymmetry in the resonance energies of right- and left-handed configurations. In a Bose-Einstein condensate, the system's evolution involving nonlinear interactions, tunneling, PVED, and thermal transitions amplifies the microscopic imbalance into a global population skew. Using coupled rate equations, it is shown that in realistic regimes, full enantiomeric excess is achievable even for extremely small PVED. The model is illustrated with examples HSOH, H₂Se₂, H₂Te₂, for which observable magnitudes are predicted. Amplification of non-PVED effects (electric and magnetic fields, thermal fluctuations) is considered. The results demonstrate that ultracold physics may become a new tool for probing molecular parity violation—a fundamental weak effect still undetected experimentally.

title: Gravitational Waves: New Method More Accurately Separates Signal from Noise
oneliner: A new method finds the tiniest glitches in gravitational telescope data, like scratches on a vinyl record.
description: Gravitational telescopes capture cosmic ripples, but their recordings are contaminated by terrestrial noise—like scratches on a vinyl record. The new method analyzes data in microscopic fragments, comparing each to a reference noise pattern. This allows even the faintest glitches to be discarded, pr
abstract: A frame-by-frame glitch detection method has been developed, overcoming the suppression of weak signals caused by global averaging in the CLS token of DINOv2 (ViT-S/14), which averages 37×37 patches and suppresses objects covering less than 5% of the spectrogram. Instead of global similarity, top-k statistics are applied to the similarity of individual patches against a vector-quantized index (1216 centroids matching 19 glitch types from Gravity Spy O3b). On injections in LIGO O4a L1, a clear separation (KS=0.963 at k=68) is achieved for extended SpiralBurst morphology, while for ultra-short AsymBlip glitches, the temporal resolution limit is confirmed. A topological significance map, built from patch-to-background similarity, correctly localizes signatures for Scattered_Light and injected SpiralBurst. The max-to-mean ratio suggests that saliency functions as a topological visualizer, aligning with the non-isotropic geometry of DINOv2 embeddings on spectrograms.

title: How AI Is Making Solar Cells Last Longer
oneliner: AI diagnoses the ailments of perovskite solar cells and discovers unexpected ways to make them durable.
description: Perovskites are cheap and efficient solar energy collectors. But humidity and heat kill them within days. Scientists have trained AI to analyze data from hundreds of experiments and identify the causes of degradation. Algorithms predict which additives will turn the fragile crystal into a sturdy mat
abstract: Three-dimensional all-inorganic halide perovskites CsPbX₃ (X = Cl, Br, I) are promising for photovoltaics, photodetectors, and light-emitting devices, yet their practical use is limited by instability under thermal, chemical, optical, and electrical stress. Traditional research faces challenges of multimodal data, interconnected degradation pathways, protocol dependence, and uncertainty quantification. This review systematizes AI advances in analyzing CsPbX₃ instability across four directions: stability diagnostics, microscopic mechanism analysis, consequence and reliability modeling, and engineering resilience enhancement. Limitations of current methods are discussed, including data quality, protocol consistency, interpretability, and cross-domain model transfer. Future paths are outlined: data standardization, interpretable cross-scale models, and integration of AI with automated experiments and physics-informed modeling.

title: Paimon: AI Chef in the World of Atoms
oneliner: A smart platform that, like a chef, automates computational experiments and catches errors.
description: Computer modeling of atoms is the key to better batteries and drugs. But setting up calculations and checking results take scientists more time than searching for ideas. Paimon acts like a skilled chef: it independently runs virtual experiments and tastes the result, catching plausible but physicall
abstract: Traditional atomistic modeling was held back by the computational cost of ab initio methods and the parametrization of empirical force fields. Universal ML potentials brought quantum accuracy and transferability, but shifted the bottleneck to tedious data prep and analysis. The Paimon platform automates these steps through agentic integration. In hundreds of liquid electrolyte tests, it expert-level suppressed 'silent errors' (plausible but physically incorrect results), greatly boosting reliability. Moreover, the platform can interact with an external science agent and autonomously reproduce computational recipes from the literature. As an agentic harness for atomistic simulations, Paimon enables a seamless science-driven workflow from preparation to analysis.

title: Axions reconcile two measurements of the Universe
oneliner: Rare axion collisions explain why different ways of measuring the expansion of the Universe give different numbers.
description: Scientists propose that dark matter consists of axions, extremely light particles. Occasionally they collide, influencing the pace of the Universe's expansion. This explains the famous gap: local measurements yield 73, global ones 67 (in cosmologists' conventional units). The new model reconciles th
abstract: We investigate the late-time dynamics of a Universe where dark matter is modeled by axion particles. The model differs from ΛCDM by including a small fraction of axions that drive the system out of thermodynamic equilibrium. The evolution of axion energy density is analyzed using both kinetic and classical field approaches, resulting in the same macroscopic equation. This scenario modifies the ΛCDM dynamics at z<1, while asymptotically recovering the standard behavior at earlier epochs. A statistical comparison with a suite of observational data shows that when including the local SH0ES calibration, the axion model is significantly favored over ΛCDM, yielding a best-fit Hubble constant H0≈73 km/s/Mpc. The scenario simultaneously reconciles local distance ladder measurements with the baryon acoustic oscillation data from the DESI collaboration.

title: Cracks in Space Send Gravitational Waves
oneliner: Pulsars hint at giant crack-walls in the universe. Future detectors LISA and Taiji will check if they're real.
description: As the early universe cooled, like a freezing lake, cracks appeared — domain walls. They continue to tremble, creating gravitational waves already picked up by pulsars. New calculations show: the space antennas LISA and Taiji will be able to catch the high-frequency part of this signal, even if weak
abstract: Domain walls provide a good fit to PTA data. Their gravitational-wave spectrum features an ultraviolet tail reaching millihertz frequencies, enabling cross-detection by the LISA and Taiji interferometers. We studied PTA-compatible parameters and the information gain from a joint analysis. We show that LISA and Taiji probe a significant region with the ultraviolet tail in the mHz range, even in the presence of astrophysical noise. However, the informative region within the PTA-supported band is limited to the edge with a high signal-to-noise ratio. Using space-based data alone, the posterior is highly degenerate, but the main parameter combination is accurately recovered when the signal is strong. Adding PTA information, the gain is localized there: the posterior update is substantial, the degeneracy is suppressed, and the axis ratio often remains PTA-like. The analysis was performed using 10-dimensional Bayesian inference (2 signal, 8 noise parameters) with 49 (LISA/Taiji) and 65 (joint) injections, and Clough–Tocher interpolation.

title: Pulsars Lock Onto the Hum of the Prehistoric Universe
oneliner: Scientists have detected background gravitational waves that may have been born during cosmic inflation.
description: Astronomers caught background noise in the rhythm of pulsars. This noise is likely gravitational waves from the infant universe. For the signal to match the inflation era, they had to assume that after inflation the universe was surprisingly cold — challenging theory.
abstract: The second data release from the European Pulsar Timing Array (EPTA) contains evidence for a stochastic gravitational-wave background. This signal is interpreted within inflationary scenarios. The tensor power spectrum is parameterized by the tensor-to-scalar ratio r, spectral index n_t, reheating temperature T_rh, and cutoff frequency f_end. Taking into account constraints from the cosmic microwave background, primordial nucleosynthesis, and LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA observations, joint 95% confidence regions are obtained: −11.66 ≲ log₁₀r ≲ −1.45, 1.32 ≲ n_t ≲ 2.47, 1.78 MeV ≲ T_rh ≲ 28.2 GeV, 75.86 nHz ≲ f_end ≲ 14.45 Hz. The result points to a scenario where the gravitational-wave background in the EPTA band is generated by tensor modes that entered the horizon during the radiation-dominated era; this allows for enhanced r and a bluer spectrum. However, the required low reheating temperature poses a theoretical challenge.

title: Radio Telescopes Hear Gravitational Ripples
oneliner: High-frequency gravitational waves passing through magnetic fields become radio signals — and ordinary radio telescopes can hear them.
description: Radio telescopes CHIME and FAST can detect high-frequency gravitational waves that, passing through magnetic fields, turn into radio signals. This method enables the detection of mergers of microscopic black holes and even clouds of invisible particles around black holes, paving the way to solving t
abstract: High-frequency gravitational waves (HFGWs) in the MHz–GHz range can convert into radio photons in astrophysical magnetic fields via the inverse Gertsenshtein effect. It is shown that existing radio telescopes such as CHIME and FAST are excellent tools for detecting HFGW sources, significantly outperforming many dedicated experiments in the search for primordial black hole mergers—the most realistic sources of transient HFGWs. Radio telescopes also possess unique sensitivity to monochromatic HFGW radiation, for example, from clouds of ultralight bosons formed via superradiance around primordial black holes. These results point to a high probability of detecting HFGWs with currently operating radio observatories, opening a new observational window into astrophysics.
threads: Невидимая рябь пространства, путешествуя сквозь магнитные поля, превращается в радиоволны и «звенит» в радиотелескопах. Так можно услышать слияние крошечных чёрных дыр, рождённых после Большого взрыва. Обычные антенны становятся детекторами гравитации, открывая дверь в скрытую Вселенную.

title: Gravity Entangles Light and a Rotating Object
oneliner: Scientists have proposed a way to see the quantum nature of gravity through the entanglement of a photon and a rotating mirror.
description: The exchange of gravitons between a photon and a rotating mirror gives rise to quantum entanglement. The strength of the link depends on the direction of rotation. This brings us closer to experimentally testing quantum gravity.
threads: Квантовая гравитация может запутывать свет и вращающийся объект. Направление вращения меняет силу их невидимой связи. Такой эксперимент в будущем проверит, из каких кирпичиков состоит гравитация — из частиц-гравитонов.

title: Nuclear Duathlon: Alpha and Beta Decay Combined
oneliner: Physicists have predicted a new type of radioactivity where a nucleus simultaneously emits an alpha particle and an electron.
description: Atomic nuclei can perform a double somersault: shooting out an alpha particle and an electron in a single act. This process links two fundamental interactions into a new probe for testing the Standard Model. Physicists have already found six examples of such decay, hidden in experimental data for de
abstract: Simultaneous alpha-beta decay is introduced as a new mode of nuclear instability, uniting strong and weak interactions in a single quantum transition. A theoretical model is developed to calculate relative probabilities and energy spectra of alpha particles; exclusive and inclusive criteria are formulated based on the openness or closure of individual alpha- and beta-decay channels. A global survey of the nuclear chart identified five exclusive αβ⁻ candidates, all predicted to be experimentally inaccessible, and ranked the leading inclusive candidates for αβ⁻ and αβ⁺ modes. Remarkably, the top six αβ⁺ candidates coincide with known nuclei that are precursors of beta-delayed alpha decay, and the observed alpha-particle spectra are naturally reproduced by direct simultaneous emission. These results establish simultaneous αβ-decay as a distinct radioactive process and a sensitive tool to probe the interplay of strong and weak interactions.
threads: Оказывается, некоторые атомные ядра способны жонглировать двумя видами излучения одновременно — выстреливать альфа-частицей и электроном в одном слаженном движении. Такой фокус позволяет природе обойти запреты и открывает новый способ заглянуть в глубины материи. Возможно, это ключ к пониманию того, как рождаются редкие изотопы в звёздах.

title: Black Hole as a Gravitational Atom: Gravitational Echo
oneliner: Spinning rapidly, a black hole gathers a cloud of ultralight particles—and turns into a giant atom. A passing ripple of spacetime triggers a cascade that produces a powerful delayed signal.
description: A spinning black hole accumulates a cloud of nearly weightless particles—a gravitational atom. A faint gravitational wave makes them synchronously "fall" from high orbits, creating a delayed burst. This flare is a key to detecting dark matter.
abstract: Boson clouds formed via superradiance around rotating black holes serve as novel probes for ultralight particles. We show that these gravitational atoms can undergo a self-stimulated avalanche: a coherent quadrupole transition is initiated by external gravitational waves and amplified by feedback from self-generated radiation. An efficient two-level description, validated by numerical calculations, reproduces the logistic population transfer and the formation of a delayed gravitational-wave pulse with a characteristic envelope. The detectability of such a pulse by future detectors is assessed. As a gravitational analog of superfluorescence, this cooperative emission mechanism opens a new observational channel into the ultralight dark sector.
threads: Вокруг вращающихся черных дыр могут скапливаться облака из сверхлегких частиц — как пушистый кокон. Слабая гравитационная волна, проходя сквозь такое облако, способна запустить лавину: частицы дружно «выстреливают» мощный гравитационный импульс. Это похоже на эхо от черной дыры, которое может выдать наличие невидимой темной материи. Будущие детекторы впервые дадут шанс поймать такой сигнал.

title: Heavy water ice found in planet-forming disk
oneliner: For the first time, the Webb telescope has spotted heavy water ice in a disk where planets are born.
description: The Webb telescope directly observed heavy water ice for the first time in a planetary disk—the birthplace of planets. There's unexpectedly a lot of it, pointing to a turbulent history of the ice that might explain the origin of Earth's oceans.
abstract: Analyzing observations of the edge-on protoplanetary disk 132-1832 in the Orion Nebula cluster with the NIRSpec spectrograph on the JWST and the ENIIGMA fitting tool, supplemented by unique laboratory data, has led to the first detection of HDO ice. The estimated upper limit of the HDO/H₂O ratio in this disk significantly exceeds values found for chondrites, comets, and embedded young stellar objects. Alongside HDO, the disk also contains ices of H₂O, CO₂, ¹³CO₂, CO, OCN⁻, and OCS, which have previously been detected in other disks. The detection of HDO points to efficient ice processing in the disk and matches laboratory experiments on deuterated ices. This result sheds light on the chemical continuity from the interstellar medium to planetary systems.
threads: Вокруг молодой звезды в туманности Ориона кружится диск, где рождаются планеты. Телескоп Уэбба впервые разглядел там лёд из тяжёлой воды — вместо водорода в ней дейтерий. Такая вода хранит память о путешествии через космос. Соотношение дейтерия к водороду оказалось рекордно высоким, намекая на то, как океаны попали на Землю.

title: Dark Energy: From Brake to Engine
oneliner: A new model shows how dark energy could first slow the expansion of the Universe and then accelerate it, avoiding catastrophe.
description: The Ph-ΛsCDM model describes the behavior of a special field that smoothly transitions from an attractive to a repulsive force. This resolves contradictions between different measurements of the expansion rate and predicts a stable future without the 'Big Rip'.
abstract: We present the Ph-ΛsCDM model—an implementation of the sign-switching cosmological constant (ΛsCDM) concept using a phantom scalar field within general relativity. The phantom field, with a kinetic term of the wrong sign, evolves on a bounded potential of hyperbolic tangent type, causing a smooth transition from a regime with negative dark energy density (analogous to anti-de Sitter) to positive (de Sitter) in the late Universe. The negative kinetic term, usually considered a pathology, here serves as a mechanism to lift the field from a vacuum state with negative energy to one with positive energy. We show that the field can create repulsion even when its energy density is still negative. Yet the cosmology remains under control: the total energy is positive, the late-time attractor is a de Sitter space without a Big Rip, and the dynamics are limited to the infrared regime. The model provides a concrete mechanism to address a number of cosmological tensions.
threads: Тёмная энергия не всегда была отталкивающей. Возможно, в прошлом она действовала как притяжение. Новая модель с фантомным полем показывает плавный переход без катастроф, проливая свет на расхождения в измерениях расширения Вселенной.

title: Triple Quantum Entanglement: The Whole Is Greater Than the Sum of the Pairs
oneliner: Three particles can be entangled more strongly together than just in pairs—and it can be measured.
description: At a future collider, electrons and positrons collide, producing a top quark, an antitop, and a Z boson. Their spins form a single quantum system, where the overall connection is more noticeable than individual pairs. Physicists have shown that such triple entanglement can actually be measured, open
abstract: Multipartite spin entanglement in the e+e- → t t̄ Z process at 1 TeV energy is investigated. Using Standard Model helicity amplitudes, the full 12×12 spin density matrix is reconstructed. Entanglement is characterized via one-to-one, one-to-others, and genuine multipartite negativities for three levels of phase-space integration. Pairwise entanglement is suppressed relative to collective (one particle vs two) and genuine multipartite types; all quantities diminish when kinematic information is excluded. Analysis of the fully leptonic decay channel shows that collective entanglement is accessible for measurement on a realistic polarized high-luminosity lepton collider; certification of genuine tripartite entanglement is possible only with limited sensitivity for a chosen polarization scenario at the planned ILC luminosity. The e+e- → t t̄ Z process serves as an attractive laboratory for studying multipartite entanglement, and the developed framework applies to any three-particle spin systems in a mixed state.
threads: На будущем коллайдере три частицы сплетаются в квантовый танец, где целое больше суммы пар. Обычно запутанность изучают на парах, но здесь впервые замахнулись на трио — и оно оказалось крепче. Это как музыкальный аккорд, который нельзя свести к отдельным нотам, и он может рассказать о глубинных законах мироздания.

title: Hunting Ghost Particles from a Supernova
oneliner: Physicists have found a way to catch mysterious particles that reach Earth with a delay — like an echo of the neutrino burst.
description: A new method will allow catching particles with a microscopic charge, born in supernova explosions. They arrive after neutrinos, like a delayed wave. Perhaps dark matter is made of exactly such particles.
abstract: A new approach is proposed for searching for millicharged particles (mCPs) using signals from collapsing supernovae. Massive mCPs born during core collapse arrive at Earth-based detectors with a time delay relative to massless neutrinos, creating a background-free window after the neutrino burst passes. A calculation of electron recoil signals in detectors XENONnT, JUNO, DUNE, and Hyper-Kamiokande for typical supernova scenarios is performed. It is shown that with an mCP charge coupling ε = 10⁻⁹ and masses from sub-MeV to MeV, more than 10 events per year can be expected. This method can strengthen the existing constraint on ε from supernova cooling analysis by roughly an order of magnitude.
threads: После взрыва сверхновой на Землю приходит не только свет и нейтрино, но и таинственные «опоздавшие» частицы — они летят медленнее из-за своей массы. Учёные поняли, как поймать этих невидимок с помощью подземных детекторов, открыв окно в новый мир тёмной материи.

title: Quantum Trick Accelerates the Universe Without Dark Energy
oneliner: The universe is expanding faster and faster — possibly due to a quantum rule, not mysterious dark energy.
description: The universe is expanding at an accelerating rate. Usually this is blamed on dark energy. But a new explanation is post-selection, a quantum trick where the future selects scenarios that suit it. This removes the mystery and explains why the acceleration started just now.
abstract: An alternative explanation for cosmic acceleration is proposed, rooted in quantum post-selection—a standard quantum procedure typically ignored in cosmology. Instead of initial-condition models, we construct a minimal predictive model where post-selection and coarse-graining create an effective late-time acceleration, with no cosmological constant or modifications to general relativity. The expansion history is theoretically tight, adding at most two parameters beyond standard Friedmann evolution. Fitting to Type Ia supernovae and cosmic chronometer data gives statistically competitive results and naturally dodges the coincidence problem. The model mirrors standard behavior in the radiation- and matter-dominated eras and predicts a jerk parameter today that sharply differs from ΛCDM. The findings hint that cosmic acceleration might be a macroscopic quantum phenomenon.
threads: Квантовая механика разрешает не только предопределённое прошлое, но и фиксированное будущее. Учёные проверили, что будет, если Вселенная «знает» свой итог: тогда её ускоренное расширение возникает само собой — без таинственной тёмной энергии. Такой фокус уже подтверждается данными с космических «маяков»-сверхновых.

title: Dark Matter Decays and Slows the Universe's Growth
oneliner: Dark matter may be slowing its own growth by decaying into invisible radiation.
description: Galaxy clusters in recent epochs have been growing more slowly than expected. The culprit could be dark matter decaying into lightweight particles that create a cosmic 'wind,' hindering clumping. This new model leaves a telltale signature that future sky surveys could detect.
abstract: Analysis of fσ8 and weak lensing data suggests that the linear growth rate at z≲1 may be lower than ΛCDM predictions. The iDCDM (interacting decaying cold dark matter) model is built, where dark matter particles experience friction with dark radiation born from their decay. The friction does not weaken but grows toward late times. The model adds two parameters, keeping background evolution, primordial nucleosynthesis, and the CMB unchanged, but leads to a step-like suppression of the growth factor f(k,z) — a target for DESI, Euclid, and Rubin. With current data, iDCDM is preferred over ΛCDM: Δχ² from -2.7 to -7.6 depending on the redshift scaling of friction and neutrino mass. Definitive tests will require k- and z-resolved measurements of structure growth.
threads: Вселенная растёт, но в последние эпохи — как-то лениво. Возможно, тёмная материя сама себя тормозит: распадается на невидимые частицы, создающие космический ветер, который мешает галактикам собираться. У этого эффекта есть чёткий отпечаток, и Euclid с Rubin скоро его поищут.

title: Gravity Entangles Particles with a Delay
oneliner: Quantum gravity creates a connection between particles, but not instantly: the delay grows with distance.
description: Physicists calculated that gravity can entangle particles, but not instantly. The delay depends on distance, like ripples running across water. The effect is tiny, but it can be slightly amplified. This confirms the quantum nature of gravity.
abstract: The interaction between propagating modes of the quantized gravitational field and two massive particles trapped in a harmonic oscillator potential is analyzed. Using an operator-based method within the Feynman-Vernon influence functional framework, it is shown that the gravitational field's commutation relations encode the effect on entanglement generation. This indicates that entanglement arises purely from quantum contributions of the field. The results reveal that entanglement is not instantaneous; instead, a time delay proportional to the inter-particle distance occurs, reflecting the causal nature of gravitational interactions. The generated entanglement is extremely small, but can be enhanced by preparing the particles in initial squeezed states, though the effect remains minuscule.
threads: Квантовая гравитация может связывать частицы, но не мгновенно. Расчёт показал, что между возникновением запутанности есть пауза, зависящая от расстояния — как эхо, которому нужно время, чтобы вернуться. Эффект крошечный, но его можно чуть усилить. Шаг к разгадке квантовой природы гравитации.

title: Mysterious Trace on Titan's Surface
oneliner: The James Webb Telescope has discovered an unexplained chemical trace on Titan and Pluto.
description: Titan's dense haze long concealed its surface. But in infrared light, the JWST telescope discerned a narrow dark band — a chemical fingerprint of an unknown substance. A similar, but broader, one was found on Pluto. This discovery brings us closer to unraveling the composition of distant icy worlds.
abstract: Spectroscopy of Titan's surface is hindered by its dense N₂–CH₄ atmosphere. Leveraging JWST's high sensitivity and broad spectral coverage, NIRSpec and MIRI spectra were acquired in the disk-center region, focusing on the 5-µm transparency window. Comparison of the averaged NIRSpec spectrum with a radiative transfer model accounting for gaseous and aerosol absorption revealed an unidentified band at 5.113 µm (1956 cm⁻¹) with a depth of 6–7%. Its width in the trailing hemisphere spectrum was 0.024±0.0008 µm (9.2±0.3 cm⁻¹), and from MIRI leading hemisphere data it might be ~25% narrower. The absorption most likely originates from the surface. Among published laboratory spectra of ices from Titan's chemical repertoire, no match was found, but several plausible candidates are proposed. A 4–5% deep absorption, roughly three times broader than on Titan, was recorded in MIRI spectra of Pluto.
threads: На Титане, скрытом плотной оранжевой дымкой, телескоп Джеймс Уэбб разглядел загадочное тёмное пятнышко в инфракрасном свете. Оно не похоже ни на один известный лёд и, возможно, указывает на неизвестное вещество на поверхности. Похожий след нашёлся и на Плутоне. Чья это химическая подпись — пока загадка.

title: Twisted Solar Loop Reveals the Secret of Magnetic Explosions
oneliner: Twisted like a phone cord, a magnetic loop on the Sun snapped and ejected gas, showing that plasma pulsations are a universal way to measure magnetic fields across the Universe.
description: A solar magnetic loop, twisted by 540 degrees, snapped at one end alone, spewing hot gas and hard X-rays. This proves that magnetic reconnection accelerates particles and heats the corona. Scientists found a pattern: the frequency of plasma pulsations directly indicates the strength of the magnetic
abstract: First direct observation of full twisted-loop reconnection in a solar flare: the loop was twisted up to 540°, far exceeding the 180° typically assumed in models. The extreme twist enables multiple X-line reconnection, similar to turbulence. Unlike simulations, the intertwined end ruptures unilaterally, creating open field lines and ejecting hot plasma — a potential mechanism for coronal heating. Hard X-ray emission from the current sheet was detected for the first time — direct evidence of particle acceleration. A power-law relation between quasi-periodic oscillation frequency and magnetic field was identified for objects ranging from solar flares to gamma-ray bursts, pointing to a universal flare mechanism and providing a "ruler" for cosmic magnetic fields. This is an observational foundation for future theories and a unified approach to magnetically driven flares.
threads: На Солнце впервые увидели, как скрученная магнитная петля порвалась с одного конца и выбросила плазму. Это помогло найти универсальное правило для магнитных полей по всей Вселенной — теперь их можно измерять простым дрожанием.

title: Gravity: Classical or Quantum?
oneliner: Classical gravity would give a single particle the power of a supercomputer, but physics forbids it—so gravity must be quantum.
description: Physicists have shown: if gravity is classical and matter is quantum, then a qubit solves problems beyond any computer. This would violate a fundamental limit on computation. Since we don't see that, gravity must be quantum.
abstract: Assuming a classical gravitational field interacting with quantum fields via semiclassical Einstein equations, it has been shown that the weak-field dynamics of a massive non-relativistic qubit could potentially solve an NP-complete problem in polynomial time. The key factor is the nonlinearity of this semiclassical dynamics. Consequently, these two assumptions lead to a violation of the extended physical Church–Turing thesis, which is interpreted as pointing to the necessity of quantizing gravity.
threads: Обычный камешек, падая, может взломать код любой сложности — если только гравитация остаётся классической. Такой сценарий рассмотрели теоретики и пришли к выводу: раз в реальности такого не происходит, гравитация обязана быть квантовой.

title: Dark Matter Sways Distant Planets
oneliner: Ultra-distant planets betray hidden clumps of dark matter.
description: Planets on the outskirts of star systems prove to be perfect scales for invisible matter. A drifting clump of dark matter nudges their orbit just enough— a shift we can now measure. This method could uncover the primordial invisible structures lingering from the Big Bang and let us chart the dark un
abstract: Ultra-wide-orbit exoplanets have been proposed as a novel tool to study small-scale dark matter objects. These systems are highly sensitive to gravitational perturbations from a galactic population of compact baryon-free objects, whether point-like or extended. Using ultracompact mini-halos, formed from large primordial perturbations deviating from a scale-invariant spectrum, new constraints have been derived on the injection scale and amplitude of such perturbations. These constraints complement existing dynamical limits and will improve with upcoming exoplanet survey data. Discovering more loosely bound planets will significantly tighten these limits. Distinct observational signatures have been identified, enabling the tracing of the dark object population. This boosts exoplanet science's potential to probe the dark Universe, down to its initial properties.
threads: Самые далёкие планеты, висящие на грани отрыва от своих звёзд, работают как космические весы: малейший тёмный сгусток качнёт их орбиту. Это открывает путь к картографированию невидимой материи во всей Галактике.

title: Information — The Shadow of What Never Was
oneliner: Physicists have proposed a new view: information is not a substance, but the shadow of all possible events.
description: What is information? Not a substance, nor an empty abstraction. Rather, it’s the shadow of all the options that never came to be. When we erase data, the shadow vanishes, releasing a tiny amount of heat. In black holes, this shadow thickens but doesn’t disappear—a puzzle leading to quantum gravity.
abstract: The paper proposes a third way to understand the ontological status of information: it is neither reducible to physical substance nor pure mathematics, but rather a counterfactual structure—the set of physically realizable alternatives that could have occurred. Drawing on Shannon’s combinatorial definition, Landauer’s principle, the no-cloning theorem, and the black hole information paradox, the author demonstrates that information resides precisely in those outcomes that were not realized. This interpretation resolves contradictions: it clarifies why erasing data releases heat without making information material; why quantum superposition is richer than a classical mixture; and why information loss in black holes is physically significant. The approach is rooted in structural realism but shifts the focus from actual relations to modal (possible) ones. The conclusion discusses implications for the foundations of quantum mechanics, quantum gravity, and scientific ontology.
threads: Каждый раз, когда вы что‑то забываете, вы не просто теряете мысль — вы превращаете её в тепло. Учёные называют это «физичностью информации». Но что, если информация — не вещество, а все несбывшиеся варианты событий? Такой взгляд помогает разобраться с главной загадкой чёрных дыр: куда исчезает всё, что в них падает.

title: Smart Pushes for Quantum Swings
oneliner: Scientists have found a quick way to calculate smart pushes that make atomic systems endlessly repeat desired movements despite energy losses.
description: Quantum devices — from masers to ultra-precise clocks — rely on repetitive motions within themselves. Energy losses disrupt these rhythms, but precise 'pushes' can sustain them. Previously, calculating such pushes consumed hours of computer time. Now a mathematical trick does it almost instantly, pa
abstract: Periodically driven dissipative quantum systems can fall into stationary orbits—closed loops in phase space. Such regimes underpin cooling devices for qubit initialization, lasers, atomic clocks, magnetometers, and magnetic resonance techniques (steady-state free precession, dynamic nuclear polarization). Controlling these states with quantum optimal control is tricky: the standard GRAPE algorithm requires numerical time propagation and can't handle an infinitely repeating cycle. An efficient strategy is proposed for synthesizing control sequences that asymptotically steer the system onto a limit cycle passing through user-specified anchor points. Unlike approaches based on the Floquet-Lindblad formalism and effective Hamiltonians, the method directly constructs pulses to achieve the desired orbit. Computational cost is comparable to GRAPE; implementation is in the Spinach library.
threads: Учёные нашли быстрый способ рассчитывать идеальные «толчки» для квантовых систем, заставляя их бесконечно кружить по нужной орбите, словно вечные качели. Это ускорит создание сверхточных приборов.

title: There Is No Singularity in Black Holes
oneliner: Quantum effects prevent a point of infinite density from forming at the center of a black hole.
description: There is no point of infinite density at the center of a black hole—quantum effects cut off space at a finite radius, creating a 'quantum boundary.' No new physics is required.
threads: В центре черной дыры исчезает само пространство — не потому, что там точка бесконечной плотности, а потому что квантовая рябь делает его негладким, как скомканная бумага. Уравнения просто перестают работать. Вместо сингулярности — гладкая стенка. Возможно, это ключ к разгадке того, что ждет путешественников за горизонтом событий.

title: Gravitational memory makes wormholes more stable
oneliner: Gravitational memory — an eternal scar from a wave — weakens the repulsion and stabilizes the wormhole.
description: Physicists added gravitational memory to models of wormholes supported by the Casimir effect. Memory, like a wave's footprint, changes the energy distribution: the wormhole becomes sturdier and outwardly resembles a black hole. Its shadow matches measurements of M87*, pointing the way to discovering
abstract: A class of traversable wormholes is investigated with an effective Casimir source supplemented by gravitational memory. On a time-dependent gravitational background, the vacuum polarization acquires a permanent shift, resulting in an energy density expressed as the sum of the usual negative term ~r^{-4} and a positive correction ~r^{-7}. The shape function is derived from Einstein's equations, and the redshift function from a barotropic equation of state with a regularity condition. The flare-out condition determines the allowed memory parameter and separates regimes dominated by Casimir versus phantom matter. Analysis of energy conditions shows inevitable violation of the radial null energy condition at the throat. The shadow radius computed for allowed solutions agrees with EHT observations of M87*. Gravitational memory softens the Casimir support, alters throat geometry, and modifies the internal stress distribution.
threads: Кротовую нору обычно удерживает отрицательная энергия, как в эффекте Казимира. Но если сквозь неё прошла гравитационная волна, память об этом событии меняет её структуру — делает устойчивее и внешне похожей на тень чёрной дыры. Возможно, телескопы уже видят такие объекты.

title: The Law of Unified Cooling: What Links Blazars and Gamma-Ray Bursts
oneliner: The light from blazars and gamma-ray bursts fades following the same law — astrophysicists have found a common mechanism behind these cosmic beacons.
description: Blazars are long-lasting beams from black holes in galactic cores; gamma-ray bursts are short flashes from stellar explosions. Scientists have discovered that, despite their different timescales, their radiation is shaped by the rapid cooling of electrons in a weakening magnetic field. This explains
abstract: Blazars and gamma-ray bursts (GRBs), despite their different natures (active galactic nuclei versus explosions of massive stars or mergers of compact objects), share a universal spectral shape described by a log-parabola. A simple model with a single optically thin region and a decreasing magnetic field naturally reproduces this spectrum for blazars. This indicates that the spectral shape is set by the cooling of relativistic electrons in the fast-cooling regime. Previously, it was shown that similar physics with a decreasing field explains the puzzling low-energy spectral index of most GRBs. Therefore, it is cooling, not particle acceleration, that determines the observed radiation. This result challenges established ideas and may underlie the empirical universal connection between the jet energetics of blazars and GRBs, opening new directions in astrophysics.
threads: Блазар-«прожектор» и гамма-вспышка-«молния» — совсем разные космические явления. Но их свет подчиняется единому закону быстрого остывания электронов. Значит, энергетика всех струй во Вселенной устроена одинаково — от гигантских чёрных дыр до нейтронных звёзд.

title: Solar Neutrinos in a Dark Matter Trap
oneliner: Dark matter detectors have been repurposed for the first time to snare nearly ghostly solar neutrinos.
description: Scientists from the XENONnT collaboration have shown that ultrasensitive detectors built to hunt dark matter can spot neutrinos born from thermal motion in the Sun's core. The energy of these particles is like a dust speck tossed by the wind, once thought nearly invisible. The method lets us study t
abstract: It has been shown that thermal solar neutrinos with energies around keV, arising from electroweak processes in the solar plasma, are kinematically accessible to large-volume dark matter detectors through electron ionization signatures. Analysis of XENONnT data in S2-only mode (ionization signal) set an upper limit on the flux of such neutrinos: η ≲ 1.2×10^8 times the Standard Model prediction. Combined searches with XENONnT, LZ, and PandaX yield slightly weaker constraints. The future XLZD experiment is expected to improve these limits by many orders of magnitude. While actual detection is still far off, this result establishes low-threshold direct search detectors as an effective tool for studying the lowest-energy neutrino sources in astrophysics, with important implications for stellar physics and beyond.
threads: Детектор для поиска тёмной материи неожиданно поймал следы солнечных нейтрино. Словно сверхчуткий микрофон, он различил шёпот среди грохота. Этот трюк откроет новое окно в физику звёзд.

title: Visitors from the Abyss: How Passing Planets Shaped Earth's Fate
oneliner: A planet flying past Earth could trigger floods, eruptions, and the death of the dinosaurs.
description: Hundreds of rogue planets lurk on the outskirts of the Solar System. Their close encounters with Earth—even without a collision—cause waves, eruptions, and climate shifts. This explains past mass extinctions.
abstract: Observational data point to a likely abundance of planetary-mass objects lurking in the outer solar system. Gravitational nudges can occasionally send them inward. While the classic Moon-forming scenario involves a Mars-sized impactor hitting early Earth, glancing blows or near-miss flybys are actually more probable. These events unleash intense tidal forces: mega-tsunamis, volcanic paroxysms, retreating seas, synchronized meteor showers, and rapid climate swings. When matched with the geological record, such cataclysms can account for several of the big mass extinctions over the past 600 million years. These episodes likely repeat throughout Earth's deeper history, too. And as smaller planets were swallowed by larger ones and by the Sun over 4 billion years, that could have added extra temperature shakes to the rocky worlds.
threads: Иногда мимо Земли проносятся планеты-странники, вызывая чудовищные приливные волны и извержения. Возможно, именно так 66 млн лет назад погибли динозавры — не от астероида, а от гравитационного удара.

title: The Mystery of Eclipse Shadow Bands Solved
oneliner: A simple explanation of the shimmering light bands appearing at the edge of a solar eclipse turns them into a tool for studying the atmosphere.
description: Shadows resembling ripples from a tossed pebble arise from light's wave nature. The edges of the solar crescent act as two sources, creating interference. This discovery lets us measure air turbulence from the bands.
abstract: Shadow bands are transient wave-like patterns of light and shadow observed before and after totality during a solar eclipse. A geometric-optical solution is proposed: the extended structure of the Sun generates a celestial analogue of Young's double-slit experiment, producing an interference pattern of intensity on the Earth's surface, modulated by atmospheric effects. The analysis combines solar limb geometry, atmospheric radiation propagation, and a wave formulation, yielding quantitative predictions of band width and spacing. The resulting model reproduces key observational characteristics of shadow bands and explains why the phenomenon is elusive and strongly dependent on observation conditions.
threads: Перед полным солнечным затмением на земле бегут дрожащие полосы-призраки. Новое открытие: это гигантский опыт с двумя щелями, где роль щелей играют края Солнца. Разгадка многовековой тайны не только объясняет феномен, но и даёт способ зондировать атмосферу.

title: Single Photons in High Harmonics
oneliner: Predicted for the first time: when ultraviolet flashes are born, light flows like a stream of individual droplets, not a continuous jet.
description: Scientists have theoretically proven that in extreme ultraviolet light from a laser, photons are born strictly one at a time. This discovery promises simple sources of single light particles for quantum communication and super-microscopy.
abstract: Photon antibunching is a direct proof of the existence of photons, with no classical analog. While bunching statistics can be described semi-classically, antibunching demands full quantization of the electromagnetic field. In high-harmonic generation (HHG), where many pump photons are converted into one high-energy photon, unambiguous evidence of single-photon emission has been missing. For the first time, photon antibunching is predicted in HHG, revealing nonclassical temporal correlations. Unlike previously discussed ensemble signatures of nonclassicality, such as sub-Poissonian statistics or squeezing, this signature corresponds to a single photon. Using a Heisenberg-picture approach for quantum-optical HHG, clear manifestations of antibunching emerge in the intensity correlation function across the entire harmonic spectrum.
threads: Лазерный луч, ударяя в газ, рождает не толпу фотонов, а одиночек — как водопад, который разбивается на отдельные капли, никогда не летящие рядом. Такой эффект, впервые предсказанный для генерации высоких гармоник, сулит дешёвые источники одиночных ультрафиолетовых фотонов для нерушимой связи и съёмки атомного кино.

title: How a tiny satellite makes a black hole 'sing'
oneliner: Scientists have discovered: a small satellite orbiting a rapidly spinning black hole can excite nearly eternal oscillations in it.
description: Researchers have shown: a tiny neighbor, through its tidal influence, rocks the almost eternal hum of a black hole. This resonance turns gravitational waves into a precise tool for measuring gravity at the very edge of the hole.
abstract: We show that extreme mass-ratio inspirals (EMRIs) can resonantly excite zero-damping modes (ZDMs) of near-extremal Kerr black holes within vacuum general relativity. Frequency-domain analysis of the Teukolsky equation for eccentric, inclined geodesics reveals an orbital harmonic whose real frequency falls within the half-width of a fundamental gravitational ZDM pole. In the complex response, the pole contribution is amplified by this narrow half-width; complex-response tomography reconstructs the independently computed Kerr pole from real-frequency orbital data. After removing the smooth non-pole part, the residual exhibits a phase jump consistent with a coherent simple pole, with the pole contribution comparable in amplitude to the non-pole part. The excited branch lies in the superradiant regime and carries a negative flux on the horizon. These results resolve the ZDM resonant response through a pole for the first time and suggest using the reconstructed half-width to measure the horizon surface gravity.
threads: У чёрных дыр есть «голос» — долгие колебания, которые почти не затухают. Крохотный спутник, падая на такую дыру, может заставить её «петь» громче, подобно тому как ритмичный стук молоточка раскачивает колокол. А по чистоте звука можно измерить силу тяжести на её поверхности.

title: Inside Uranus and Neptune Lie Oceans of Magma
oneliner: Raging hot magma saturated with hydrogen churns inside Uranus and Neptune — not frozen water and methane.
description: A new study turns our understanding of Uranus and Neptune upside down. Instead of layers of frozen water and methane, an ocean of magma soaked with hydrogen likely sloshes inside them. This idea explains all the mysteries of these planets and makes them kin to distant worlds around other stars.
abstract: Uranus and Neptune are commonly interpreted as 'ice giants' with a high abundance of volatiles, forming the basis of most interior structure models. Here we show that their observed radii, mean densities, gravitational harmonics, normalized moments of inertia, intrinsic luminosities, and key atmospheric composition features are consistent with models featuring supercritical, hydrogen-rich magma oceans overlain by hydrogen-helium envelopes. Using only three fitting parameters per planet, the results provide a parsimonious explanation for the structure, thermal state, and atmospheric chemistry of Uranus and Neptune. We find that the Solar System's ice giants are better described as magma-ocean giants, whose origin parallels that of gas dwarf planets like sub-Neptunes. This continuum among gas dwarfs makes Uranus and Neptune accessible, data-driven benchmarks for structural models and material properties applied to the study of sub-Neptunes.
threads: Уран и Нептун всегда считались ледяными гигантами. Но, возможно, внутри них плещется раскалённый океан магмы, пропитанный водородом. Это делает их похожими на далёкие экзопланеты и превращает в ключ к пониманию тысяч других миров.

title: Tiny Diamond Compass Peers Inside Batteries
oneliner: A miniature diamond sensor, like a tiny compass, effortlessly finds currents inside batteries without opening them or needing bulky shielding.
description: They broke the old trade-off between size and sensitivity. The new 6mm probe picks up magnetic fields millions of times weaker than Earth's. Tests on a lithium-ion battery let them 'see' the currents inside without opening the case. This opens doors to non-destructive testing in tech and medicine.
abstract: A mini diamond quantum magnetometer with a head diameter of 6 mm and sensitivity of 91 pT/√Hz (2 kHz bandwidth) operates in an unshielded environment by solving the fluorescence collection problem. This is achieved using a multi-core optical fiber bundle that separates excitation and collection channels, along with a custom micro-objective with a high numerical aperture. A compact FPGA unit handles microwave control, synchronous detection, and resonance tracking, ensuring stability during imaging. Its capabilities were demonstrated by mapping the magnetic field of a commercial lithium-ion battery, reconstructing depth-resolved current maps. The combination of sensitivity and probe geometry opens new avenues for measurements in confined volumes, particularly in battery technology.
threads: Крошечный алмаз, тоньше шарика в ручке, превратили в сверхчувствительный магнитный сенсор. Он улавливает колебания магнитного поля внутри работающего аккумулятора и показывает, где течёт ток. Теперь можно проверять батареи без разборки и без громоздкой защиты от помех — достаточно тонкого зонда.

title: Politics as a Galaxy: Public Opinion Obeys Physics
oneliner: Quantum math helped model how government support rises and falls over time.
description: Researchers built a model where the ruling coalition is a galaxy: parties are stars, voters are invisible dark matter. Using equations from quantum physics, they showed how government cohesion and actual outcomes shape public trust. The model predicts three scenarios and reveals why some governments
abstract: We examine the use of non-self-adjoint Hamiltonians and the associated Heisenberg dynamics to model a political system comprising a coalition of parties (the government) and their supporters. The aim is to describe how supporter opinions evolve in response to the perceived effectiveness, competence, and coherence of the coalition's actions. After a general introduction, three specific models are proposed, and the dynamical behavior of the full system (supporters and coalition) is investigated and discussed. Detailed analysis is given to balanced Hamiltonians—a class of operators recently introduced in the literature in connection with integrals of motion.
threads: Оказывается, правительство и галактику роднят невидимые силы: колебания поддержки миллионов можно рассчитать, как движение тёмной материи, — с помощью квантовых уравнений. Физики нашли способ предсказывать рост и падение рейтинга коалиции без соцопросов.

title: Early dark energy reconciles the different ‘thermometers’ of the Universe
oneliner: Data from the Webb telescope and the DESI survey have nearly settled the long-standing dispute over the expansion rate of the Universe.
description: Astronomers added a short-lived dark energy to the standard model, which appeared right after the Big Bang. This idea reconciled two methods of measuring cosmic expansion that previously gave different speeds—like two different thermometers. This approach strengthens the model without a major overha
abstract: Observations of distant galaxies by JWST and baryon acoustic oscillations from DESI seriously test the standard ΛCDM model, exacerbating the Hubble tension (H₀). The ability of the early dark energy (EDE) model, within a canonical axion EDE variant, to alleviate this tension is investigated using data from: Planck, ACT, SPT cosmic microwave background; DESI baryon acoustic oscillations; and JWST ultraviolet luminosity functions of galaxies. A joint analysis of CMB+DESI+JWST raises H₀ to 71.58±1.05 km/s/Mpc, reducing the tension to 1.0σ. The model also improves the fit to JWST data and shows statistically significant superiority over ΛCDM: Δχ²_total = -18.26, ΔDIC = -11.89. These results highlight the synergy between JWST high-redshift galaxy data and both early- and late-time cosmological observations for testing EDE and resolving the H₀ tension.
threads: Два космических «термометра» десятилетиями спорили о скорости расширения Вселенной. Оказалось, виной могла быть невидимая сила, которая на миг ускорила рост мира сразу после его рождения — как дрожжи, добавленные в тесто. Новые данные с Уэбба почти помирили астрономов.

title: Young Stars: Gamma Rays from Jets
oneliner: Newborn stars have been found emitting gamma rays: their jets accelerate protons, which produce powerful radiation.
description: It turns out young stars don't just warm their cradles but also emit gamma rays. This happens when their jets, like from a hose, slam into clouds of gas and dust. Now we can peer through the dust into the hidden processes of planet birth.
abstract: A statistically significant gamma-ray signal has been detected from a population of young stellar objects, establishing a galactic class of gamma-ray-bright protostars. The emission arises from protons accelerated in protostellar jets and their subsequent interaction with surrounding molecular clouds via pion production and decay. A correlation has been found between the cosmic-ray flux and bolometric luminosity, indicating that particle acceleration scales with the mechanical power of the system. These results open a new observational window onto the role of nonthermal processes in protostellar evolution and provide a key to understanding accretion, outflows, and feedback in star formation. This previously overlooked emission serves as an indicator of the energetic feedback that young stars exert on the interstellar medium, shaping conditions for future generations of stars and planets.
threads: Рождение звезды — это не просто тихое нагревание. Новое исследование показало: многие протозвёзды — это мощные гамма-излучатели. Их джеты, словно гигантские ускорители, разгоняют протоны до околосветовых скоростей, и те, врезаясь в облака газа, рождают вспышки гамма-лучей. Это скрытое свечение может менять всю колыбель, где формируются планеты.

title: A Planet's Rotation Revealed Its Fiery Secrets
oneliner: The Webb Space Telescope captured for the first time how a scorching planet's rotation reshapes its atmosphere right during an eclipse.
description: Astronomers studied WASP-121 b, a gas giant always facing its star with one side. As the planet transited the star, they detected changes in light absorption: the eastern limb was hotter than the western, which destroys water and leaves carbon monoxide. This opens a method for scanning weather on su
abstract: Observations of two transits of the ultra-hot Jupiter WASP-121 b, made with the NIRSpec and NIRISS instruments on the James Webb Space Telescope, revealed an asymmetry in transit light curves caused by the planet's rotation. The transmission spectrum shows an increase in CO absorption and a weak decline in H2O absorption as the planet rotates, indicating a stronger longitudinal temperature gradient on the evening terminator than on the morning one. This aligns with a higher temperature on the eastern half of the dayside compared to the western half. The observed changes in atmospheric composition correspond to thermal dissociation of H2O at high temperatures, while CO remains stable. The detection of longitudinal gradients in temperature and chemical composition due to the planet's rotation during transit opens a new method for probing atmospheric inhomogeneity on exoplanets with JWST, complementing studies of morning-evening terminator asymmetry.
threads: Планета WASP-121 b всегда обращена к своей звезде одной стороной, но когда она проходит перед звездой, телескоп Уэбба видит, как жара перемещается по её поверхности: восточная сторона оказалась горячее западной. Это открытие может помочь предсказывать погоду на других мирах.

title: The Earth may survive when the Sun becomes a giant
oneliner: New calculations: Earth likely won't burn up when the Sun turns into a red giant — it will move away to a safe distance.
description: The main enemy here is not heat, but gravity. If the Sun sheds mass fast enough, Earth will retreat to a more distant orbit. Judging by observations of similar stars, we have a pretty good chance.
abstract: We analyzed Earth's long-term orbital evolution factoring in the Sun's expansion, tidal interactions, and mass loss across the red giant branch (RGB) and asymptotic giant branch (AGB) stages. We used modern stellar tracks for a solar-mass star and updated tidal dissipation models, exploring different mass-loss rate scenarios. The outcome hinges critically on the tidal model: with current parameters, Earth survives both giant phases, whereas older recipes lead to engulfment during the AGB. Low mass-loss rates mean engulfment; high rates mean survival. Adopting the observed mass-loss rate of L2 Puppis as a proxy for the future Sun points to Earth's survival. But observational uncertainties leave Earth's final fate murky, spotlighting the need for tighter constraints on late-stage stellar evolution.
threads: Солнце раздуется и может поглотить Землю, но новые модели приливов дают шанс: планета способна выжить. Ключевой фактор — скорость потери массы звездой. Наблюдения звезды L2 Pup вселяют оптимизм.

title: Tiny Crystal Measures Temperature and Magnetic Field Simultaneously
oneliner: A microscopic flaw in a transparent crystal works as a two-way sensor: its color reveals temperature, while its blinking rhythm tracks magnetic fields.
description: Scientists built a sensor from boron nitride — a transparent cousin of graphite. It contains a glowing speck whose hue shifts with heat and flicker rate changes with magnetism. So one miniature device replaces both thermometer and magnetometer, with no cross-talk. The technology will find use in mic
abstract: Simultaneous nanoscale measurement of temperature and magnetic field is in demand across many scientific and technological fields. However, cross-sensitivity to different physical quantities makes it difficult to use a single sensor for multiple parameters. This work demonstrates independent dual sensing of temperature and magnetic field using single quantum emitters in hexagonal boron nitride (hBN). The independence of the zero-phonon line (ZPL) position's response to temperature and the optically detected magnetic resonance (ODMR) to magnetic field is experimentally confirmed. Local temperature measurement of a microchip was performed with simultaneous monitoring of the external magnetic field. The results establish quantum emitters in hBN as a reliable platform for multifunctional quantum sensing under realistic conditions.
threads: Крошечный дефект в кристалле работает как градусник и компас одновременно: от тепла меняет цвет, от магнита — яркость. Такие двухрежимные квантовые сенсоры позволят следить за перегревом чипов и считывать магнитные сигналы в мозге.

title: Vacuum’s Memory: How Information Survives in a Black Hole
oneliner: The vacuum is no emptiness—it’s a cunning web of threads that stops information from vanishing inside a black hole.
description: A new hypothesis suggests the vacuum is a network of invisible threads. When a star collapses, the threads tangle into a dense knot—a fuzzball—whose surface imprints everything that fell in. Nature saves information from disappearing.
abstract: The paradox of information loss in a black hole requires a breakdown of the semiclassical approximation in a region of weak curvature, which seems impossible. The vecron hypothesis offers a structure of the gravitational vacuum capable of such a breakthrough. This paper presents the essence of the hypothesis and constructs a lattice model illustrating its key idea. The Hamiltonian of the model is fully local, but the vacuum exhibits correlations between Planck-scale fluctuations that decay rather slowly with distance. These large-scale correlations allow one to "feel" the region where a closed trapped surface is about to form, and respond by creating a fuzzy ball structure that destroys semiclassical spacetime.
threads: Квантовая пена космоса не просто шумит — она плетёт защитную сеть от чёрных дыр. Когда дыра пытается родиться, вакуум вяжет клубок, сохраняя каждую крупицу информации.

title: The Quantum Secret to Fast AI Training
oneliner: Quantum communication has doubled neural network training speed and made it fully private.
description: When training neural networks on thousands of machines, data exchange slows down the process and creates a risk of leaks. Replacing ordinary signals with quantum communication allows transmitting twice as much information at once with full privacy: entangled particles prevent spying. The method work
abstract: For distributed training, we propose a quantum ring all-reduce protocol that uses pre-distributed entanglement and superdense coding to halve per-connection traffic without reworking the computation model. On top of that, verified entanglement achieves composable ε-secure aggregation — impossible for any classical protocol — at the cost of twice the GHZ state consumption. The hybrid architecture yields simultaneous gains in efficiency and security for both classical and quantum learning. After the all-reduce step, we address gradient conflict detection in server-client communication: in the GapIP_{τ} variant, we get a quadratic quantum advantage in parameter τ (qubit complexity \widetilde{O}(τ^{-1} \log P) versus classical \widetilde{O}(\min(τ^{-2},P)) bits), and in TieAudit_{ε}, an exponential gap emerges (O(ε^{-2} \log P) qubits suffice, classically requiring Ω(\sqrt{P}) bits).
threads: Обучение больших нейросетей на сотнях машин тормозят их «переговоры». Квантовый подход решает это: один сигнал несёт два бита данных с помощью запутанных частиц, и подслушать невозможно. ИИ учится вдвое быстрее и приватно, а проверка результатов даёт выигрыш, классике недостижимый.

title: Why the Moon Is a Copy of Earth? The Fluidity Clue
oneliner: The collision of solid Theia with a liquid Earth explains why the Moon is so similar to our planet.
description: The Moon is almost identical to Earth in composition, which contradicts giant impact models. A new hypothesis: Theia was solid and cold, while Earth was a boiling ball. The impact splashed mostly terrestrial material into space, and the Moon inherited our planet’s chemical signature.
abstract: Numerical modeling indicates that over 40% of the material in the circumterrestrial disk produced by the impact originates from the impactor Theia, contradicting the Earth-like isotopic composition of the Moon (isotopic crisis). Accounting for viscosity differences between the colliding bodies provides a solution: smaller planets with magma oceans cool faster and become highly viscous, while larger ones remain low-viscosity. Simulating a collision between a high-viscosity (nearly solid) Theia and a low-viscosity (molten) proto-Earth yields a disk dominated by proto-Earth material, without violating modern constraints on the angular momentum of the Earth-Moon system. This mechanism naturally explains the observed geochemical similarity.
threads: Столкновение «твёрдой» Тейи с жидкой магмой Земли выбросило в космос в основном земное вещество. Поэтому Луна — почти близнец Земли по составу. Загадка лунных изотопов решена простой разницей в вязкости.

title: Gravity doesn't always hide the heart of a dying star
oneliner: In extended theories of gravity, dying stars may not turn into a black hole, but instead leave their core open for observation.
description: Imagine gravity as a stretched rubber sheet. A heavy star pushes it down into a deep funnel, which normally snaps shut, hiding the core. But new research shows: in some theories, the rubber is too bouncy, the funnel doesn't close, and light from the core seeps out.
abstract: An analysis of the role of higher Lovelock curvatures in the collapse of a dust spherical cloud is performed. It is shown that such terms do not restore local cosmic censorship, but rather promote local visibility of central singularities. In the branch with a positive leading Lovelock coefficient c_N, the order N governs the near-singularity collapse and the formation of trapped surfaces. In non-critical dimensions (D-1-2N>0), the apparent horizon curve approaches the singularity curve with a trapping exponent β_N=(D-1)/(D-1-2N); the local visibility condition is ℓ<β_N for an outward-opening singularity curve and a first-order correction r^ℓ. As N increases, the class of inhomogeneous initial data giving rise to outgoing rays from the center expands. In the critical case D=2N+1, the apparent horizon does not form near the center, and any opening of the singularity curve yields visibility. The singularities are Krolak-strong along the rays, and Tipler-strength is achieved at the threshold. The energy function influences the opening of the curve in the non-critical case and the final collapse velocity in the critical one.
threads: Умирающие звёзды обычно прячут свои ядра в чёрных дырах, но недавнее исследование показало: если гравитация чуть сложнее, ядро может остаться видимым. Как если бы дно пропасти вдруг осветилось. Такие «голые сингулярности» могли бы стать природными лабораториями, где законы физики ломаются у всех на виду.

title: Dark bubble and microcosm: gravity at millionths of a meter
oneliner: A new model explains the accelerated expansion of the Universe using a hidden dimension the size of a micron.
description: The 'dark bubble' model explains why the Universe is expanding faster and faster. It suggests a hidden dimension about a micron thick. At such distances, gravity should weaken dramatically, linking the microcosm to cosmology and providing testable predictions.
abstract: The dark bubble model exploits instabilities underlying the swampland conjectures of de Sitter space, making the accelerating expansion of the universe inevitable and explaining the positive cosmological constant. Its distinctive consequence is a unique hierarchy of scales: cosmological, gravitational, string, and higher-dimensional scale. In particular, the model naturally predicts the existence of a micron-sized dark dimension and the weakening of gravity at corresponding distances, realizing the fat graviton scenario. Additional predictions include a string scale on the order of tens of TeV and a measurable positive spatial curvature of the universe. An overview of key aspects highlights testable implications for gravity and cosmology.
threads: Спрятанное измерение размером с бактерию — вот что предлагает модель «тёмного пузыря». Гравитация на таких масштабах исчезает, и это приводит к ускоряющемуся расширению космоса. Микромир перестаёт играть по правилам Ньютона.

title: Red Dots in the Early Universe Are Black Holes Viewed Edge-On
oneliner: Distant 'red dots' are giant black holes turned sideways toward us: their thick gas disks hide the blinding radiation.
description: In the young universe, telescopes found many reddish dots. A new explanation: these are huge black holes that we see from the side. The gas disk around them blocks the most energetic radiation, allowing only dim red light and hydrogen glow. A similar object exists right in the Milky Way. If viewed f
abstract: Little red dots (LRDs) at high redshifts are compact sources with V-shaped spectra, broad emission lines, and strong Balmer jumps. The proposed model casts LRDs as supermassive analogues of the microquasar SS 433 observed at high inclination. Super-Eddington accretion, scaled up to supermassive scales, creates a self-screening inflated disk: X-ray weakness, soft optical continuum, apparent sub-Eddington luminosity, and the Balmer jump all emerge naturally. Anisotropic hard radiation from the inner disk ionizes the broad-line region, akin to the W50 nebula around SS 433. The model predicts a correlation between Balmer jump strength and line width, greater line variability relative to the continuum, that LRDs are brighter than observed, and that their low-inclination or less active cousins appear as blue dots. This viewpoint transforms LRDs into laboratories for studying rapid accretion growth at the dawn of galaxy formation.
threads: Загадочные «красные точки» в ранней Вселенной оказались гигантскими чёрными дырами, повёрнутыми к нам ребром — как прожектор, который сбоку светит красным. Возможно, так росли первые сверхмассивные чёрные дыры.

title: Сверхновые против космологического принципа: новый вердикт
oneliner: Анализ каталога Pantheon+ не находит убедительных доказательств крупномасштабной анизотропии, указывая на локальные эффекты и неоднородность данных.
description: Используя обновлённую компиляцию Pantheon+, содержащую 1701 сверхновую типа Ia, учёные применили методы дипольной аппроксимации и сравнения полушарий для поиска возможной анизотропии расширения Вселенной. Хотя в полной выборке значимых отклонений от изотропии не обнаружено, на малых красных смещения
threads: Космологи искали трещину в принципе изотропии, перебрав 1701 сверхновую. Оказалось, что виновато не мироздание, а лоскутное одеяло данных: один яркий обзор создал фантомное направление. Этот детектив напоминает, что даже космос может быть иллюзией, порождённой нашими инструментами. И пока мы не соткём равномерное небесное полотно, мы будем всматриваться в узор собственных предубеждений.

title: Гром и Нимбус: рождение ударной туманности от нейтронной звезды
oneliner: Радиотелескопы открыли кометарную туманность «Гром», выдуваемую пульсаром внутри остатка сверхновой «Нимбус», что проливает свет на финальные стадии эволюции таких систем.
description: Впервые детально исследована редкая система из остатка сверхновой и bow-shock плериона — туманности, создаваемой сверхзвуковым пульсаром. Благодаря наблюдениям на ASKAP, MeerKAT, ATCA и XMM-Newton удалось измерить плоский радиоспектр, высокую поляризацию и жёсткое рентгеновское излучение, подтвердив
