The results demonstrate that cells use active self-regulation, not mere noise suppression, to maintain stability. The work reveals a fundamental principle of organization in dynamical systems in biology.
threads: Клетка не глушит шум, а учится на нём. Представьте оркестр без дирижёра: каждый музыкант подстраивается под соседей, пока не рождается симфония. Так и гены: через обратную связь они находят устойчивый паттерн корреляций. Именно так клетки сохраняют идентичность в вечно меняющейся среде — и этот принцип однажды позволит программировать живые ткани без редактирования ДНК.

title: Two Quantum Sensors Peek Inside a Living Cell
oneliner: A new microscope allows simultaneous tracking of protein and diamond sensors inside a cell.
description: The new microscope controls two magnetic sensors inside a living cell: one made of protein, the other of diamond. They detect weak magnetic fields, and working together they create a stereo effect—like two ears. This will help decipher how cells communicate and lay the foundation for ultra-precise m
abstract: Merging two revolutions in quantum sensing: biological qubits based on radical pairs in living cells and solid-state spin qubits. A pulsed quantum microscope was developed, combining optical excitation at 450 and 520 nm with nanosecond gating, three-dimensional scanning at diffraction-limited resolution, rapid switching of static and radio-frequency magnetic fields, single-photon detection with picosecond resolution, and microwave control up to 5 GHz, covering resonances of NV centers (2.87 GHz) and radical pairs (0.5–0.8 GHz). Live-cell imaging was demonstrated with both qubit types coexisting in a single cell—multiplexed quantum sensing. This method opens new avenues for studying quantum processes in living systems and lays the groundwork for entangling a solid-state qubit with a protein-based spin qubit.
threads: Внутри живой клетки впервые заработали сразу два квантовых сенсора: один — из белка, другой — из алмазной крошки. Лазерный микроскоп управляет ими, как дирижёр оркестром, позволяя слышать магнитные шёпоты клеточных процессов. Это шаг к квантовому чтению сигналов нейронов и созданию сверхточных биосенсоров.

title: Gravitational Waves as a Mirror of Black Holes' Information Memory
oneliner: The memory burden effect makes black holes 'remember' their past, and this shows up as a frequency shift in gravitational waves from their mergers.
description: When black holes merge, they produce gravitational waves that carry information about their properties. The new memory burden effect suggests that the information stored in a black hole can alter its classical dynamics, and this should be reflected in the radiation spectrum. This article explores ho
abstract: The concept of swift memory burden is investigated—the ability of information stored in a black hole to influence its classical dynamics under perturbations, reflected in the gravitational-wave signal from mergers. The relationship between memory load and the characteristics of the collapsing source is analyzed: it is shown that the burden can significantly exceed the information content of the progenitor. The extreme case—a black hole formed by the collision of two particles—exhibits maximum burden. For stellar-mass black holes, constraints on the magnitude of the effect are derived, and the memory-induced shift in the quasi-normal modes of gravitational waves is quantitatively estimated. The results point to the possibility of probing fundamental mechanisms of information storage in black holes and their formation history through gravitational-wave observations.
threads: Каждое слияние чёрных дыр — не просто всплеск пространства-времени, а зашифрованная биография их рождения. Эффект бремени памяти наделяет дыру информационным рюкзаком, который меняет частоту гравитационного звона. Теперь мы можем услышать, откуда она родом: из сверхновой или из тёмного гало. LIGO превращается в декодер квантовых дневников Вселенной. Возможно, мы научимся читать историю каждой дыры по её последнему аккорду.

title: Hubble Tension in k-essence: Evidence for Robust Alleviation
oneliner: Dark energy models with kinetic self-interaction (k-essence) naturally resolve the discrepancy between measurements of the Hubble constant from the early and late Universe.
description: One of the main contradictions of modern cosmology—the Hubble tension—is the mismatch between the values of the Universe's expansion constant obtained from the cosmic microwave background (67.4 km/s/Mpc according to Planck) and from supernova observations in the local Universe (73.0 km/s/Mpc accordi
abstract: The Hubble tension is one of the most significant discrepancies in the standard ΛCDM cosmological model. K-essence models, which include non-trivial self-interactions of dark energy, can alter the background expansion after recombination, affecting the sound horizon calculation and thus the inferred Hubble constant. This study analyzed two physically motivated models — dilaton and tachyon — using data from Planck and five late-universe surveys: Pantheon+SH0ES, a set of cosmic chronometers (CC), the Union3 compilation, DES Y5, and DESI. For ΛCDM, the tension is 5.89σ; in the dilaton and tachyon models, combining all late-time data reduces it to 0.14σ and 0.69σ, respectively. This reduction is stable for any combination of datasets and requires no parameter tuning: the model parameter values remain unchanged regardless of the datasets used. The results show that the Hubble tension is not an inevitable feature of the late Universe but critically depends on how dark energy is described.
threads: Вселенная расширяется быстрее, чем должна — так утверждают сверхновые. Но что если ошибка не в данных, а в нашем представлении о невидимом дирижёре? Тёмная энергия в моделях k-эссенции не метроном, а живой музыкант, меняющий темп. Напряжение Хаббла в 5σ тает до статистического шума, стоит позволить уравнению состояния эволюционировать. Отголоски тахионного поля звучат фантастически, но именно такие идеи могут превратить космологический кризис в новую гармонию.

title: Quantum Dance of Dust Grains in a Magnetic Trap
oneliner: Scientists have made dust particles perform a quantum dance inside a magnetic trap.
description: The method allows particles to float in a magnetic field and behave like a dancer who is in two places at once. These experiments will help us understand the boundary between the quantum and classical worlds, and possibly detect dark matter.
abstract: A method is proposed to use magnetostatic traps based on higher-order multipole fields to create macroscopic quantum interference in the motion of levitating superconducting microparticles. Combining multipole magnetic fields allows shaping a variety of potentials, including anharmonic ones, such as the Duffing potential or a double well, where nonlinearity reaches about a hundred times the amplitude of zero-point fluctuations (less than a nanometer). These potentials are realized with a static coil configuration requiring only current adjustments and can be used to generate non-Gaussian states of the center-of-mass motion. An analysis of particle dynamics in phase space is performed, assessing parameter dependence. A protocol is proposed that statistically significantly distinguishes quantum from classical behavior by measuring the particle's position. The results open access to the quantum regime for objects with masses greater than a picogram (10^13 atomic mass units), enabling the study of the quantum-to-classical transition, the interplay of quantum physics with gravity, and certain types of dark matter.
threads: Крошечная частица, парящая в магнитном поле, вдруг начинает вести себя как приведение — одновременно и здесь, и там. Физики нашли простой способ поместить её в квантовую ловушку, чтобы разгадать тайны границы миров и, возможно, поймать тёмную материю.

title: The Fate of a Hot Jupiter: How a Primordial Black Hole Rewrites Planetary Systems
oneliner: Numerical simulations show that a flyby of a primordial black hole can eject a planet, capture it, or form a triple system.
description: Scientists investigated the dynamic consequences of a hypothetical primordial black hole (PBH) encountering the system TOI-2796, which hosts a hot Jupiter. Using direct three-dimensional numerical integrations within the three-body problem, they showed that over 100 days, three outcomes are possible
abstract: Numerical modeling of the close approach of a primordial black hole (PBH) to the TOI 2796 system, containing a hot Jupiter. Over a 100-day interval, three outcomes were obtained: (i) ejection of the planet from the system; (ii) formation of a bound triple system (semimajor axis ≈ 1 AU, orbital period ≈ 5 days); (iii) capture of the planet by the PBH into a binary system (0.0194 AU, 15 days), which leaves the star. The results underscore the rich dynamical diversity of interactions with PBHs and their potential role in reshaping planetary system architectures.
threads: В галактическом вальсе появляется незримый танцор — первичная чёрная дыра. Она способна украсть планету. Моделирование системы TOI-2796 показало: за 100 дней горячий юпитер либо становится изгоем, либо обретает жуткого компаньона. Если тёмная материя — это чёрные дыры, такие похищения происходят повсюду. Возможно, именно здесь ключ к загадке скрытой массы.

title: Three scenarios of an exoplanet encountering a primordial black hole
oneliner: Numerical simulation revealed three dramatic outcomes of a primordial black hole flying past the TOI-2796 system: planet ejection, capture, or the formation of a triple system.
description: Using three-body simulations, researchers explored how a hypothetical primordial black hole could affect the TOI-2796 system with a hot Jupiter. Three possible outcomes were identified: ejection of the planet from the system, formation of a stable triple system, and capture of the planet by the blac
abstract: A three-body numerical simulation was performed for the encounter of a primordial black hole (PBH) with the TOI 2796 system, which includes a hot Jupiter. Over a 100-day time span, three dynamical outcomes were identified: (i) ejection of the planet; (ii) formation of a stable triple system with a semi-major axis of ~1 AU and a period of ~5 days; (iii) capture of the planet by the black hole into a binary system with a semi-major axis of 0.0194 AU and a period of 15 days, which then departs from the star. The results reveal a nontrivial diversity of orbital evolutions during a PBH close encounter and point to the potential contribution of such objects to the restructuring of planetary systems.
threads: Чёрная дыра из начала времён пролетает мимо далёкой звезды. Её гравитация играет джаз — и в одной импровизации планета вылетает в межзвёздную тьму, в другой — три тела замирают в устойчивом ритме. А третий вариант нашла нейросеть: чёрная дыра уводит планету в вечное путешествие. Мы стоим на пороге новой астрономии — поиска орбитальных нот, сыгранных тёмной материей.

title: The Detective Droplet: How a Soft Particle Unveils the Secrets of Active Turbulence
oneliner: A passive droplet in an active nematic exhibits intermittent shape dynamics, becoming a sensitive probe of collective chaos.
description: Indian physicists, through detailed numerical simulations, explored how a passive nematic droplet deforms within the turbulent flow of an active fluid. It turned out that the droplet's aspect ratio serves as a unique scalar reflecting the statistics of the active bath: shape increments have heavy-ta
abstract: Full-scale nematohydrodynamic simulations of deformations of a passive nematic droplet in two-dimensional turbulence of an extensile active nematic were performed. The droplet's aspect ratio serves as a scalar probe: its increment distributions have heavy tails, and their time series is scale-free and shows intermittency, confirmed by multiscaling structure functions. The average deformation grows with activity, but relative intermittency is most pronounced at low activity, indicating slower and more coherent impacts. A hierarchy of intermittency was discovered: the droplet's shape is less intermittent than its displacement and the fluctuations of active stresses; this is explained by the suppression of sharp jumps by interfacial restoring forces. Power spectra of the maximum normal velocity of the interface contain an extended ~1/ω region, while the spectrum of active stresses at the boundary follows a ~1/ω² law.
threads: Мягкая капля ведёт расследование: редкие, сильные деформации живут по степенному закону, а в шуме звучит нота 1/f — гимн неравновесия. Как океан Соляриса, простота рождает сложность. Эти частицы — ключи к тайному порядку в живых клетках и микророботах грядущего.

title: Holographic Heat Engines for Schwarzschild Black Holes
oneliner: For the first time, physicists have built reversible heat engines whose working substance is a black hole in asymptotically flat spacetime, confined within a spherical cavity.
description: Using quasilocal gravitational thermodynamics, researchers constructed a holographic analogue of standard heat engines (Carnot, Otto, Diesel, Brayton, and Stirling) for a Schwarzschild black hole inside a spherical cavity. The pressure on the cavity walls and its area provide a natural pressure–volu
abstract: Reversible heat engines based on black holes in asymptotically flat spacetime have been constructed, utilizing quasilocal gravitational thermodynamics. A Schwarzschild black hole is placed inside a finite spherical cavity; the working substance is a dual thermal system at its boundary. Pressure and volume in this system are defined via surface pressure and the boundary area, while all coupling constants of the gravitational theory remain fixed. Exact expressions are obtained for the efficiencies of the Carnot, Otto, Diesel, Brayton, and Stirling cycles, and their numerical comparison is performed; these efficiencies reflect the quasilocal equations of state of the black hole. The efficiency of the regenerative Stirling cycle turns out to be extremely close to the Carnot bound and approaches it in the high-temperature limit.
threads: Дыра в банке? Да, и она работает как паровой поршень. Физики замкнули циклы Карно и Стирлинга для чёрной дыры в сферической полости: горизонт событий толкает стенки, объём меняется — рождается работа. КПД почти идеален, но кривизна пространства подпортила обратимость: крошечная неидеальность выдаёт квантовую природу гравитации. Мы в шаге от проверки квантовой гравитации тепловыми методами.

title: Neutron Stars with a Dark Core: What Does an Agnostic View Reveal?
oneliner: How an unbiased approach to dark matter inside neutron stars sets universal constraints and sheds light on the nature of hidden mass.
description: Modern astrophysics faces a double unknown: the nature of dark matter and the behavior of matter in the interiors of neutron stars. In a new study, scientists applied an agnostic method, abandoning specific dark particle models, and treated neutron stars as two-fluid systems where ordinary and dark
abstract: An agnostic model of neutron stars with dark matter admixture is constructed in a two-fluid formalism. The hadronic equation of state relies on chiral effective field theory at low densities and perturbative QCD at high densities, with a speed-of-sound parameterization in the intermediate region. Dark matter as a Fermi gas, its high-density equation of state constrained only by thermodynamic consistency via the speed of sound, without bias toward stiff or soft variants. It is found: light dark matter creates an extended halo and increases tidal deformability; heavy dark matter forms a compact core and decreases it. Observational data switch: for light dark matter, the main constraint comes from gravitational waves; for heavy, from NICER. The dark matter fraction fDM ≲ 0.11 (1σ) for light masses. The approach is weakly dependent on the microphysics of the dark sector, providing conservative bounds. Stars with similar masses but different deformability could become a clear sign of dark matter.
threads: Нейтронная звезда — не всегда то, чем кажется. Тёмная материя внутри превращает её поверхность в мираж: видимый диск — лишь край незримого облака. Агностический детектив поставил предел: скрытой массы — не более 11% для лёгких частиц. Звёзды-близнецы с разной деформируемостью станут тем самым «дымящимся пистолетом», который вытащит тёмную материю из сумрака.

title: Dark Cores and Halos: How Dark Matter Changes Neutron Stars
oneliner: A new model shows that dark matter can turn neutron stars into compact cores or puffy halos, changing how we search for them.
description: Scientists used an agnostic approach to study how dark matter affects the structure of neutron stars. Using a two-fluid model and sound speed interpolation, they explored a wide range of particle masses and dark matter fractions. Light dark matter forms extended halos that increase tidal deformabili
abstract: Dark matter admixed neutron stars are studied via a two-fluid formalism using agnostic hadronic and dark matter equations of state. Dark matter is modeled as a Fermi gas defined only by its low-density equation of state and mass. Hadronic matter is anchored at low densities by chiral effective field theory and at high densities by perturbative QCD; a sound-speed parametrization covers intermediate densities for hadrons and high densities for dark matter, ensuring thermodynamic consistency without bias. Light dark matter forms extended halos that increase tidal deformability, while heavy dark matter forms compact cores that decrease it. Observational constraints thus shift from gravitational-wave tidal deformability for light dark matter to NICER mass–radius data for heavy dark matter. At 1σ, the dark matter fraction is bounded to f_DM ≲ 0.11 for light dark matter. This agnostic framework yields conservative, broadly applicable bounds, and neutron stars with similar masses but very different tidal deformabilities could be a smoking-gun signature of dark matter.
threads: Нейтронные звёзды — космические жемчужины, но некоторые носят тёмную мантию или скрывают тёмное ядро. Лёгкая тёмная материя окутывает звезду, как слой перламутра, а тяжёлая сжимает её в компактный объект. Два «близнеца» с одинаковой массой, но разной податливостью к приливам — вот «дымящийся пистолет» для открытия тёмной материи. Следующее поколение телескопов, возможно, найдёт такие пары.

title: Quantum Tunneling Mpemba Effect: Paradoxical Cooling in Open Double-Well Potentials
oneliner: A hot quantum system can cool faster than a cold one thanks to tunneling and boundary absorption — this is the new quantum tunneling Mpemba effect.
description: Scientists have developed a continuous theory of the quantum tunneling Mpemba effect for a particle in a symmetric double-well potential trap with absorbing boundaries. Using a non-Hermitian spectral decomposition, they proved that the non-monotonic temperature dependence of the first non-trivial ev
abstract: The quantum tunneling Mpemba effect in a symmetric double well with boundary sinks has been studied. Using non-Hermitian decomposition of the effective Hamiltonian, relaxation without quenches was investigated. It is proven that the nonmonotonicity of the coefficient a2(Ti) with respect to the initial temperature Ti is a universal topological property governed by the Sturm–Liouville theorem and invariant under system size changes. The peak arises from a geometric coincidence of localized states and extended non-Hermitian decay channels. Anomalies in S(t,Ti) and D(t,Ti) require a separation of scales: above-barrier escape must be much faster than tunneling decay (Γ2 >> Γ0, Γ1). The approach unites classical boundary dissipation with open quantum dynamics, opening possibilities for engineering nonequilibrium states via boundary losses.
threads: Горячая вода иногда замерзает быстрее холодной — этот парадокс известен со времён Аристотеля. Теперь физики обнаружили его квантового двойника. Представьте квантовый колокол с микроскопической трещиной: холодный удар оставляет долгий, вязкий гул — энергия утекает сквозь барьер туннельным шёпотом. Горячий же удар рождает высокую ноту, которая мгновенно находит трещину и гаснет. Укрощая такие потери, мы сможем сверхбыстро перезаряжать квантовые процессоры или готовить идеально

title: X-ray Silence of PSR J0901-4046: The Mystery of Slow Pulsars Deepens
oneliner: Chandra observations rule out an isolated white dwarf as the central engine of the enigmatic long-period radio transient PSR J0901-4046, pointing to a neutron star with a non-rotational emission mechanism.
description: Observations by the Chandra X-ray space telescope detected no emission from the source PSR J0901-4046—a pulsar with a period of 75.9 seconds. The upper limit on X-ray luminosity turned out to be tens of times lower than predictions for a rotating white dwarf, but consistent with a neutron star model
abstract: Based on data from the Chandra observatory, an upper limit on the X-ray luminosity of the long-period radio transient PSR J0901-4046 (period 75.89 s) at a distance of 467 pc is derived: L_X ≤ a few × 10²⁸ erg/s. This limit is 50 times deeper than the previous Swift result and comparable to the spin-down luminosity of a neutron star, but about four orders of magnitude lower than the power expected from a spinning-down white dwarf. This discrepancy rules out isolated white dwarfs as the central source. It is suggested that the emission of isolated long-period transients is powered by dissipation of magnetic energy, similar to the radio emission mechanism of magnetars, rather than rotational spin-down.
threads: Обычный маятник со временем затихает. Но космический маятник PSR J0901-4046 — пульсар с периодом 76 секунд — продолжает посылать радиоимпульсы, хотя его рентгеновский жар должен быть виден. Chandra показала: жара нет. Гипотеза белого карлика опровергнута. Возможно, это нейтронная звезда, где энергию даёт не вращение, а магнитные поля. Так мы приближаемся к разгадке того, как устроены самые тихие обитатели галактик.

title: Physics helps neural networks find lensed gravitational waves
oneliner: A hybrid method combines neural network estimation of source parameters and direct waveform analysis to separate lensed mergers from overlaps of independent events.
description: Gravitational wave lensing is predicted by general relativity but has yet to be unambiguously detected. Scientists have developed a method that combines direct waveform classification with posterior estimation of common source parameters (chirp mass and symmetric mass ratio). A physical constraint:
abstract: A ranking method based on physically motivated posterior learning is proposed for identifying gravitationally lensed gravitational-wave signals. It exploits geometric-optics consistency: lensing alters amplitudes, arrival times, and Morse phase shifts while preserving the source’s intrinsic phase evolution. The posterior distribution for the detector-frame chirp mass and symmetric mass ratio is estimated using a simulation-trained neural network and combined with direct waveform shape features. Training is performed on generalized simulations; test lenses — point mass, singular isothermal sphere, ellipsoid, and shear-perturbed models — are reserved for out-of-sample evaluation. For an observationally motivated binary black hole population, the combined ranking boosts detection efficiency from 20.8% to 35.2% at a 1% false alarm threshold calibrated on a control sample of unrelated mergers. The network signal-to-noise ratio required for 50% efficiency decreases from 45.3 to 33.5, corresponding to a 1.35-fold increase in equivalent distance.
threads: Гравитационные линзы — карнавальные маски Вселенной: они искажают облик далёких слияний, но не могут изменить их суть. Сигнал линзированной волны подобен одной мелодии, звучащей с разными задержками и громкостью. Новый детектор, словно опытный слушатель, узнаёт знакомый мотив в хаосе нот. Это повышает шансы найти подлинные линзы и в перспективе позволит взвешивать сгустки тёмной материи, как невидимые инструменты, по отголоскам их гравитационных аккордов.

title: AI with Physical Intuition: How Transformers Help Hear the Dance of Black Holes
oneliner: A new machine learning method that embeds orbital physics into transformer architecture allows fast and accurate estimation of binary black hole parameters from pulsar timing array data.
description: Pulsar timing arrays (PTAs) capture nanohertz gravitational waves from merging supermassive black holes, but extracting information from noisy multi-year data is an immensely challenging computational task. A recent study proposes a hybrid approach: a transformer neural network augmented with physic
abstract: Analysis of pulsar timing array (PTA) data for detecting nanohertz gravitational waves faces computational difficulties due to the high dimensionality of the parameter space, complex noise models, and expensive Bayesian methods. A transformer with physically informed positional encoding is proposed, into which an analytical description of the phase evolution of the gravitational wave from an eccentric relativistic binary object is embedded. Training with the inclusion of generative models (discrete and continuous conditional normalizing flows) within simulation-based Bayesian inference allowed the recovery of posterior parameter distributions faster and with narrower confidence intervals compared to approaches that do not take physics into account. Experiments on signals with different signal-to-noise ratios (white noise) showed improved accuracy. The modular architecture easily extends to realistic scenarios with red noise and additional components, making the method a scalable alternative to standard pipelines for the next generation of PTA experiments.
threads: Гравитационные волны от чёрных дыр — это космическое танго, меняющее темп самой Вселенной. Нейросеть, обученная их ритму, как дирижёр, расслышала отдельную пару в шумном хоре. Это не просто анализ: это рождение гравитационной картографии.

title: Turning an Antiferromagnet into an Altermagnet: Surface Functionalization Opens the Way to Giant Magnetoresistance
oneliner: Chemical modification of a FeSe monolayer turns an ordinary antiferromagnet into an altermagnet with record tunneling magnetoresistance.
description: The work proposes a surface functionalization method to transform two-dimensional antiferromagnets into altermagnets—a new class of magnetic materials with zero net magnetization but spin-polarized currents. Using a FeSe monolayer as an example, it is shown that one-sided hydrogenation, oxidation, o
abstract: A method is proposed for converting two-dimensional antiferromagnets into altermagnets via one-sided chemical functionalization driven by symmetry. By breaking inversion and out-of-plane mirror symmetries while preserving the rotational symmetry that connects opposite spin sublattices, such treatment lifts spin degeneracy and induces momentum-dependent splitting. Using monolayer FeSe as an example, first-principles calculations show that hydrogenation, oxidation, or fluorination transform it into a d-wave altermagnet with pronounced spin splitting. Modeling of tunnel junctions based on functionalized FeSe revealed giant tunneling magnetoresistance (up to 1.87×10^3%), caused by momentum-selective spin filtering between parallel and antiparallel configurations of the Néel vector. The strong dependence on functionalization geometry further confirms that surface chemistry serves as an effective lever for controlling altermagnetic transport. The work establishes a “symmetry–chemistry–device” strategy for designing two-dimensional altermagnets and high-performance spintronic devices.
threads: Крошечная асимметрия — словно невидимая бабочка взмахнула крылом на одной стороне монослоя — и привычный антиферромагнетик обретает скрытый танец спинов. Альтермагнетизм, подобно дирижёру, разводит токи без внешнего поля. Сопротивление взлетает до 1870% (почти в 20 раз выше обычных устройств), открывая путь к сверхплотной памяти и квантовым вычислениям. Мы на пороге эпохи, где химия научилась дирижировать электронами.

title: Aromas of Cosmic Noon: PAHs in a Binary Quasar at the Edge of the Universe
oneliner: Using the James Webb Space Telescope, astronomers have detected widespread emission from aromatic and aliphatic hydrocarbons in a system of two merging quasars during the cosmic noon era.
description: In a paper accepted for publication in The Astrophysical Journal Letters, an international team led by Mentzer and Lee analyzed integral field spectroscopy data from the MIRI instrument on the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) for the dual quasar J0749+2255 at redshift 2.17. By applying spectral dec
abstract: Using integral field spectroscopy with JWST/MIRI, in the double quasar SDSSJ074922.96+225511.7 at redshift z~2.17 (corresponding to a Universe age of about 3 billion years, the peak of star formation and black hole growth—"cosmic noon"), we have for the first time recorded joint emission from aromatic (3.3 µm, C–H stretching vibrations in small PAHs) and aliphatic (3.4 µm, C–H stretches of aliphatic side groups) hydrocarbons. From measured band intensities, the aliphatic fraction of PAHs and its spatial variations were derived. It turns out that in the brightest regions, concentrated around the nuclei of the two quasars, the aliphatic fraction is anomalously high. This indicates the survival of not only small PAHs (~20–30 carbon atoms) but also their attached aliphatic chains under powerful ultraviolet radiation from extreme star formation outbursts.
threads: Три миллиарда лет назад две чёрные дыры кружились в гравитационном вальсе, разбрызгивая вокруг ароматические молекулы. JWST уловил этот шлейф — и удивил: молекулы не гибнут вблизи монстров, а процветают. Мы на пороге химии слияний: откроем, чем пахнет рождение галактик.

title: Inflation in Generalized Entropic Cosmology and f(Q)-Gravity from ACT Data
oneliner: Entropic cosmology and f(Q)-gravity allow us to build inflationary models that agree with the latest data from the ACT telescope.
description: This study explores building inflationary models within generalized entropic cosmology and its equivalent f(Q)-gravity, satisfying the new observational constraints from the Atacama Cosmology Telescope (ACT) combined with Planck and BAO data. Using the reconstruction method, the authors find explici
abstract: Within the framework of generalized entropic cosmology and its equivalent f(Q)-gravity, inflationary models have been built that are consistent with the latest ACT DR6, Planck, and BAO data. Using a reconstruction scheme, explicit inflationary solutions satisfying observational constraints have been obtained. For f(Q)-gravity with a scalar field, inflation compatible with ACT is achieved for a broad class of f(Q) through the choice of potentials. Cosmology based on generalized entropy (encompassing Tsallis, Rényi, Barrow, and other entropies) has been investigated, and the corresponding Friedmann equations derived. By utilizing the correspondence between generalized entropy and f(Q)-gravity, generalized entropic inflation passing the ACT DR6 test is obtained. It is demonstrated that for any such entropy, ACT-consistent inflation is possible with a suitable scalar potential. The results hint at the existence of a unified entropic mechanism governing the early Universe.
threads: Телескоп ACT взял фальшивую ноту у целого класса инфляционных моделей. Его данные показали: первичные возмущения чуть «голубее», чем предполагалось. Теперь физики переписывают партитуру ранней Вселенной. Их инструмент — обобщённая энтропия, где пространство-время теряет метрическую стройность. Без скалярного дирижёра лишь редкие энтропийные мотивы попадают в тон, но с ним звучит целый оркестр возможных миров. Так микроскопическая статистика космоса выходит из тени, а первичны

title: Fully gated control of a parity-protected superconducting qubit on an altermagnetic Josephson junction
oneliner: A superconducting qubit with an altermagnetic Josephson junction is proposed, controlled solely by electric voltage, providing parity protection and coherence times up to tens of milliseconds.
description: This work proposes a parity-protected superconducting qubit based on an altermagnetic Josephson junction with fully electrical control. Altermagnets exhibit spin-dependent band splitting without a net magnetic moment, allowing Josephson channel interference without external fields. By tuning the che
abstract: A parity-protected qubit based on a superconductor–altermagnet–superconductor Josephson junction with electrical control is proposed. Altermagnets — compensated magnets with momentum-dependent spin splitting and zero net magnetization — provide spin splitting without an external field. The two spin channels acquire opposite phase shifts; their interference is tuned by a gate voltage. In the 0-π transition regime, the first Josephson harmonic is suppressed, the second dominates, forming a double-well potential with nearly degenerate states of opposite Cooper pair parity. For parameters compatible with gatemon qubits, coherence times reach tens of milliseconds under fully electrical control. Altermagnetic Josephson junctions are promising for protected qubits with local, scalable, and fluxonium-free control.
threads: Альтермагнетики играют в прятки со спинами: намагниченность отсутствует, но зоны расщеплены по спину. В сверхпроводящем переходе ток звучит как дуэт — две гармоники, бегущие навстречу. Химический потенциал выступает дирижёром: заглушая первую гармонику, он рождает двойную потенциальную яму, в которой кубит защищён от зарядового шума самой топологией состояний. Время когерентности — десятки миллисекунд. Это маршрут к квантовым процессорам, где ошибки гасятся не кодами, а физик

title: Fast Radio Bursts: Angular Power Spectrum as a Cosmological Probe
oneliner: The angular autocorrelation spectrum of dispersion measures of fast radio bursts has been measured, paving the way for independent determination of cosmological parameters and mapping of hidden baryons.
description: An international team of astronomers has for the first time measured the angular power spectrum of dispersion measure (DM) fluctuations of fast radio bursts (FRBs) using a catalog of 3,455 events recorded by the CHIME telescope. This spectrum reflects large-scale inhomogeneities in the distribution
abstract: The first measurement of the angular auto-power spectrum of dispersion measures (DMs) of fast radio bursts (FRBs) is presented, based on a sample of 3455 presumably single events from the CHIME/FRB Catalog 2. An angular correlation signal with a significance level >3σ is detected, linked to large-scale fluctuations in free-electron density. Fitting the measured spectrum to theoretical models enabled constraints on two parameter combinations: Ω_b h²–H₀ (baryonic matter density and the Universe’s expansion rate) and Ω_b h²–f_d (the fraction of baryons in large-scale structure). Using simulated catalogs, the method’s robustness was tested against systematic uncertainties from the redshift distribution of FRBs and DM contributions from host galaxies, the Milky Way halo, and the Galactic interstellar medium. The angular power spectrum proved nearly insensitive to uncorrelated DM components, like the host galaxy contribution, effectively reducing the impact of poorly constrained systematics. Unlike traditional DM–z analysis, this method does not require individual redshifts — only the overall distribution — and partially breaks parameter degeneracies in the Ω_b h²–H₀ and Ω_b h²–f_d planes. These results establish the DM angular power spectrum as a robust cosmological probe and a powerful indicator of baryon distribution.
threads: Каждый быстрый радиовсплеск — как удар молнии в безоблачную ночь: мы не видим грозу, но задержка грома выдаёт облака. 3455 таких «громовых» сигналов, собранных телескопом CHIME, впервые обрисовали контуры барионной паутины. Когда счёт пойдёт на сотни тысяч, мы не только найдём все пропавшие барионы, но и, возможно, услышим дыхание тёмной энергии.

title: Infrared Divergences as a Wandering Vacuum: A New Look at Quantum Noise
oneliner: Infrared divergences in quantum field theory are not a pathology, but a signature of a 'wandering vacuum' that generates classical stochastic fields.
description: Traditionally, infrared divergences (IRDs) are viewed as a technical problem that needs fixing. A new work offers a constructive interpretation: IRDs signal that the quantum vacuum continuously 'wanders' through a family of inequivalent states as a classical order parameter evolves. Using the closed
abstract: Infrared divergences are reinterpreted as a signature of a wandering vacuum: the quantum ground state continuously transitions through a family of inequivalent states as the classical order parameter changes. The technical core is an exact splitting within the closed-time-path (CTP) action. The imaginary part of the influence, divergent in the infrared, is transformed via a Hubbard–Stratonovich identity into a classical noise source; the retarded kernel stores memory of past vacuum transitions, while the noise kernel encodes the quantum uncertainty of the next. The construction is applied to soft QED, scalar fields in de Sitter space, soft gravitons, and nonequilibrium phase transitions, showing that a unified wandering vacuum mechanism generates 1/f noise, the primordial perturbation spectrum, gravitational memory, and order parameter dynamics. A geometric formulation in terms of a Hilbert space bundle over the vacuum manifold is discussed as an outlook.
threads: Шум в электронике, который мы привыкли списывать на дефекты, на самом деле — почерк блуждающего квантового вакуума. Подобно тому как прилив напоминает о невидимой Луне, инфракрасные расходимости указывают на глубинные сдвиги в ткани полей. Возможно, именно эти шумы помогут нам услышать гравитационную память или природу тёмной материи.

title: Cosmic Billiards: Triple Black Holes in Realistic Simulations
oneliner: A new computer model has for the first time traced the evolution of triple massive black holes within live galaxy simulations.
description: An international team of astrophysicists has extended the RAMCOAL model to describe the interaction of three massive black holes at all stages—from inspiral to merger driven by gravitational waves. Tests revealed that even small changes in initial conditions can drastically alter the system's fate:
abstract: The RAMCOAL method is extended to directly model subgrid dynamics of triple supermassive black hole systems in hydrodynamic simulations of galaxies. Black holes go through a phase of dynamical friction, form a bound pair, which hardens via stellar scattering, gas torques, and coupling with a circumnuclear disk; when the hierarchical triple becomes chaotic, the interaction is matched to a library of outcomes from N-body numerical experiments, allowing to account for mergers, exchanges, and ejections. Tests on isolated galaxies with different geometries showed that the approach geometry changes which pair ultimately merges and after what time. For the first time, the full dynamical evolution of a triple black hole system up to merger is demonstrated inside a live hydrodynamic simulation. This provides an end-to-end method for predicting black hole coalescences induced by triple interactions, self-consistently linked to the evolution of the host galaxy. As a result, each merger case preserves the history of its environment, paving the way to merger catalogs that directly connect gravitational wave signatures with the galaxies where they form.
threads: В центрах галактик идёт невидимая игра: чёрные дыры кружатся в тройном вальсе, и иногда третий партнёр резко ускоряет развязку. Наклон орбиты на несколько градусов — и миллиарды лет ожидания схлопываются в сотни миллионов. Хаос оказывается архитектором слияний. К 2035 году детектор LISA сможет услышать эту гравитационную музыку — осталось только расшифровать партитуру.

title: How Faster R-CNN Neural Network Finds Giant Star Clumps in Hundreds of Thousands of Galaxies
oneliner: Deep learning has unlocked the automated hunt for stellar nurseries in the nearby Universe, beyond the reach of traditional algorithms.
description: Astronomers developed a model based on Faster R-CNN that simultaneously analyzes six photometric filters to detect kiloparsec-scale star clumps—the ‘construction sites’ of massive stars. Using the foundational Zoobot neural network, trained on millions of galaxy images, the model achieves >90% compl
abstract: Giant star-forming clumps (GSFCs) — kiloparsec-scale regions of intense star formation with masses of 10^7–10^9 M⊙ — are ubiquitous in high-redshift galaxies but nearly absent in the nearby Universe (z ≲ 0.5). To find their analogs in modern galaxies, a deep learning-based object detection model was developed. The method extends the Faster R-CNN architecture to simultaneously process six photometric bands (ugrizy) from the HSC-SSP and CLAUDS surveys, enabling identification of clumps, their positions, and contaminants. A pre-trained Zoobot model was used as the feature extractor — one of its first applications to astronomical detection. Accuracy was tested on a large sample of real galaxies with simulated clumps injected: detection completeness exceeds 0.9, purity 0.8. The result opens the door to systematic searches for such structures in the local Universe and refinement of galaxy evolution models.
threads: Космос — океан галактических полей. Когда-то в них буйно всходили звёздные «урожаи» — сгустки размером в тысячи световых лет. Сегодня эти плодородные участки почти исчезли. Нейросеть-комбайн, обученная на миллионах снимков, прочесала 710 тысяч галактик и нашла 1,5 миллиона таких всходов. Мы впервые можем проследить, как усыхала почва звездорождения за миллиарды лет. Возможно, это путь к пониманию старения галактик.

title: Quasi-interstellar objects: when the Solar System catches up with its own ejections
oneliner: Small bodies ejected from the Solar System in the past may return after tens of millions of years, mimicking interstellar visitors.
description: Imagine a comet that left our system millions of years ago, made a loop around the Galaxy, and now is flying back into the inner regions of the Solar System. Such "quasi-interstellar objects" (quasi-ISOs) could be mistaken for interstellar wanderers from other stars, skewing statistics and misleadin
abstract: Interstellar objects originating from the Solar System, rather than other stars, could complicate upcoming surveys. This study examines whether unbound Solar System small bodies can dynamically evolve in the Galactic potential to re-encounter the Sun. Such 'quasi-interstellar objects' (quasi-ISOs) are primarily ejected from the outer Oort cloud through erosion in the past few hundred Myr, except the most recent ~10 Myr. After orbiting near the solar neighborhood beyond the tidal radius, objects on specific paths can return. The larger population of ISOs produced early in the Solar System's history is too dispersed to contribute noticeably. Quasi-ISOs are predicted to be rare, with hyperbolic excess velocities around 0.1 km/s, making them easily distinguishable from extrasolar ISOs and confirming the sample's Galactic origin. Detection of a quasi-ISO would suggest larger-than-expected Oort cloud losses or a catastrophic erosion event 10–300 Myr ago, otherwise undetectable.
threads: 0,1 км/с — с такой скоростью забытые осколки Солнечной системы возвращаются домой. Это галактический вальс, в котором танцоры почти не разлучаются, отчего возвращение растягивается на миллионы лет. Скоро LSST начнёт ловить тысячи межзвёздных объектов, и мы впервые сможем опознать среди них «призраков» нашего прошлого — или убедиться, что облако Оорта хранит свои тайны куда надёжнее, чем мы предполагали.

title: Quasi-interstellar objects: when the Solar System meets its own exiles
oneliner: Simulations show that comets ejected from the Oort Cloud can return with negligible speeds, creating a unique class of objects distinguishable from true interstellar wanderers.
description: The discovery of the first interstellar object, 1I/‘Oumuamua, raised the question: could such bodies actually hail from our own Solar System? New research models the fates of comets flung out of the outer Oort Cloud by galactic tides and passing stars, revealing that they can return to the inner reg
abstract: The possibility of return of small bodies ejected from the solar system due to erosion of the outer Oort cloud over the last few hundred million years (excluding the last 10 million years) has been investigated. It is shown that some of these objects, after moving in the galactic potential near the Sun but outside the tidal radius, can re-approach the solar system. Bodies that left the system at earlier stages are too scattered across the galaxy and do not contribute noticeably. These 'quasi-interstellar objects' (quasi-ISOs) are predicted to be very rare and have a velocity at infinity on the order of 0.1 km/s, sharply distinguishing them from objects of stellar origin. Their detection would indicate anomalously high losses from the Oort cloud or a catastrophic erosion event 10–300 million years ago, not detectable by other means.
threads: Галактический прилив выносит кометы из облака Оорта, словно щепки с берега. Миллионы лет странствий — и одна-две из миллиардов едва заметно возвращаются, почти замирая на пороге родного дома. Их скорость меньше 0.1 км/с. Отыскать такой «возвращенец» — всё равно что найти бутылку, брошенную в океан в юрском периоде. Такие находки раскроют, насколько бурным было прошлое Солнечной системы, и, возможно, подскажут, одиноки ли мы во тьме.

title: The Pioneers' Dance: Universal Fluctuation Laws in Competitive Exploration
oneliner: The spectral dimension of the environment determines whether an early advantage in a resource race will vanish or persist forever.
description: In the competitive struggle for resources, where the first-come gets exclusive rights, it's not just how much territory is covered, but who grabs the key spots. A team of physicists has shown that the fate of such contests is governed by a universal parameter — the spectral dimension. Depending on i
abstract: In competitive search, where the first agent to find a resource claims it exclusively, the key measure is the share of first discoveries made by one of the participants. For two identical competitors, we introduce the quantity X_n and study its fluctuations. Despite symmetry, equality holds only on average; whether an initial lead persists or fades is governed by the spectral dimension d_s, which sets how quickly the probability of returning to the starting point decays. For d_s<2, the advantage never vanishes; in the range 2≤d_s<3, we observe anomalously slow non-Gaussian concentration; for d_s≥3, fluctuations become Gaussian (with a logarithmic correction at d_s=3). For d_s≥2, exact asymptotic variances and the characteristic scales at which inequalities disappear are derived. Analyzing two-point correlations of first-passage labels reveals memory mechanisms. The picture is robust to variations in geometry, number of agents, and their heterogeneity, pointing to a general fluctuation theory of first-passage inequalities.
threads: Когда двое ищут золото, удача первопроходца может стать вечной, если ландшафт обладает низкой спектральной размерностью. Математика показывает: в пористых мирах неравенство застывает навсегда. Универсальный ключ — d_s. Это проливает свет на природу справедливости в экосистемах и алгоритмах.

title: Majorana Zero Modes in Quasi-Two-Dimensional Systems: A New Approach via Chiral Hinge States
oneliner: Scientists have proposed using the hybridization of chiral hinge states in higher-order topological insulators to create four localized Majorana modes.
description: The work theoretically explores the possibility of obtaining spatially separated Majorana zero modes in a quasi-two-dimensional geometry based on a chiral higher-order topological insulator coated with a conventional s-wave superconductor. Due to the proximity effect and finite size along one direct
abstract: A quasi-two-dimensional platform is proposed for creating Majorana zero modes: a chiral higher-order topological insulator in contact with an s-wave superconductor. In the bulk three-dimensional insulator, an anisotropic mass term provides gapless hinge states along the z-axis. In a sample with finite thickness along x and finite length along z, these hinge states hybridize into one-dimensional helical channels. The proximity effect induces p-wave pairing and opens a topological superconducting gap; as a result, four Majorana modes localize at the corners of a fully open sample, forming a second-order topological superconductor. In the three-dimensional limit, extended Majorana hinge modes are also discovered. Numerical calculations demonstrate the robustness of the corner modes against weak and moderate disorder in the presence of a non-zero superconducting gap. Thus, chiral hinge states confined by finite size represent a promising system for obtaining multiple Majorana modes via the standard proximity effect.
threads: В 1937 году Этторе Майорана предсказал частицу, тождественную своей античастице. Сегодня эти призраки «ткут» на углах кристаллов. Физики превратили шарнирные состояния высших топологических изоляторов в квантовое кружево: тонкая плёнка, сверхпроводящее покрытие — и на четырёх углах загораются майорановские моды. Они не боятся беспорядка и обещают стать идеальными кубитами. Шаг к компьютеру, который не ошибается.

title: Chaos, Diffusion, and Surfing: Particle Dynamics in Nonlinear Electromagnetic Waves
oneliner: How particles in extreme magnetic fields of neutron stars interact with ultra-strong radio waves, spawning deterministic chaos and acceleration to incredible energies.
description: Using fast radio bursts (FRBs) as a case study, the dynamics of charged particles in nonlinear X-modes in magnetized plasma are investigated. Through Hamiltonian formalism and numerical simulations, it is shown that the transition to global chaos occurs at a relative wave amplitude of δ = Bw/B0 ≥ 0.
abstract: The dynamics of charged particles in super-strong X-mode electromagnetic waves within a strongly magnetized plasma are examined. Chaos in particle motion emerges at a relative wave amplitude δ = B_w/B_0 ≈ 0.25, well below the field-reversal threshold (δ ≥ 1). The transition occurs via Chirikov resonance overlap, which destroys Kolmogorov–Arnold–Moser (KAM) tori. The maximal Lyapunov exponent grows logarithmically with δ, despite the strict integrability in the limit of vanishing magnetic field (δ → ∞). For δ ≫ 1, incomplete relaminarization of the phase flow spawns two particle types: the majority undergo stochastic diffusion, while a small fraction are caught by phase-locking with the wave and perform macroscopic intermittent jumps (Lévy flights). One-dimensional particle-in-cell (PIC) simulations in the strong magnetization (σ ≫ 1) and low-density limit confirm the single-particle Hamiltonian theory. The fraction of initial field energy dissipated remains moderate.
threads: В магнетаре царит хаос, где лишь считанные частицы ловят волну и разгоняются до энергий, недоступных земным коллайдерам. Раньше думали, что для этого нужен монстр-цунами, но новый анализ показывает: достаточно лёгкой зыби в четверть силы, чтобы запустить процесс. Это напоминает шторм, где большинство захлебывается в пене, а редкий серфер скользит по гребню. Возможно, именно так рождаются самые быстрые скитальцы космоса.

title: Constraints on dark matter-dark energy interaction from strong lensing
oneliner: Analysis of strong gravitational lensing data points to a strong interaction between dark matter and dark energy, capable of easing the Hubble tension.
description: Using observations of strong gravitational lensing on galaxy and cluster scales, astrophysicists estimated the parameters of a sign-switching interacting dark energy model. The results indicate a significant energy flow from dark energy to dark matter, leading to an earlier onset of accelerated cosm
abstract: An interaction between dark matter and dark energy, proportional to the dark matter density and dependent on the deceleration parameter q, is considered as a way to alleviate the coincidence problem and the Hubble tension. Data from strong gravitational lensing are used to constrain cosmological parameters in this model. The obtained estimates agree with an accelerating universe. The results support earlier findings that a coupling in the dark sector can ease tensions in the expansion history, and highlight the promise of strong lensing as an independent cosmological tool.
threads: Вселенная дышит? Гравитационные линзы зафиксировали «выдох» тёмной энергии, подпитывающий материю. Параметр связи β = -0.83 говорит: ускорение началось на два миллиарда лет раньше. Космический метаболизм реален — и мы только начали считать вдохи и выдохи.

title: How to predict a new material: a marriage of machine learning and quantum mechanics
oneliner: High-throughput screening of entropy-stabilized oxides using DFT and a neural network paves the way to accelerated design of functional materials.
description: A team from France and Japan has developed a computational pipeline to search for new entropy-stabilized oxides (ESOs) with a rock-salt structure. Using density functional theory, special quasi-random structures, and machine learning, they assessed the stability of 4368 five-component oxides from 16
abstract: To search for high-entropy oxides with the NaCl structure, a high-throughput computational approach has been developed, combining DFT calculations, special quasirandom structures (SQS), convex hull thermodynamics, and supervised learning. Based on GGA and meta-GGA calculations, a reference database of binary and ternary oxides was first created, including disordered phases. Quinary disordered systems were described by SQS supercells and used to train models predicting the distance to the convex hull and the stabilization temperature for all 4368 equimolar compositions derived from 16 cations. The optimized multilayer perceptron showed an error of ~4 kJ/mol with explicit DFT calculations for only 10% of the systems. Comparison with experimental synthesis and calculated decomposition pathways confirms that the method captures the main stability trends and dominant competing phases, although absolute stabilization temperatures are subject to systematic thermodynamic approximations. The results offer an efficient route for the data-driven design of multicomponent oxides.
threads: В мире материалов действует необычный закон: чем больше хаоса, тем прочнее структура. Это не парадокс, а магия энтропии: нейросеть, обученная на квантовой механике, предсказывает, какие «коктейли» из пяти металлов не распадутся. Подобно дирижёру, она слышит гармонию там, где мы воспринимаем лишь шум. Эти материалы преобразят аккумуляторы и катализ в ближайшее десятилетие.

title: Reflected Polarization of Close Binaries: A New Probe of Axion Birefringence
oneliner: Close binary stars act as natural polarization clocks, enabling the detection of the ultra-weak jitter of the polarization plane caused by axion dark matter.
description: The paper proposes a method for searching for ultralight axions via polarization modulation of reflected light in close binary systems. The periodic polarization, tightly locked to the orbital motion, creates an ideal template against which axion birefringence appears as sidebands of the orbital har
abstract: A method is proposed to search for birefringence induced by ultralight axion dark matter using polarimetry of close binary systems. In these systems, linear polarization of reflected or scattered light is modulated by orbital motion, creating a phase pattern. The axion oscillation of the polarization angle manifests as sidebands around the orbital harmonics. For a single bright binary, assuming white noise, a sensitivity to the axion-photon coupling of about 10^-12 GeV^-1 is achievable at an axion mass of 10^-20 eV. An array of suitable systems in an optimistic scenario could improve this to 10^-13 GeV^-1. The method complements existing astrophysical searches, offering a high-frequency optical probe of axion birefringence.
threads: Двойные звёзды пульсируют поляризованным светом, как точнейшие орбитальные часы. Аксионная тёмная материя вплетает в этот ритм едва слышные ноты — боковые частоты в спектре гармоник. Поймав это дрожание, мы услышим музыку невидимой Вселенной. Быть может, так мы впервые уловим дыхание скрытого сектора.

title: Polarimetry of Close Binary Stars: A New Probe for Axion Dark Matter
oneliner: Reflected polarization in close binary systems can capture ultra-small oscillations in polarization angle caused by axion birefringence, opening a window into the dark sector.
description: A new astrophysical method is proposed to search for ultralight axions—dark matter particle candidates—using high-precision polarimetric observations of close binary stars. In such systems, light scattering in the companion's atmosphere produces a small linearly polarized signal, whose phase depende
abstract: A method is proposed for searching for ultralight axion dark matter via polarization measurements of close binary stars. In such systems, reflected or scattered light acquires a small linear polarization, whose phase is tightly locked to the orbital motion. Axion-induced birefringence leads to oscillations in the polarization angle, which appear as sidebands around the orbital harmonics. For a single bright binary, assuming parameters typical of known systems and white noise, the projected sensitivity to the axion-photon coupling constant g_{a\gamma} is estimated at the level of 10⁻¹² GeV⁻¹ for an axion mass m_a = 10⁻²⁰ eV. Employing an array of suitable binaries in an optimistic scenario could improve the sensitivity to 10⁻¹³ GeV⁻¹. The method opens an additional high-frequency optical channel for probing axion birefringence, complementing existing astrophysical approaches.
threads: Поляризация света двойных звёзд колеблется в такт орбитальному движению, словно космический метроном. Но если через этот метроном пройдёт невидимое поле аксионов, тёмной материи, стрелка едва заметно дрогнет, создав неслышный аккомпанемент. Уловить этот шепот — значит впервые прикоснуться к субстанции, из которой, возможно, состоит четверть Вселенной. И для этого не нужно строить гигантские ускорители — достаточно всмотреться в танец света.

title: Cold Giants Beyond the Snow Line: Discovery of Four Planets by Microlensing
oneliner: Analysis of high-cadence survey data revealed four giant planets around low-mass stars in the Galactic bulge.
description: This work presents four new cold giant planets discovered through the analysis of gravitational microlensing events. The light curves exhibited brief anomalies, explained by a binary lens model with a single source. Component masses ranged from 0.07 to 0.63 M☉ for the host stars and from 0.2 to 2.5
abstract: We report the discovery of four cold giants from high-cadence microlensing surveys: OGLE-2016-BLG-0261, KMT-2025-BLG-0026, KMT-2025-BLG-0030, and KMT-2025-BLG-2272. Brief anomalies in the light curves correspond to a binary lens with a single source model at mass ratio q~10^{-3}. Finite-source effects measured in three events yielded the angular Einstein radius. A Bayesian analysis incorporating timescale and Einstein radius indicates host masses of 0.07–0.6 M⊙ and planet masses of 0.2–2.5 M_J, consistent with giant planets. Projected separations of 0.7–6 AU place them beyond the snow line. Lens distances of 6.6–7.9 kpc are consistent with bulge lenses. This result expands the sample of cold giants from uniform surveys, highlighting the effectiveness of microlensing for finding planets beyond the snow line and refining the properties of giants around low-mass stars.
threads: Капля дождя на оконном стекле на мгновение увеличивает далёкий огонёк. В космосе ту же роль играет гравитация: пролетая перед фоновой звездой, планета-невидимка искривляет свет и заставляет её вспыхнуть. Именно так обнаружили четыре ледяных гиганта в самом центре Галактики — там, где звёздная теснота рождает чудеса. Будущие телескопы превратят этот дождь из света в настоящий ливень открытий.

title: Why Double Diffusion Doesn’t Mix the Cores of Jupiter and Saturn
oneliner: New self-consistent modeling shows that double diffusive convection transports less than one Earth mass of heavy elements over the Solar System's lifetime.
description: A team of scientists has, for the first time, incorporated a recipe for transport via convective 'staircases' into a planetary evolution code. The evolution of Jupiter and Saturn was tracked over 4.56 billion years, starting from initial models with fuzzy cores. It turns out that double diffusion is
abstract: Double-diffusive convection in the "fuzzy" cores of Jupiter and Saturn is investigated as a mechanism for heavy element transport. Transport via convective staircases was added to the APPLE evolutionary code; calculations were performed for models with composition gradients set by accretion. Evolution was followed for 4.56 Gyr, accounting for convection, diffusion, and double diffusion. Result: less than ~1 M⊕ of heavy material is mixed over the entire cooling time; initial composition gradients persist. The cause: buoyancy work for material transport is limited by the thermal energy of the interior, whereas idealized Boussinesq simulations encourage layer merging. Thus, double-diffusive convection alone does not erase gradients; observed heavy element distributions point to additional mechanisms—such as giant collisions—that provide broader initial mixing than in standard accretion models.
threads: Океанографы давно знают: тёплые и солёные слои воды образуют ступеньки, не смешиваясь. Внутри Юпитера те же законы могли бы размыть ядро — но не смогли. Двойная диффузия, ленивый бармен планетных недр, потратила бы почти всю энергию планеты на то, чтобы поднять лишь пару земных масс. А значит, перемешивание задано рождением, и будущие наблюдения экзопланет увидят древние слои.

title: Hierarchical Black Hole Mergers: New Evidence from GW241011 and GW241110
oneliner: The gravitational wave events GW241011 and GW241110 are most likely mergers involving a second-generation black hole, confirmed by Bayesian analysis and the search for electromagnetic counterparts.
description: In a new study, two recent gravitational wave events from the GWTC-5.0 catalog—GW241011 and GW241110—are analyzed. Using a Bayesian approach, scientists compared hypotheses about first-generation black holes and hierarchical merger scenarios in star clusters and active galactic nucleus disks. Both e
abstract: For the gravitational-wave events GW241011 and GW241110, characterized by rapidly spinning primary components, unequal masses, and a nonzero angle between spins and orbital angular momentum, a Bayesian analysis of population models was performed. The scenario of first-generation black hole mergers (1G+1G) was compared with second-generation hierarchical models (2G+1G) in star clusters and disks of active galactic nuclei (AGN). Both events favor the 2G+1G interpretation, with log Bayes factors ln B ≈ 6.5–8.6 for GW241011 and ≈ 3.0–4.5 for GW241110, depending on the gravitational waveform model and assumed environment. AGN disk models show somewhat higher posterior probability, mainly due to the spin tilt distribution, but the data do not allow unambiguous classification of the environment. Additionally, the 3G+1G hypothesis was considered but did not receive statistically significant preference. A search for optical transients in the three-dimensional localization volumes was conducted using ZTF and ATLAS photometry; one candidate with a faint flare was found, possibly associated with GW241110.
threads: Чёрная дыра может родиться дважды: сначала из умирающей звезды, а затем — из столкновения с другой. Каждый такой шаг оставляет след в её массе и вращении, словно строчки в космическом палимпсесте. Гравитационные волны снимают слой за слоем, открывая историю поколений. Возможно, именно так росли сверхмассивные дыры в центрах галактик, а часть тёмной материи — это древние чёрные дыры, прошедшие множество слияний.

title: Collisionless damping of gravitational instability in fuzzy dark matter
oneliner: Scientists have developed a quantum kinetic theory explaining how initial particle velocities and quantum pressure shape the spectrum of inhomogeneities in ultralight dark matter.
description: The study explores gravitational instability in the fuzzy dark matter model, considering quantum effects and thermal velocity dispersion. Using the Wigner–Poisson kinetic equation, the authors derive a dispersion relation generalizing classical and quantum pictures, and find a sharp transition in th
abstract: A quantum-kinetic linear theory of gravitational instability is presented for a universe with 'fuzzy' dark matter. Starting from the Wigner transport equation and using Landau's approach to the linearized Wigner-Poisson system, a kinetic dispersion relation is derived that fully accounts for quantum effects via the plasma dispersion function. The growth rate of inhomogeneities is characterized by the dimensionless parameter α = k_qJ/k_J, where k_qJ and k_J are the quantum and thermal Jeans wavenumbers. An analytic expression is obtained for the spectral slope at the cutoff wavenumber; the spectrum undergoes a sharp transition at α ~ 0.5, separating regimes dominated by thermal Landau damping and quantum pressure. Application to fuzzy dark matter shows that the cutoff scale and spectral shape are sensitive to the particle mass and initial velocity dispersion, providing a method to simultaneously constrain these parameters from the observed matter power spectrum. The theory lays the groundwork for studying the transition from early thermal states to the formation of Bose-Einstein condensates in galactic structures.
threads: Тёмная материя как фотобумага: гравитация проявляет на ней узор, а квантовое давление и тепловой разброс борются за резкость. При α<0,5 структура застывает ледяной чёткостью — так рождаются солитонные ядра галактик. Этот же рубильник объясняет, почему масса частиц так долго ускользала от единой оценки. Теперь в проявителе настроен баланс, и остаётся лишь рассмотреть отпечатки в Лайман-альфа лесе.

title: Quantum-Kinetic Instability: How Fuzzy Dark Matter Shapes Cosmic Structures
oneliner: A theory merging quantum mechanics and kinetics reveals how ultralight dark matter could solve the dwarf galaxy puzzle.
description: Scientists have developed a rigorous quantum-kinetic theory of gravitational instability for the fuzzy dark matter model, which consists of ultralight bosons with masses around 10⁻²² eV. Using the Wigner equation and the Landau method, they derived a dispersion relation describing the growth of dens
abstract: A quantum-kinetic linear theory of gravitational instability for the fuzzy dark matter model is proposed. Based on the Wigner transport equation and the linearized Wigner–Poisson system using the Landau approach, an exact kinetic dispersion relation is obtained, which includes the plasma dispersion function. The growth rate of perturbations as a function of wavenumber is characterized by the dimensionless ratio of the quantum to thermal Jeans wavenumbers α = k_qJ/k_J. An analytical expression for the spectral slope at the cutoff scale reveals a sharp transition at α ~ 0.5: from a kinetic regime with collisionless damping via phase mixing and Landau resonance to a regime dominated by quantum pressure. Application to fuzzy dark matter shows that the cutoff scale and spectral shape are sensitive to the particle mass and initial velocity dispersion, providing a way to simultaneously constrain these parameters from the observed matter power spectrum. This work lays the theoretical foundation for studying the transition from early thermal states to the formation of Bose–Einstein condensates in galactic structures.
threads: Тёмная материя оказалась не просто холодным скелетом космоса, а квантовым танцполом, где тепловой шум борется с волнами-дирижёрами. Физики нашли точку смены ритма — параметр α около 0.5, когда квантовое давление резко перехватывает инициативу у случайных движений, словно невидимый диджей. Этот переход не только объяснит, почему карликовые галактики выглядят пушистыми, а не острыми, но и позволит, наблюдая за далёкими квазарами, взвесить частицы тёмной материи и измерить их ск

title: Inverse Oscillations in the Primordial Spectrum: Symbolic Regression Finds Unconventional Features
oneliner: Symbolic regression has uncovered periodic features of inverse scale in cosmic microwave background data, pointing to new physics of the early Universe.
description: Analysis of data from the Planck satellite and ground-based telescopes ACT and SPT-3G using symbolic regression revealed a preference for inverse k-oscillations in the primordial perturbation spectrum. These oscillations of the form cos(B/k) with B≈4 Mpc⁻¹ fit the data better than standard linear or
abstract: The study performed a template-free search for oscillatory features in the primordial power spectrum. Symbolic regression was applied to Planck data and a joint dataset of Planck+ACT+SPT-3G; both samples independently identify an inverse-scale oscillation of the form cos(B/k) with B ≈ 4 Mpc⁻¹ as the best low-complexity feature. Compared to traditional linear and logarithmic oscillating models, this shape shows better agreement with observations, showing weak evidence for a non-zero amplitude. The results position symbolic regression as an interpretable, model-independent machine learning tool for cosmological discoveries, avoiding the bias of fixed templates.
threads: Алгоритм, перебиравший миллионы формул, расслышал в данных Planck ритм, бьющийся в обратную сторону. Это как камень, брошенный наоборот: круги не расходятся, а сбегаются к центру. Обратные колебания в спектре первичных возмущений намекают на фазу расширения, где время могло течь иначе. Если сигнал реален, мы впервые слышим эхо квантовой гравитации.

title: Inverse Oscillations: Symbolic Regression Discovers a New Rhythm of the Early Universe
oneliner: Symbolic regression has revealed inverse oscillations in the primordial power spectrum, hinting at a possible super-expansion phase after the Big Bang.
description: Researchers searched for primordial oscillations without fixed templates using symbolic regression. Analysis of data from the Planck satellite and ground-based telescopes ACT and SPT-3G pointed to an oscillation of the form cos(B/k) with B≈4 Mpc⁻¹. This result is slightly preferred over standard lin
abstract: The search for oscillatory features in the primordial power spectrum, pointing to new physics in the early Universe, is typically conducted using fixed templates. In this study, a template-free search was performed for the first time using symbolic regression. Analysis of Planck data and the combination Planck+ACT+SPT-3G independently singled out an inverse k-oscillation: cos(B/k) with B≃4 Mpc⁻¹ as the most simple interpretable structure. Comparison with linear and logarithmic oscillating models showed that the inverse cosine better describes the observations, demonstrating a mild preference for a nonzero amplitude. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of symbolic regression as an interpretable, model-independent tool for cosmological discoveries.
threads: Алгоритм без подсказок разобрал реликтовое излучение по нотам и нашёл ритм, который не предсказывала ни одна теория. Это не простая волна: на больших масштабах она замедляется, как басовая струна космоса. Возможно, так отозвалась эпоха, где само пространство расширялось быстрее мыслимого, нарушая обычные законы. От этой находки до пульсирующих вселенных из фантастики — один научный шаг.

title: Stellar Nurseries in the Shadow of an Active Nucleus: JWST Peers into Centaurus A
oneliner: Using the MIRI instrument on the JWST, astronomers have for the first time resolved the inner region of Centaurus A into individual stars, discovering 928 candidate young stellar objects immersed in a dusty disk.
description: The work presents the results of observations of the central region (~4×2 kpc) of the nearest radio galaxy Centaurus A using the MIRI camera on the James Webb Space Telescope in three infrared filters. Analysis of color-magnitude diagrams revealed a population of 928 point sources with strong infrar
abstract: The study presents images of the central disk of galaxy Centaurus A, taken by the MIRI camera on the James Webb Space Telescope in three filters (F560W, F770W, F1130W) with a resolution of ~4×2 kpc. Previously known as the 'oval dust shell,' the structure resolves into numerous loop-like filaments, bright in F1130W and linked to the warped disk. Photometric color-magnitude and color-color diagrams revealed 928 red point sources with infrared excess (~36% of objects with quality photometry), spatially concentrated in the disk. Their mid-IR spectral slopes point to warm dust emission; colors and distribution match a population of embedded young stellar objects (YSOs)—signposts of recent (10⁵–10⁶ years) star formation. The geometric correlation with the disk and the lack of connection to the radio jet show that star formation in the central regions of Centaurus A is governed by gas accreted during a merger, with no signs of interaction between the AGN jet and the interstellar medium.
threads: В центре Центавра А, прямо под носом у сверхмассивной чёрной дыры, телескоп Уэбба насчитал 928 новорождённых звёзд, спрятанных в пылевом коконе. Это как обнаружить детский сад в цеху металлургического завода. Джет чёрной дыры оказался ни при чём — звезды рождает газ, принесённый древним слиянием. Заглянув в эти звёздные ясли, мы, возможно, поймём, как галактики тихо взрослеют, пока их ядра грохочут.

title: CausticFlow: how machine learning accelerates the analysis of binary gravitational lenses
oneliner: The neural network CausticFlow delivers parameters of binary lenses in seconds, replacing hours of computation and paving the way for systematic processing of large surveys.
description: The CausticFlow neural network framework is introduced, combining neural differential equations and normalizing flows for rapid inference of binary gravitational microlensing parameters. Trained on synthetic light curves, the model generates samples from the posterior distribution in a fraction of a
abstract: We present CausticFlow, which combines neural controlled differential equations with normalizing flows for Bayesian inference of binary gravitational microlensing parameters. The model handles irregular time series and missing data, capturing multimodal posterior distributions. After training on simulated light curves (analogous to KMTNet data), CausticFlow generates posterior samples in a fraction of a second; MAP estimates achieve ~17% accuracy for the mass ratio q and ~3% for the projected separation s. With subsequent local optimization, accuracy improves to <5% and <1%; the model reproduces χ² for ~80% of simulated events. Testing on 10 real events with higher-order effects, irregular cadence, and real noise showed that after simple local fine-tuning, CausticFlow recovers parameters, light curve morphology, and lensing geometry for 7 out of 10 events in ~10 minutes of CPU time per event.
threads: Свет далёкой звезды исполняет танец на каустиках двойной линзы. CausticFlow — цифровой шлифовщик — считывает этот танец за секунды и находит скрытую геометрию. Где раньше требовались сутки вычислений, теперь хватает 10 минут. Так мы учимся видеть невидимые планеты и, быть может, следы тёмной материи.

title: Canonical Quantization of Neurons: How to Apply Quantum Mechanics Laws to Machine Learning
oneliner: Scientists turned classical neurons into quantum observables, paving the way for new machine learning algorithms on quantum data.
description: Researchers from Cornell and Shanghai Universities proposed a method of canonical quantization of classical neurons—replacing the energy function with a quantum Hamiltonian and applying nonlinear activation via matrix calculus. This enables the creation of quantum neurons capable of outperforming cl
abstract: Canonical quantization has been applied to the artificial neuron: the energy function is replaced by a quantum Hamiltonian, and activation is implemented via matrix functional calculus, resulting in an activation observable measured on the input state. Quantized neurons were investigated for function approximation with the goal of reconstructing an unknown observable from labeled quantum data. Hybrid algorithms were developed: for measuring the activation observable, quantum algorithms 'degree of one cumulant' and 'Schrödingerization' are used, while for estimating the gradients of the quadratic error — classical random sampling, the Hadamard test, and Hamiltonian simulation. Experiments show increased expressiveness of quantized neurons compared to classical ones on representative tasks. This approach systematizes the construction of quantum machine learning primitives and lays the foundation for neural networks optimized for quantum data.
threads: Классический нейрон — камерный музыкант. Квантовый — филармония, где каждый инструмент звучит во всех тональностях разом. Каноническое квантование меняет веса на гамильтониан, активацию — на матричную функцию, и нейрон улавливает скрытую гармонию данных. В численных тестах квантовый нейрон обучился в десять раз точнее: некоммутирующие операторы, как обертоны, открывают новые измерения. Это прямой путь к нейросетям, обучающимся на квантовых состояниях — и, быть может, к разгад

title: The Quantum Wave That Gives Birth to the Universe
oneliner: A scientist shows how a frozen quantum wave can hide curved spacetime within it.
description: A physicist found that interpreting a motionless quantum wave as a probability distribution for a particle’s internal clock naturally yields the math of an expanding universe. Gravity becomes unnecessary—spacetime bends purely from statistical chances. This offers a route to merging quantum physics
threads: Представьте: статичная квантовая волна, застывшая во времени, таит в себе целую вселенную. Если интерпретировать её амплитуду как распределение вероятности «внутреннего времени», из неё сама собой вырастает кривизна пространства-времени — безо всякой гравитации. Это значит, что тяготение может быть не фундаментальной силой, а тенью квантовых законов.

title: Dark energy with a sign change: How the transition from negative to positive density smooths out the Hubble tension
oneliner: The ECDM model with a smooth change in the sign of dark energy density resolves the contradiction between early and late measurements of the Hubble constant.
description: The standard cosmological model ΛCDM faces the Hubble tension — a discrepancy in the expansion rate of the Universe measured by different methods. This work studies the ECDM model, where dark energy density smoothly transitions from negative to positive values, modifying the late expansion history.
abstract: A sign-changing dark energy scenario (ECDM model) is considered, where the dark energy density gradually transitions from negative to positive.
